        return schedules.get(frequency, schedules['daily'])
    
    def list_backups(self, table_name, max_results=20):
        """List available backups for a table"""
        
        try:
            response = self.client.list_backups(
                TableName=table_name,
                Limit=max_results
            )
            
            backups = []
            for backup in response.get('BackupSummaries', []):
                backups.append({
                    'backup_name': backup['BackupName'],
                    'backup_arn': backup['BackupArn'],
                    'creation_time': backup['BackupCreationDateTime'],
                    'status': backup['BackupStatus'],
                    'size_bytes': backup.get('BackupSizeBytes', 0)
                })
            
            return {'success': True, 'backups': backups, 'count': len(backups)}
            
        except Exception as e:
            return {'success': False, 'error': str(e)}

# Initialize backup manager
backup_manager = DynamoDBBackupManager()
🔐 Backup & Recovery Strategies
Strategy Method RPO/RTO anihpj Usage
On-Demand Backups Manual/scheduled full backups RPO: Hours, RTO: 30-60 min Pre-deployment backups
Point-in-Time Recovery Continuous backups (35 days) RPO: 5 min, RTO: 15-30 min Critical tables (job analytics)
AWS Backup Automated backup plans RPO: Daily, RTO: 30-60 min All production tables
Global Tables Multi-region replication RPO: < 1 sec, RTO: < 1 min High-availability requirements
💾 anihpj Backup Strategy:
  • Critical Tables (job_analytics): PITR enabled, daily AWS Backup, 90-day retention
  • High-Priority Tables (user_activities): PITR enabled, daily AWS Backup, 35-day retention
  • Standard Tables (notifications): Weekly AWS Backup, 7-day retention
  • Pre-Deployment: On-demand backups before major releases
  • Cross-Region: Global tables for critical data in US-East and US-West
  • Testing: Quarterly disaster recovery drills
  • Monitoring: CloudWatch alarms for backup failures
  • Documentation: Maintain runbooks for recovery scenarios

🚀 Deployment & CI/CD

Implement Infrastructure as Code and automated deployment pipelines for DynamoDB tables in your anihpj job portal.

🏗️ Infrastructure as Code Options
Tool Pros Cons Best For
CloudFormation Native AWS, complete features Verbose YAML/JSON AWS-only infrastructure
Terraform Multi-cloud, HCL syntax State management complexity Multi-cloud environments
AWS CDK Python/TypeScript code Learning curve Developers preferring code
Pulumi Real programming languages Newer, smaller community Complex logic requirements
📋 Infrastructure as Code - CloudFormation
# infrastructure/dynamodb-tables.yaml - CloudFormation template for anihpj DynamoDB tables
AWSTemplateFormatVersion: '2010-09-09'
Description: DynamoDB tables for anihpj job portal application

Parameters:
  Environment:
    Type: String
    Default: production
    AllowedValues:
      - development
      - staging
      - production
    Description: Deployment environment
  
  BillingMode:
    Type: String
    Default: PAY_PER_REQUEST
    AllowedValues:
      - PAY_PER_REQUEST
      - PROVISIONED
    Description: Billing mode for DynamoDB tables

Resources:
  # User Sessions Table
  UserSessionsTable:
    Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table
    Properties:
      TableName: !Sub 'anihpj-user-sessions-${Environment}'
      BillingMode: !Ref BillingMode
      AttributeDefinitions:
        - AttributeName: session_key
          AttributeType: S
        - AttributeName: user_id
          AttributeType: N
      KeySchema:
        - AttributeName: session_key
          KeyType: HASH
      GlobalSecondaryIndexes:
        - IndexName: UserIdIndex
          KeySchema:
            - AttributeName: user_id
              KeyType: HASH
          Projection:
            ProjectionType: ALL
      TimeToLiveSpecification:
        AttributeName: expires_at
        Enabled: true
      PointInTimeRecoverySpecification:
        PointInTimeRecoveryEnabled: true
      SSESpecification:
        SSEEnabled: true
        SSEType: KMS
        KMSMasterKeyId: !Ref DynamoDBKMSKey
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment
        - Key: Application
          Value: anihpj
        - Key: ManagedBy
          Value: CloudFormation

  # User Activity Table
  UserActivityTable:
    Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table
    Properties:
      TableName: !Sub 'anihpj-user-activity-${Environment}'
      BillingMode: !Ref BillingMode
      AttributeDefinitions:
        - AttributeName: user_id
          AttributeType: N
        - AttributeName: timestamp
          AttributeType: S
        - AttributeName: action_type
          AttributeType: S
      KeySchema:
        - AttributeName: user_id
          KeyType: HASH
        - AttributeName: timestamp
          KeyType: RANGE
      GlobalSecondaryIndexes:
        - IndexName: ActionTypeIndex
          KeySchema:
            - AttributeName: action_type
              KeyType: HASH
            - AttributeName: timestamp
              KeyType: RANGE
          Projection:
            ProjectionType: ALL
      StreamSpecification:
        StreamViewType: NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES
      PointInTimeRecoverySpecification:
        PointInTimeRecoveryEnabled: true
      SSESpecification:
        SSEEnabled: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment
        - Key: Application
          Value: anihpj

  # Job Analytics Table
  JobAnalyticsTable:
    Type: AWS::DynamoDB::Table
    Properties:
      TableName: !Sub 'anihpj-job-analytics-${Environment}'
      BillingMode: !Ref BillingMode
      AttributeDefinitions:
        - AttributeName: job_id
          AttributeType: N
        - AttributeName: date
          AttributeType: S
      KeySchema:
        - AttributeName: job_id
          KeyType: HASH
        - AttributeName: date
          KeyType: RANGE
      PointInTimeRecoverySpecification:
        PointInTimeRecoveryEnabled: true
      SSESpecification:
        SSEEnabled: true
      Tags:
        - Key: Environment
          Value: !Ref Environment
        - Key: Application
          Value: anihpj

  # KMS Key for Encryption
  DynamoDBKMSKey:
    Type: AWS::KMS::Key
    Properties:
      Description: KMS key for DynamoDB table encryption (anihpj)
      KeyPolicy:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Sid: Enable IAM User Permissions
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              AWS: !Sub 'arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:root'
            Action: 'kms:*'
            Resource: '*'
          - Sid: Allow DynamoDB to use the key
            Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: dynamodb.amazonaws.com
            Action:
              - 'kms:Decrypt'
              - 'kms:DescribeKey'
            Resource: '*'

  # Auto-scaling for User Activity Table (if PROVISIONED mode)
  UserActivityReadCapacityScalableTarget:
    Type: AWS::ApplicationAutoScaling::ScalableTarget
    Condition: IsProvisionedBilling
    Properties:
      MaxCapacity: 100
      MinCapacity: 5
      ResourceId: !Sub 'table/${UserActivityTable}'
      RoleARN: !GetAtt DynamoDBAutoScalingRole.Arn
      ScalableDimension: dynamodb:table:ReadCapacityUnits
      ServiceNamespace: dynamodb

  UserActivityReadScalingPolicy:
    Type: AWS::ApplicationAutoScaling::ScalingPolicy
    Condition: IsProvisionedBilling
    Properties:
      PolicyName: ReadAutoScalingPolicy
      PolicyType: TargetTrackingScaling
      ScalingTargetId: !Ref UserActivityReadCapacityScalableTarget
      TargetTrackingScalingPolicyConfiguration:
        TargetValue: 70.0
        PredefinedMetricSpecification:
          PredefinedMetricType: DynamoDBReadCapacityUtilization

  # IAM Role for Auto-scaling
  DynamoDBAutoScalingRole:
    Type: AWS::IAM::Role
    Condition: IsProvisionedBilling
    Properties:
      AssumeRolePolicyDocument:
        Version: '2012-10-17'
        Statement:
          - Effect: Allow
            Principal:
              Service: application-autoscaling.amazonaws.com
            Action: 'sts:AssumeRole'
      ManagedPolicyArns:
        - 'arn:aws:iam::aws:policy/service-role/DynamoDBAutoscalingRole'

Conditions:
  IsProvisionedBilling: !Equals [!Ref BillingMode, 'PROVISIONED']

Outputs:
  UserSessionsTableName:
    Description: User sessions table name
    Value: !Ref UserSessionsTable
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-UserSessionsTable'
  
  UserActivityTableName:
    Description: User activity table name
    Value: !Ref UserActivityTable
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-UserActivityTable'
  
  JobAnalyticsTableName:
    Description: Job analytics table name
    Value: !Ref JobAnalyticsTable
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-JobAnalyticsTable'
  
  UserActivityStreamArn:
    Description: Stream ARN for user activity table
    Value: !GetAtt UserActivityTable.StreamArn
    Export:
      Name: !Sub '${AWS::StackName}-UserActivityStreamArn'
🏗️ Terraform Configuration
# infrastructure/terraform/dynamodb.tf - Terraform configuration for anihpj DynamoDB
variable "environment" {
  description = "Deployment environment"
  type        = string
  default     = "production"
}

variable "billing_mode" {
  description = "DynamoDB billing mode"
  type        = string
  default     = "PAY_PER_REQUEST"
}

# User Sessions Table
resource "aws_dynamodb_table" "user_sessions" {
  name           = "anihpj-user-sessions-${var.environment}"
  billing_mode   = var.billing_mode
  hash_key       = "session_key"

  attribute {
    name = "session_key"
    type = "S"
  }

  attribute {
    name = "user_id"
    type = "N"
  }

  global_secondary_index {
    name            = "UserIdIndex"
    hash_key        = "user_id"
    projection_type = "ALL"
  }

  ttl {
    attribute_name = "expires_at"
    enabled        = true
  }

  point_in_time_recovery {
    enabled = true
  }

  server_side_encryption {
    enabled     = true
    kms_key_arn = aws_kms_key.dynamodb.arn
  }

  tags = {
    Environment = var.environment
    Application = "anihpj"
    ManagedBy   = "Terraform"
  }
}

# User Activity Table with Streams
resource "aws_dynamodb_table" "user_activity" {
  name           = "anihpj-user-activity-${var.environment}"
  billing_mode   = var.billing_mode
  hash_key       = "user_id"
  range_key      = "timestamp"

  attribute {
    name = "user_id"
    type = "N"
  }

  attribute {
    name = "timestamp"
    type = "S"
  }

  attribute {
    name = "action_type"
    type = "S"
  }

  global_secondary_index {
    name            = "ActionTypeIndex"
    hash_key        = "action_type"
    range_key       = "timestamp"
    projection_type = "ALL"
  }

  stream_enabled   = true
  stream_view_type = "NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES"

  point_in_time_recovery {
    enabled = true
  }

  server_side_encryption {
    enabled = true
  }

  tags = {
    Environment = var.environment
    Application = "anihpj"
    ManagedBy   = "Terraform"
  }
}

# KMS Key for Encryption
resource "aws_kms_key" "dynamodb" {
  description             = "KMS key for DynamoDB table encryption (anihpj)"
  deletion_window_in_days = 10
  enable_key_rotation     = true

  tags = {
    Environment = var.environment
    Application = "anihpj"
  }
}

# Outputs
output "user_sessions_table_name" {
  description = "User sessions table name"
  value       = aws_dynamodb_table.user_sessions.name
}

output "user_activity_table_name" {
  description = "User activity table name"
  value       = aws_dynamodb_table.user_activity.name
}

output "user_activity_stream_arn" {
  description = "Stream ARN for user activity table"
  value       = aws_dynamodb_table.user_activity.stream_arn
}
🔄 CI/CD Pipeline Configuration
# .github/workflows/deploy-dynamodb.yml - GitHub Actions CI/CD pipeline
name: Deploy DynamoDB Infrastructure

on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - staging
    paths:
      - 'infrastructure/**'
  pull_request:
    branches:
      - main
      - staging

env:
  AWS_REGION: us-east-1
  TERRAFORM_VERSION: 1.6.0

jobs:
  validate:
    name: Validate Infrastructure Code
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Setup Terraform
        uses: hashicorp/setup-terraform@v3
        with:
          terraform_version: ${{ env.TERRAFORM_VERSION }}
      
      - name: Terraform Format Check
        run: terraform fmt -check -recursive
        working-directory: infrastructure/terraform
      
      - name: Terraform Init
        run: terraform init
        working-directory: infrastructure/terraform
      
      - name: Terraform Validate
        run: terraform validate
        working-directory: infrastructure/terraform
      
      - name: CloudFormation Lint
        uses: scottbrenner/cfn-lint-action@v2
        with:
          args: infrastructure/*.yaml

  plan:
    name: Plan Infrastructure Changes
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: validate
    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request'
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Configure AWS Credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          aws-access-key-id: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          aws-secret-access-key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}
      
      - name: Setup Terraform
        uses: hashicorp/setup-terraform@v3
        with:
          terraform_version: ${{ env.TERRAFORM_VERSION }}
      
      - name: Terraform Plan
        id: plan
        run: |
          terraform init
          terraform plan -out=tfplan
        working-directory: infrastructure/terraform
      
      - name: Comment PR with Plan
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const output = `#### Terraform Plan 📋
            \`\`\`
            ${{ steps.plan.outputs.stdout }}
            \`\`\`
            
            *Pushed by: @${{ github.actor }}, Action: \`${{ github.event_name }}\`*`;
            
            github.rest.issues.createComment({
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              body: output
            })

  deploy-staging:
    name: Deploy to Staging
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: validate
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/staging' && github.event_name == 'push'
    environment: staging
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Configure AWS Credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          aws-access-key-id: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          aws-secret-access-key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}
      
      - name: Setup Terraform
        uses: hashicorp/setup-terraform@v3
        with:
          terraform_version: ${{ env.TERRAFORM_VERSION }}
      
      - name: Deploy Infrastructure
        run: |
          terraform init
          terraform apply -auto-approve -var="environment=staging"
        working-directory: infrastructure/terraform
      
      - name: Run Smoke Tests
        run: |
          python scripts/test_dynamodb_tables.py --environment staging

  deploy-production:
    name: Deploy to Production
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: validate
    if: github.ref == 'refs/heads/main' && github.event_name == 'push'
    environment: production
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
      
      - name: Configure AWS Credentials
        uses: aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials@v4
        with:
          aws-access-key-id: ${{ secrets.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
          aws-secret-access-key: ${{ secrets.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
          aws-region: ${{ env.AWS_REGION }}
      
      - name: Create Backup Before Deployment
        run: |
          aws dynamodb create-backup \
            --table-name anihpj-user-sessions-production \
            --backup-name pre-deployment-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
          
          aws dynamodb create-backup \
            --table-name anihpj-user-activity-production \
            --backup-name pre-deployment-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S)
      
      - name: Setup Terraform
        uses: hashicorp/setup-terraform@v3
        with:
          terraform_version: ${{ env.TERRAFORM_VERSION }}
      
      - name: Deploy Infrastructure
        run: |
          terraform init
          terraform apply -auto-approve -var="environment=production"
        working-directory: infrastructure/terraform
      
      - name: Run Production Smoke Tests
        run: |
          python scripts/test_dynamodb_tables.py --environment production
      
      - name: Notify Success
        uses: 8398a7/action-slack@v3
        with:
          status: ${{ job.status }}
          text: 'DynamoDB production deployment completed successfully!'
          webhook_url: ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK }}
        if: success()
      
      - name: Notify Failure
        uses: 8398a7/action-slack@v3
        with:
          status: ${{ job.status }}
          text: 'DynamoDB production deployment FAILED! Please investigate.'
          webhook_url: ${{ secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK }}
        if: failure()
🐍 Automated Testing Script
# scripts/test_dynamodb_tables.py - Smoke tests for DynamoDB deployment
import boto3
import sys
import argparse
from datetime import datetime

class DynamoDBDeploymentTester:
    """Test DynamoDB tables after deployment"""
    
    def __init__(self, environment):
        self.environment = environment
        self.dynamodb = boto3.resource('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
        self.client = boto3.client('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
        self.test_results = []
    
    def test_table_exists(self, table_name):
        """Test if table exists"""
        try:
            table = self.dynamodb.Table(table_name)
            table.load()
            self.test_results.append({
                'test': f'Table Exists: {table_name}',
                'status': 'PASS'
            })
            return True
        except Exception as e:
            self.test_results.append({
                'test': f'Table Exists: {table_name}',
                'status': 'FAIL',
                'error': str(e)
            })
            return False
    
    def test_encryption_enabled(self, table_name):
        """Test if encryption is enabled"""
        try:
            response = self.client.describe_table(TableName=table_name)
            sse = response['Table'].get('SSEDescription', {})
            
            if sse.get('Status') == 'ENABLED':
                self.test_results.append({
                    'test': f'Encryption Enabled: {table_name}',
                    'status': 'PASS'
                })
                return True
            else:
                self.test_results.append({
                    'test': f'Encryption Enabled: {table_name}',
                    'status': 'FAIL',
                    'error': 'Encryption not enabled'
                })
                return False
        except Exception as e:
            self.test_results.append({
                'test': f'Encryption Enabled: {table_name}',
                'status': 'FAIL',
                'error': str(e)
            })
            return False
    
    def test_pitr_enabled(self, table_name):
        """Test if Point-in-Time Recovery is enabled"""
        try:
            response = self.client.describe_continuous_backups(TableName=table_name)
            pitr = response['ContinuousBackupsDescription']['PointInTimeRecoveryDescription']
            
            if pitr.get('PointInTimeRecoveryStatus') == 'ENABLED':
                self.test_results.append({
                    'test': f'PITR Enabled: {table_name}',
                    'status': 'PASS'
                })
                return True
            else:
                self.test_results.append({
                    'test': f'PITR Enabled: {table_name}',
                    'status': 'FAIL',
                    'error': 'PITR not enabled'
                })
                return False
        except Exception as e:
            self.test_results.append({
                'test': f'PITR Enabled: {table_name}',
                'status': 'FAIL',
                'error': str(e)
            })
            return False
    
    def test_read_write_operations(self, table_name):
        """Test basic read/write operations"""
        try:
            table = self.dynamodb.Table(table_name)
            
            # Test write
            test_item = {
                'session_key' if 'sessions' in table_name else 'user_id': 'test-key-123',
                'timestamp': datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
                'test_data': f'deployment-test-{self.environment}'
            }
            table.put_item(Item=test_item)
            
            # Test read
            key = {'session_key': 'test-key-123'} if 'sessions' in table_name else {'user_id': 'test-key-123'}
            response = table.get_item(Key=key)
            
            # Clean up test data
            table.delete_item(Key=key)
            
            if 'Item' in response:
                self.test_results.append({
                    'test': f'Read/Write Operations: {table_name}',
                    'status': 'PASS'
                })
                return True
            else:
                self.test_results.append({
                    'test': f'Read/Write Operations: {table_name}',
                    'status': 'FAIL',
                    'error': 'Item not found after write'
                })
                return False
        except Exception as e:
            self.test_results.append({
                'test': f'Read/Write Operations: {table_name}',
                'status': 'FAIL',
                'error': str(e)
            })
            return False
    
    def run_all_tests(self):
        """Run all deployment tests"""
        tables = [
            f'anihpj-user-sessions-{self.environment}',
            f'anihpj-user-activity-{self.environment}',
            f'anihpj-job-analytics-{self.environment}'
        ]
        
        print(f"\n{'='*60}")
        print(f"Running DynamoDB Deployment Tests - {self.environment.upper()}")
        print(f"{'='*60}\n")
        
        for table_name in tables:
            print(f"Testing {table_name}...")
            
            if not self.test_table_exists(table_name):
                continue
            
            self.test_encryption_enabled(table_name)
            self.test_pitr_enabled(table_name)
            self.test_read_write_operations(table_name)
        
        # Print results
        print(f"\n{'='*60}")
        print("TEST RESULTS")
        print(f"{'='*60}\n")
        
        passed = sum(1 for r in self.test_results if r['status'] == 'PASS')
        failed = sum(1 for r in self.test_results if r['status'] == 'FAIL')
        
        for result in self.test_results:
            status_symbol = '✅' if result['status'] == 'PASS' else '❌'
            print(f"{status_symbol} {result['test']}: {result['status']}")
            if result.get('error'):
                print(f"   Error: {result['error']}")
        
        print(f"\n{'='*60}")
        print(f"Total: {len(self.test_results)} | Passed: {passed} | Failed: {failed}")
        print(f"{'='*60}\n")
        
        # Exit with error code if any tests failed
        if failed > 0:
            sys.exit(1)
        else:
            print("✅ All tests passed!")
            sys.exit(0)

if __name__ == '__main__':
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description='Test DynamoDB deployment')
    parser.add_argument('--environment', required=True, choices=['development', 'staging', 'production'])
    args = parser.parse_args()
    
    tester = DynamoDBDeploymentTester(args.environment)
    tester.run_all_tests()
🚀 Deployment Best Practices for anihpj:
  • Infrastructure as Code: Use Terraform/CloudFormation for all table definitions
  • Environment Separation: Maintain separate tables for dev/staging/production
  • Version Control: Track all infrastructure changes in Git
  • Automated Testing: Run smoke tests after every deployment
  • Pre-Deployment Backups: Always backup critical tables before changes
  • Gradual Rollout: Deploy to staging first, then production
  • Monitoring: Set up CloudWatch alarms before deployment
  • Rollback Plan: Document and test rollback procedures
  • Change Management: Use pull requests for infrastructure changes
  • Cost Estimation: Review pricing impact before deployment

🔄 Migration

Strategies and tools for migrating data to DynamoDB from other databases for the anihpj job portal.

📦 Migration Implementation
# migration/dynamodb_migration_manager.py - Database migration utilities
import boto3
from django.db import connection
import json
from datetime import datetime
from decimal import Decimal

class DynamoDBMigrationManager:
    """Manage migrations from PostgreSQL/MySQL to DynamoDB for anihpj"""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.dynamodb = boto3.resource('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
        self.client = boto3.client('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
    
    # MIGRATION FROM SQL DATABASES
    def migrate_user_activities_from_sql(self, batch_size=100):
        """Migrate user activities from SQL database to DynamoDB"""
        
        activities_table = self.dynamodb.Table('anihpj_user_activities')
        
        with connection.cursor() as cursor:
            cursor.execute("""
                SELECT user_id, activity_type, activity_data, created_at, session_id
                FROM user_activities ORDER BY created_at
            """)
            rows = cursor.fetchall()
        
        migration_stats = {'total_records': len(rows), 'migrated': 0, 'failed': 0}
        
        with activities_table.batch_writer() as batch:
            for row in rows:
                try:
                    item = {
                        'user_id': str(row[0]),
                        'timestamp': row[3].isoformat() if row[3] else datetime.utcnow().isoformat(),
                        'activity_type': row[1],
                        'activity_data': json.loads(row[2]) if row[2] else {},
                        'session_id': row[4] if row[4] else 'unknown'
                    }
                    batch.put_item(Item=item)
                    migration_stats['migrated'] += 1
                except Exception as e:
                    migration_stats['failed'] += 1
        
        return migration_stats
    
    # SCHEMA TRANSFORMATION
    def transform_relational_to_nosql(self, sql_data, entity_type):
        """Transform relational data to DynamoDB single-table design"""
        
        if entity_type == 'user_profile':
            return {
                'PK': f"USER#{sql_data['user_id']}",
                'SK': f"PROFILE#{sql_data['user_id']}",
                'GSI1PK': f"EMAIL#{sql_data['email']}",
                'entity_type': 'user_profile',
                'user_id': str(sql_data['user_id']),
                'email': sql_data['email'],
                'created_at': sql_data['created_at'].isoformat()
            }
        elif entity_type == 'job_posting':
            return {
                'PK': f"JOB#{sql_data['job_id']}",
                'SK': f"METADATA#{sql_data['job_id']}",
                'GSI1PK': f"CATEGORY#{sql_data['category']}",
                'entity_type': 'job_posting',
                'job_id': str(sql_data['job_id']),
                'title': sql_data['title'],
                'category': sql_data['category']
            }
    
    # VALIDATION
    def validate_migration(self, source_table, target_table, sample_size=1000):
        """Validate migrated data integrity"""
        
        with connection.cursor() as cursor:
            cursor.execute(f"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM {source_table}")
            source_count = cursor.fetchone()[0]
        
        dynamodb_table = self.dynamodb.Table(target_table)
        target_count = dynamodb_table.item_count
        
        validation_results = {
            'source_count': source_count,
            'target_count': target_count,
            'status': 'pass' if source_count == target_count else 'fail',
            'variance': abs(source_count - target_count)
        }
        
        return validation_results

migration_manager = DynamoDBMigrationManager()
🔀 Migration Strategies
Strategy Method Downtime Best For
Batch Migration Python scripts Hours Small datasets
AWS DMS Full load + CDC Minutes Large datasets
Dual Write Write to both DBs Zero Gradual migration
🔄 anihpj Migration Plan:
  • Phase 1: Migrate read-heavy tables (analytics)
  • Phase 2: Implement dual-write for critical tables
  • Phase 3: Migrate write-heavy tables with DMS
  • Phase 4: Switch read traffic incrementally
  • Phase 5: Decommission PostgreSQL after validation

🛡️ Compliance & Governance

Security frameworks and compliance standards for DynamoDB in the anihpj job portal.

📜 Compliance Implementation
# compliance/dynamodb_compliance_manager.py
import boto3
from datetime import datetime

class DynamoDBComplianceManager:
    """Manage compliance for anihpj DynamoDB tables"""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.client = boto3.client('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
        self.config_client = boto3.client('config', region_name='us-east-1')
    
    # GDPR COMPLIANCE
    def implement_gdpr_controls(self, table_name):
        """Implement GDPR compliance controls"""
        
        gdpr_controls = {
            'table_name': table_name,
            'compliance_framework': 'GDPR',
            'controls': [
                {'control': 'right_to_deletion', 'status': 'implemented'},
                {'control': 'data_encryption', 'status': 'compliant'},
                {'control': 'access_logging', 'status': 'implemented'},
                {'control': 'data_minimization', 'status': 'compliant'}
            ]
        }
        
        return gdpr_controls
    
    # AWS CONFIG RULES
    def setup_config_rules(self):
        """Set up AWS Config rules for compliance"""
        
        config_rules = [
            {
                'ConfigRuleName': 'dynamodb-encryption-enabled',
                'Source': {
                    'Owner': 'AWS',
                    'SourceIdentifier': 'DYNAMODB_TABLE_ENCRYPTED_KMS'
                }
            },
            {
                'ConfigRuleName': 'dynamodb-pitr-enabled',
                'Source': {
                    'Owner': 'AWS',
                    'SourceIdentifier': 'DYNAMODB_PITR_ENABLED'
                }
            }
        ]
        
        created_rules = []
        for rule in config_rules:
            try:
                self.config_client.put_config_rule(ConfigRule=rule)
                created_rules.append(rule['ConfigRuleName'])
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"Failed to create rule: {e}")
        
        return {'success': True, 'created_rules': created_rules}
    
    # DATA CLASSIFICATION
    def classify_data(self, table_name):
        """Classify data sensitivity level"""
        
        classifications = {
            'anihpj_user_activities': 'PII - Personal Identifiable Information',
            'anihpj_job_analytics': 'Confidential - Business Data',
            'anihpj_notifications': 'Internal - User Communications'
        }
        
        return classifications.get(table_name, 'Unclassified')

compliance_manager = DynamoDBComplianceManager()
✅ Compliance Frameworks
Framework Requirements DynamoDB Controls Status
GDPR Data protection Encryption, TTL, deletion APIs ✅ Compliant
HIPAA PHI protection KMS encryption, CloudTrail ✅ Compliant
SOC 2 Security, availability Encryption, backups, monitoring ✅ Compliant
🛡️ anihpj Compliance Requirements:
  • Data Protection: KMS encryption for all PII tables
  • Access Control: IAM least privilege policies
  • Audit Logging: CloudTrail for all operations
  • Data Retention: TTL policies for auto-expiration
  • Right to Deletion: User data deletion APIs
  • Regular Audits: Quarterly compliance reviews

📖 Glossary

Common DynamoDB terminology and concepts.

  • Partition Key: Primary key attribute that determines data distribution
  • Sort Key: Optional secondary key for range queries within partition
  • GSI (Global Secondary Index): Alternative key schema for different query patterns
  • LSI (Local Secondary Index): Alternative sort key within same partition
  • RCU (Read Capacity Unit): One strongly consistent read per second for 4KB
  • WCU (Write Capacity Unit): One write per second for 1KB
  • PITR (Point-in-Time Recovery): Continuous backups for 35 days
  • TTL (Time To Live): Automatic item expiration
  • DynamoDB Streams: Change data capture for real-time processing
  • Global Tables: Multi-region, fully replicated tables

📝 Changelog

Recent updates and changes to DynamoDB features and the anihpj implementation.

🆕 2025 Updates
Date Feature Description Impact on anihpj
Nov 2025 Enhanced Auto-Scaling Faster scaling response, ML-based predictions Better handling of job posting spikes
Oct 2025 Standard-IA Pricing Reduction 20% price reduction for infrequent access tables Cost savings on archived job applications
Sep 2025 Global Tables v3 Improved conflict resolution, faster replication Enhanced multi-region user experience
Aug 2025 PartiQL Enhancements SQL-like queries with improved performance Easier analytics queries for reporting
Jul 2025 DynamoDB Export to S3 Export full tables to S3 without affecting performance Data lake integration for ML training
📅 2024 Updates
Date Feature Description Impact on anihpj
Dec 2024 Increased Item Size Maximum item size increased to 500KB Store more detailed job descriptions
Nov 2024 CloudWatch Contributor Insights Identify most frequently accessed items Optimize popular job queries
Sep 2024 Kinesis Data Streams Integration Direct streaming to Kinesis from DynamoDB Streams Real-time analytics pipeline
Jun 2024 Table Classes Standard vs Standard-IA for cost optimization 30% cost reduction on old data
Mar 2024 On-Demand Throughput Increase Doubled default on-demand capacity Handle larger traffic spikes
🏗️ anihpj Implementation Changelog
Version 2.0.0 (November 2025)
  • Added: Global tables for US-East and EU-West regions
  • Added: DynamoDB Streams integration with Lambda for real-time notifications
  • Added: PartiQL support for analytics queries
  • Improved: Auto-scaling configuration with ML-based predictions
  • Fixed: Hot partition issue on user_activity table
  • Security: Enabled customer-managed KMS keys for all tables
Version 1.5.0 (August 2025)
  • Added: Standard-IA table class for archived applications
  • Added: Automated backup strategy with AWS Backup
  • Added: TTL configuration for session cleanup
  • Improved: GSI design for better query performance
  • Performance: Reduced average query latency by 40%
Version 1.0.0 (March 2025)
  • Initial: DynamoDB integration for session management
  • Initial: User activity tracking table
  • Initial: Job analytics table with daily aggregations
  • Initial: Notification delivery system
  • Initial: Point-in-time recovery enabled
📊 Upcoming Features (Roadmap):
  • Q1 2026: DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) for sub-millisecond caching
  • Q1 2026: Advanced analytics with Athena and S3 export
  • Q2 2026: Machine learning integration for job recommendations
  • Q2 2026: Multi-region active-active architecture
  • Q3 2026: Zero-downtime table schema migrations
  • Q4 2026: Enhanced security with fine-grained access control
🔔 Stay Updated

Subscribe to AWS Database What's New for the latest DynamoDB announcements and features.

Follow the AWS Database Blog for in-depth articles and best practices.

⚖️ Service Comparison

Comprehensive comparison of DynamoDB with other AWS database services to help you choose the right solution for anihpj.

Feature DynamoDB RDS Aurora
Type NoSQL Key-Value Relational (SQL) Relational (SQL)
Performance Single-digit ms 10-50ms typical 5-15ms typical
Scaling Automatic, unlimited Manual, limited Automatic, limited
Best For High-scale apps, simple queries Complex queries, ACID High throughput, MySQL compat
💡 When to Use DynamoDB for anihpj:
  • User Activity Tracking: High-volume writes with simple lookups
  • Real-time Notifications: Fast reads/writes, auto-scaling
  • Session Management: TTL for auto-cleanup
  • Job Analytics: Time-series data with DynamoDB Streams
  • Trend Analysis: Monitor weekly patterns to optimize capacity and costs
  • Health Checks: Implement automated health reports and anomaly detection
  • Cost Monitoring: Track spend trends and optimize billing modes accordingly

    • 🌊 Advanced Topics - DynamoDB Streams

    Real-time data processing capabilities for anihpj using DynamoDB Streams to trigger automatic actions and maintain data consistency.

    🔄 Stream Processing Use Cases
    Use Case Trigger Event anihpj Action Implementation
    🔔 Notifications New job application Send employer alert Lambda + SES/SNS
    📊 Analytics User activity logged Update job recommendations Lambda + ML Pipeline
    🔄 Data Sync Profile updated Sync to search index Lambda + Elasticsearch
    🎯 Personalization Job viewed/applied Update ML model features Kinesis Data Firehose
    🛡️ Audit Sensitive data access Log to compliance system Lambda + CloudWatch Logs
    🌊 DynamoDB Streams Architecture
    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         DYNAMODB STREAMS PROCESSING ARCHITECTURE                       │
│                                    anihpj Implementation                                │
│                                                                                         │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                              DATA FLOW DIAGRAM                                     │ │
│  │                                                                                     │ │
│  │   📊 DynamoDB Tables                   🌊 Streams                🚀 Lambda Functions │ │
│  │   ┌─────────────────────┐              ┌─────────────────┐      ┌──────────────────┐ │ │
│  │   │  anihpj-user-       │──── INSERT ──▶│ User Activity  │─────▶│ Recommendation   │ │ │
│  │   │  activity           │              │ Stream          │      │ Engine Lambda    │ │ │
│  │   │                     │──── MODIFY ──▶│                │      │                  │ │ │
│  │   │ PK: user_id         │              │ Stream ARN:     │      │ ┌──────────────┐ │ │ │
│  │   │ SK: timestamp       │──── REMOVE ──▶│ arn:aws:...     │      │ │ Process:     │ │ │ │
│  │   │                     │              │                │      │ │ • Update ML  │ │ │ │
│  │   │ • action_type       │              │ Shard Count: 2  │      │ │ • Send notif │ │ │ │
│  │   │ • action_data       │              │ Retention: 24h  │      │ │ • Sync search│ │ │ │
│  │   │ • session_id        │              │                │      │ └──────────────┘ │ │ │
│  │   └─────────────────────┘              └─────────────────┘      └──────────────────┘ │ │
│  │                                                │                                     │ │
│  │   ┌─────────────────────┐              ┌─────────────────┐      ┌──────────────────┐ │ │
│  │   │  anihpj-job-        │──── INSERT ──▶│ Job Analytics  │─────▶│ Employer         │ │ │
│  │   │  analytics          │              │ Stream          │      │ Dashboard Lambda │ │ │
│  │   │                     │──── MODIFY ──▶│                │      │                  │ │ │
│  │   │ PK: job_id          │              │ View Patterns:  │      │ ┌──────────────┐ │ │ │
│  │   │ SK: date            │              │ • NEW_IMAGE     │      │ │ Process:     │ │ │ │
│  │   │                     │              │ • OLD_IMAGE     │      │ │ • Update     │ │ │ │
│  │   │ • view_count        │              │ • KEYS_ONLY     │      │ │   dashboard  │ │ │ │
│  │   │ • application_count │              │ • NEW_AND_OLD   │      │ │ • Send       │ │ │ │
│  │   │ • conversion_rate   │              │                │      │ │   reports    │ │ │ │
│  │   └─────────────────────┘              └─────────────────┘      │ └──────────────┘ │ │ │
│  │                                                │                └──────────────────┘ │ │
│  │                                        ┌─────────────────┐                          │ │
│  │                                        │ Stream Record   │                          │ │
│  │                                        │ ┌─────────────┐ │      ┌──────────────────┐ │ │
│  │                                        │ │ eventName   │ │─────▶│ Additional       │ │ │
│  │                                        │ │ eventSource │ │      │ Processing:      │ │ │
│  │                                        │ │ awsRegion   │ │      │ • SNS Topics     │ │ │
│  │                                        │ │ dynamodb:   │ │      │ • SQS Queues     │ │ │
│  │                                        │ │   Keys      │ │      │ • Kinesis        │ │ │
│  │                                        │ │   NewImage  │ │      │ • EventBridge    │ │ │
│  │                                        │ │   OldImage  │ │      │ • Step Functions │ │ │
│  │                                        │ └─────────────┘ │      └──────────────────┘ │ │
│  │                                        └─────────────────┘                          │ │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                         │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                               STREAM CONFIGURATION                                 │ │
│  │                                                                                     │ │
│  │  📋 Stream Settings:                                                               │ │
│  │  ├─ Stream View Type: NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES (capture before/after)                   │ │
│  │  ├─ Shard Count: Auto-managed based on write volume                               │ │
│  │  ├─ Retention Period: 24 hours (default)                                          │ │
│  │  ├─ Lambda Trigger: Event-driven processing                                       │ │
│  │  └─ Error Handling: DLQ for failed records                                        │ │
│  │                                                                                     │ │
│  │  ⚡ Processing Guarantees:                                                         │ │
│  │  ├─ Exactly-once processing per shard                                             │ │
│  │  ├─ Ordered processing within partition key                                       │ │
│  │  ├─ At-least-once delivery guarantee                                              │ │
│  │  └─ Sub-second latency for stream processing                                      │ │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    🔧 Stream Processing Lambda Functions
    # lambda_functions/user_activity_processor.py
import json
import boto3
import logging
from decimal import Decimal

# Initialize AWS services
dynamodb = boto3.resource('dynamodb')
sns = boto3.client('sns')
ses = boto3.client('ses')

# Configure logging
logger = logging.getLogger()
logger.setLevel(logging.INFO)

def lambda_handler(event, context):
    """Process DynamoDB stream records for user activity"""
    
    processed_records = 0
    failed_records = 0
    
    for record in event['Records']:
        try:
            # Process each stream record
            process_stream_record(record)
            processed_records += 1
            
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Error processing record: {str(e)}")
            failed_records += 1
            
            # Send to DLQ for manual review
            send_to_dlq(record, str(e))
    
    logger.info(f"Processed: {processed_records}, Failed: {failed_records}")
    
    return {
        'statusCode': 200,
        'body': json.dumps({
            'processed': processed_records,
            'failed': failed_records
        })
    }

def process_stream_record(record):
    """Process individual stream record based on event type"""
    
    event_name = record['eventName']  # INSERT, MODIFY, REMOVE
    table_name = record['eventSourceARN'].split('/')[-3]
    
    logger.info(f"Processing {event_name} event from {table_name}")
    
    if event_name == 'INSERT':
        handle_new_activity(record)
    elif event_name == 'MODIFY':
        handle_activity_update(record)
    elif event_name == 'REMOVE':
        handle_activity_removal(record)

def handle_new_activity(record):
    """Handle new user activity insertion"""
    
    # Extract data from stream record
    new_image = record['dynamodb']['NewImage']
    
    user_id = int(new_image['user_id']['N'])
    action_type = new_image['action_type']['S']
    timestamp = new_image['timestamp']['S']
    
    action_data = {}
    if 'action_data' in new_image:
        action_data = json.loads(new_image['action_data']['S'])
    
    logger.info(f"New activity: User {user_id} performed {action_type}")
    
    # Route to appropriate handlers
    if action_type == 'apply':
        handle_job_application(user_id, action_data, timestamp)
    elif action_type == 'view_job':
        handle_job_view(user_id, action_data, timestamp)
    elif action_type == 'search':
        handle_job_search(user_id, action_data, timestamp)
    elif action_type == 'profile_update':
        handle_profile_update(user_id, action_data, timestamp)

def handle_job_application(user_id, action_data, timestamp):
    """Handle job application events"""
    
    job_id = action_data.get('job_id')
    employer_id = action_data.get('employer_id')
    
    if not job_id or not employer_id:
        logger.warning("Missing job_id or employer_id in application data")
        return
    
    try:
        # 1. Send notification to employer
        send_employer_notification(employer_id, job_id, user_id)
        
        # 2. Update job analytics
        update_job_application_count(job_id)
        
        # 3. Trigger recommendation engine update
        trigger_recommendation_update(user_id, 'application', {'job_id': job_id})
        
        # 4. Log to analytics pipeline
        send_to_analytics_pipeline({
            'event_type': 'job_application',
            'user_id': user_id,
            'job_id': job_id,
            'employer_id': employer_id,
            'timestamp': timestamp
        })
        
        logger.info(f"Processed job application: User {user_id} -> Job {job_id}")
        
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Error handling job application: {str(e)}")
        raise

def handle_job_view(user_id, action_data, timestamp):
    """Handle job view events for analytics and recommendations"""
    
    job_id = action_data.get('job_id')
    
    if not job_id:
        return
    
    try:
        # 1. Update job view analytics
        update_job_view_count(job_id)
        
        # 2. Update user interest profile
        update_user_interests(user_id, action_data)
        
        # 3. Check for trending jobs
        check_trending_status(job_id)
        
        logger.info(f"Processed job view: User {user_id} viewed Job {job_id}")
        
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Error handling job view: {str(e)}")
        raise

def send_employer_notification(employer_id, job_id, applicant_user_id):
    """Send real-time notification to employer about new application"""
    
    try:
        # Get employer details
        users_table = dynamodb.Table('anihpj-users')  # Assuming Django User table in DynamoDB
        employer_response = users_table.get_item(
            Key={'user_id': employer_id}
        )
        
        if 'Item' not in employer_response:
            logger.warning(f"Employer {employer_id} not found")
            return
        
        employer_email = employer_response['Item'].get('email')
        employer_name = employer_response['Item'].get('first_name', 'Employer')
        
        # Get job details
        jobs_table = dynamodb.Table('anihpj-jobs')
        job_response = jobs_table.get_item(
            Key={'job_id': job_id}
        )
        
        job_title = "Unknown Job"
        if 'Item' in job_response:
            job_title = job_response['Item'].get('title', 'Unknown Job')
        
        # Send email notification
        email_subject = f"New Application for {job_title}"
        email_body = f"""
        Hi {employer_name},
        
        You have received a new job application for "{job_title}".
        
        Application Details:
        - Job ID: {job_id}
        - Applicant ID: {applicant_user_id}
        - Applied at: {timezone.now().strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S')}
        
        Please log in to your anihpj dashboard to review the application.
        
        Best regards,
        anihpj Team
        """
        
        ses.send_email(
            Source='noreply@anihpj.com',
            Destination={'ToAddresses': [employer_email]},
            Message={
                'Subject': {'Data': email_subject},
                'Body': {'Text': {'Data': email_body}}
            }
        )
        
        # Also send SNS notification for real-time dashboard updates  
        sns.publish(
            TopicArn='arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-employer-notifications',
            Subject='New Job Application',
            Message=json.dumps({
                'type': 'new_application',
                'employer_id': employer_id,
                'job_id': job_id,
                'applicant_user_id': applicant_user_id,
                'job_title': job_title
            })
        )
        
        logger.info(f"Sent employer notification: {employer_id} for job {job_id}")
        
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Error sending employer notification: {str(e)}")
        raise

def update_job_application_count(job_id):
    """Atomically increment job application count"""
    
    try:
        analytics_table = dynamodb.Table('anihpj-job-analytics')
        today = timezone.now().date().isoformat()
        
        analytics_table.update_item(
            Key={
                'job_id': job_id,
                'date': today
            },
            UpdateExpression='ADD application_count :inc SET last_updated = :timestamp',
            ExpressionAttributeValues={
                ':inc': 1,
                ':timestamp': timezone.now().isoformat()
            }
        )
        
        logger.info(f"Updated application count for job {job_id}")
        
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Error updating job application count: {str(e)}")
        raise

def trigger_recommendation_update(user_id, activity_type, activity_data):
    """Trigger ML recommendation engine update"""
    
    try:
        # Send to recommendation processing queue
        sqs = boto3.client('sqs')
        
        message = {
            'user_id': user_id,
            'activity_type': activity_type,
            'activity_data': activity_data,
            'timestamp': timezone.now().isoformat(),
            'processing_priority': 'high' if activity_type == 'application' else 'normal'
        }
        
        sqs.send_message(
            QueueUrl='https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-recommendation-updates',
            MessageBody=json.dumps(message, default=str)
        )
        
        logger.info(f"Triggered recommendation update for user {user_id}")
        
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Error triggering recommendation update: {str(e)}")

def send_to_dlq(record, error_message):
    """Send failed records to Dead Letter Queue"""
    
    try:
        sqs = boto3.client('sqs')
        
        dlq_message = {
            'original_record': record,
            'error_message': error_message,
            'timestamp': timezone.now().isoformat(),
            'lambda_function': context.function_name
        }
        
        sqs.send_message(
            QueueUrl='https://sqs.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/123456789012/anihpj-stream-processing-dlq',
            MessageBody=json.dumps(dlq_message, default=str)
        )
        
    except Exception as dlq_error:
        logger.error(f"Error sending to DLQ: {str(dlq_error)}")

# Helper function for analytics pipeline
def send_to_analytics_pipeline(event_data):
    """Send processed events to analytics pipeline"""
    
    try:
        firehose = boto3.client('firehose')
        
        # Send to Kinesis Data Firehose for S3 storage and analytics
        firehose.put_record(
            DeliveryStreamName='anihpj-user-events-stream',
            Record={
                'Data': json.dumps(event_data, default=str) + '\n'
            }
        )
        
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Error sending to analytics pipeline: {str(e)}")
    🌊 Stream Processing Benefits for anihpj:
    • Real-time Notifications: Instant alerts for job applications and matches
    • Live Analytics: Update dashboards and reports as data changes
    • Data Consistency: Automatically sync data across multiple systems
    • Personalization: Update ML models and recommendations in real-time
    • Audit Trail: Comprehensive logging of all data changes
    • Scalability: Handles millions of events per second automatically

    🌍 Advanced Topics - Global Tables

    Multi-region deployment capabilities for anihpj to provide low-latency access worldwide and disaster recovery.

    🗺️ Global Deployment Strategy
    Region Primary Use Case anihpj Market Data Residency
    🇺🇸 us-east-1 Primary region North America US/Canada compliance
    🇪🇺 eu-west-1 European operations Europe, Africa GDPR compliance
    🇸🇬 ap-southeast-1 Asia-Pacific hub Southeast Asia Local data laws
    🇯🇵 ap-northeast-1 Japan market Japan, Korea Japan privacy laws
    🇦🇺 ap-southeast-2 Australia/NZ Australia, New Zealand Australian Privacy Act
    🌐 Global Tables Architecture

│                              anihpj Worldwide Deployment                               │
│                                                                                         │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │

│  │                                                                                     │ │
│  │   🇺🇸 US East (N. Virginia)           🇪🇺 Europe (Ireland)                         │ │
