│  │   │  anihpj-user-sessions   │◄────────┤  anihpj-user-sessions   │                 │ │
│  │   │  ├─ Read: 2ms latency   │   ▲     │  ├─ Read: 2ms latency   │                 │ │
│  │   │  ├─ Write: 3ms latency  │   │     │  ├─ Write: 3ms latency  │                 │ │
│  │   │  └─ Items: 45,672       │   │     │  └─ Items: 45,672       │                 │ │
│  │   │                         │   │     │                         │                 │ │
│  │   │  anihpj-user-activity   │   │     │  anihpj-user-activity   │                 │ │
│  │   │  ├─ Read: 1ms latency   │   │     │  ├─ Read: 1ms latency   │                 │ │
│  │   │  ├─ Write: 2ms latency  │   │     │  ├─ Write: 2ms latency  │                 │ │
│  │   │  └─ Items: 1.2M         │   │     │  └─ Items: 1.2M         │                 │ │
│  │   └─────────────────────────┘   │     └─────────────────────────┘                 │ │
│  │             │                   │               │                                 │ │
│  │             │    Bi-directional Replication    │                                 │ │
│  │             │      ⚡ Sub-second sync          │                                 │ │
│  │             ▼                   │               ▼                                 │ │
│  │   ┌─────────────────────────┐   │     ┌─────────────────────────┐                 │ │
│  │   │🇸🇬 Asia Pacific         │   │     │🇯🇵 Asia Pacific         │                 │ │
│  │   │   (Singapore)           │   │     │   (Tokyo)               │                 │ │
│  │   │  anihpj-user-sessions   │   │     │  anihpj-user-sessions   │                 │ │
│  │   │  ├─ Read: 2ms latency   │   │     │  ├─ Read: 2ms latency   │                 │ │
│  │   │  ├─ Write: 3ms latency  │   │     │  ├─ Write: 3ms latency  │                 │ │
│  │   │  └─ Items: 45,672       │   │     │  └─ Items: 45,672       │                 │ │
│  │   │                         │   │     │                         │                 │ │
│  │   │  anihpj-user-activity   │───┘     │  anihpj-user-activity   │                 │ │
│  │   │  ├─ Read: 1ms latency   │         │  ├─ Read: 1ms latency   │                 │ │
│  │   │  ├─ Write: 2ms latency  │         │  ├─ Write: 2ms latency  │                 │ │
│  │   │  └─ Items: 1.2M         │         │  └─ Items: 1.2M         │                 │ │
│  │   └─────────────────────────┘         └─────────────────────────┘                 │ │
│  │                                                                                     │ │
│  │           🔄 Automatic Conflict Resolution                                          │ │
│  │           Last Writer Wins (LWW) with Timestamps                                   │ │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                         │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                              ROUTING STRATEGY                                      │ │
│  │                                                                                     │ │
│  │   📍 User Location Detection:                                                      │ │
│  │   ┌─────────────┐    ┌─────────────┐    ┌─────────────┐    ┌─────────────┐       │ │
│  │   │    User     │───▶│ CloudFront  │───▶│ Route 53    │───▶│  Nearest    │       │ │
│  │   │  (Browser)  │    │   (CDN)     │    │ (DNS)       │    │   Region    │       │ │
│  │   └─────────────┘    └─────────────┘    └─────────────┘    └─────────────┘       │ │
│  │                                                                  │                │ │
│  │   🎯 Intelligent Routing Rules:                                  │                │ │
│  │   ├─ Geographic proximity (lowest latency)                       ▼                │ │
│  │   ├─ Data residency requirements (GDPR, local laws)    ┌─────────────────┐       │ │
│  │   ├─ Service availability (failover to healthy region) │ Regional Django │       │ │
│  │   └─ Load balancing (distribute traffic evenly)        │   Application   │       │ │
│  │                                                         │                 │       │ │
│  │   📊 Performance Metrics:                               │ ┌─────────────┐ │       │ │
│  │   • North America: avg 15ms response time              │ │ DynamoDB    │ │       │ │
│  │   • Europe: avg 12ms response time                     │ │ Local Read  │ │       │ │
│  │   • Asia-Pacific: avg 18ms response time               │ │ 2ms latency │ │       │ │
│  │   • Global average: 99.9% availability                 │ └─────────────┘ │       │ │
│  │                                                         └─────────────────┘       │ │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    🔧 Global Tables Implementation
    # global_deployment/global_tables_manager.py
import boto3
import json
from django.conf import settings
from django.utils import timezone
import logging

class GlobalTablesManager:
    def __init__(self):
        """Initialize Global Tables management for anihpj"""
        self.regions = [
            'us-east-1',      # Primary - North America
            'eu-west-1',      # Europe
            'ap-southeast-1', # Asia-Pacific (Singapore)
            'ap-northeast-1', # Asia-Pacific (Tokyo)
            'ap-southeast-2'  # Australia
        ]
        
        # Create DynamoDB clients for each region
        self.dynamodb_clients = {}
        self.dynamodb_resources = {}
        
        for region in self.regions:
            self.dynamodb_clients[region] = boto3.client('dynamodb', region_name=region)
            self.dynamodb_resources[region] = boto3.resource('dynamodb', region_name=region)
        
        # Tables to replicate globally
        self.global_tables = [
            'anihpj-user-sessions',
            'anihpj-user-activity',
            'anihpj-notifications'
        ]
        
        # Regional tables (not replicated)
        self.regional_tables = [
            'anihpj-job-analytics',  # Region-specific job market data
            'anihpj-compliance-logs' # Local data residency requirements
        ]
    
    def create_global_table(self, table_name):
        """Create a Global Table across all regions"""
        
        try:
            # Step 1: Create table in primary region if it doesn't exist
            primary_region = 'us-east-1'
            self._ensure_table_exists(table_name, primary_region)
            
            # Step 2: Create replica tables in other regions
            replica_regions = [r for r in self.regions if r != primary_region]
            
            for region in replica_regions:
                self._create_replica_table(table_name, primary_region, region)
            
            # Step 3: Enable Global Tables
            self._enable_global_table(table_name)
            
            logger.info(f"Global table {table_name} created successfully")
            
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Error creating global table {table_name}: {str(e)}")
            raise
    
    def _ensure_table_exists(self, table_name, region):
        """Ensure table exists in the specified region"""
        
        client = self.dynamodb_clients[region]
        
        try:
            # Check if table exists
            response = client.describe_table(TableName=table_name)
            logger.info(f"Table {table_name} already exists in {region}")
            
        except client.exceptions.ResourceNotFoundException:
            # Create table with Global Tables requirements
            self._create_table_for_global_replication(table_name, region)
    
    def _create_table_for_global_replication(self, table_name, region):
        """Create table optimized for Global Tables"""
        
        client = self.dynamodb_clients[region]
        
        # Define table schema based on table type
        table_schemas = {
            'anihpj-user-sessions': {
                'KeySchema': [
                    {'AttributeName': 'session_key', 'KeyType': 'HASH'}
                ],
                'AttributeDefinitions': [
                    {'AttributeName': 'session_key', 'AttributeType': 'S'}
                ]
            },
            'anihpj-user-activity': {
                'KeySchema': [
                    {'AttributeName': 'user_id', 'KeyType': 'HASH'},
                    {'AttributeName': 'timestamp', 'KeyType': 'RANGE'}
                ],
                'AttributeDefinitions': [
                    {'AttributeName': 'user_id', 'AttributeType': 'N'},
                    {'AttributeName': 'timestamp', 'AttributeType': 'S'}
                ]
            },
            'anihpj-notifications': {
                'KeySchema': [
                    {'AttributeName': 'user_id', 'KeyType': 'HASH'},
                    {'AttributeName': 'notification_id', 'KeyType': 'RANGE'}
                ],
                'AttributeDefinitions': [
                    {'AttributeName': 'user_id', 'AttributeType': 'N'},
                    {'AttributeName': 'notification_id', 'AttributeType': 'S'}
                ]
            }
        }
        
        schema = table_schemas.get(table_name)
        if not schema:
            raise ValueError(f"Unknown table schema for {table_name}")
        
        # Create table with Global Tables requirements
        response = client.create_table(
            TableName=table_name,
            KeySchema=schema['KeySchema'],
            AttributeDefinitions=schema['AttributeDefinitions'],
            BillingMode='PAY_PER_REQUEST',  # Recommended for Global Tables
            
            # Enable streams for Global Tables replication
            StreamSpecification={
                'StreamEnabled': True,
                'StreamViewType': 'NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES'
            },
            
            # Enable point-in-time recovery
            PointInTimeRecoverySpecification={
                'PointInTimeRecoveryEnabled': True
            },
            
            # Tags for management
            Tags=[
                {'Key': 'Application', 'Value': 'anihpj'},
                {'Key': 'Environment', 'Value': settings.ENVIRONMENT},
                {'Key': 'GlobalTable', 'Value': 'true'},
                {'Key': 'Region', 'Value': region}
            ]
        )
        
        # Wait for table to be active
        waiter = client.get_waiter('table_exists')
        waiter.wait(TableName=table_name)
        
        logger.info(f"Created table {table_name} in {region}")
    
    def _enable_global_table(self, table_name):
        """Enable Global Tables replication"""
        
        primary_client = self.dynamodb_clients['us-east-1']
        
        try:
            # Create Global Table
            replica_list = []
            for region in self.regions:
                replica_list.append({'RegionName': region})
            
            response = primary_client.create_global_table(
                GlobalTableName=table_name,
                ReplicationGroup=replica_list
            )
            
            logger.info(f"Global Table {table_name} enabled with replicas: {self.regions}")
            
        except Exception as e:
            if "GlobalTable already exists" in str(e):
                logger.info(f"Global Table {table_name} already exists")
            else:
                raise
    
    def get_regional_endpoint(self, user_location=None):
        """Determine optimal DynamoDB endpoint based on user location"""
        
        # Mapping of user locations to optimal regions
        location_mapping = {
            'US': 'us-east-1',
            'CA': 'us-east-1',        # Canada -> US East
            'GB': 'eu-west-1',
            'DE': 'eu-west-1',
            'FR': 'eu-west-1',
            'SG': 'ap-southeast-1',
            'MY': 'ap-southeast-1',    # Malaysia -> Singapore
            'TH': 'ap-southeast-1',    # Thailand -> Singapore
            'JP': 'ap-northeast-1',
            'KR': 'ap-northeast-1',    # Korea -> Tokyo
            'AU': 'ap-southeast-2',
            'NZ': 'ap-southeast-2'     # New Zealand -> Australia
        }
        
        if user_location and user_location in location_mapping:
            optimal_region = location_mapping[user_location]
        else:
            # Default to primary region
            optimal_region = 'us-east-1'
        
        return optimal_region
    
    def create_regional_dynamodb_client(self, region=None, user_location=None):
        """Create DynamoDB client for optimal region"""
        
        if not region:
            region = self.get_regional_endpoint(user_location)
        
        return boto3.resource(
            'dynamodb',
            region_name=region,
            aws_access_key_id=settings.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID,
            aws_secret_access_key=settings.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY
        )
    
    def monitor_global_table_health(self):
        """Monitor health of Global Tables across all regions"""
        
        health_report = {
            'timestamp': timezone.now().isoformat(),
            'overall_status': 'HEALTHY',
            'regions': {}
        }
        
        for region in self.regions:
            region_health = self._check_region_health(region)
            health_report['regions'][region] = region_health
            
            if region_health['status'] != 'HEALTHY':
                health_report['overall_status'] = 'DEGRADED'
        
        return health_report
    
    def _check_region_health(self, region):
        """Check health of DynamoDB tables in a specific region"""
        
        client = self.dynamodb_clients[region]
        
        region_health = {
            'status': 'HEALTHY',
            'tables': {},
            'replication_lag': {},
            'issues': []
        }
        
        for table_name in self.global_tables:
            try:
                # Check table status
                response = client.describe_table(TableName=table_name)
                table_status = response['Table']['TableStatus']
                
                region_health['tables'][table_name] = {
                    'status': table_status,
                    'item_count': response['Table'].get('ItemCount', 0),
                    'size_bytes': response['Table'].get('TableSizeBytes', 0)
                }
                
                if table_status != 'ACTIVE':
                    region_health['status'] = 'DEGRADED'
                    region_health['issues'].append(f"{table_name} is {table_status}")
                
                # Check Global Table replication status
                if 'GlobalTableVersion' in response['Table']:
                    gt_response = client.describe_global_table(
                        GlobalTableName=table_name
                    )
                    
                    for replica in gt_response['GlobalTableDescription']['ReplicationGroup']:
                        if replica['RegionName'] == region:
                            replication_status = replica.get('ReplicaStatus', 'UNKNOWN')
                            if replication_status != 'ACTIVE':
                                region_health['status'] = 'DEGRADED'
                                region_health['issues'].append(
                                    f"{table_name} replication is {replication_status}"
                                )
                
            except Exception as e:
                region_health['status'] = 'ERROR'
                region_health['issues'].append(f"{table_name}: {str(e)}")
        
        return region_health
    
    def setup_disaster_recovery(self):
        """Setup disaster recovery capabilities"""
        
        # Configure cross-region backup
        for region in self.regions:
            for table_name in self.global_tables:
                self._enable_continuous_backup(table_name, region)
        
        # Setup monitoring and alerting
        self._setup_global_monitoring()
        
        logger.info("Disaster recovery setup completed")
    
    def _enable_continuous_backup(self, table_name, region):
        """Enable continuous backup for a table"""
        
        client = self.dynamodb_clients[region]
        
        try:
            client.update_continuous_backups(
                TableName=table_name,
                PointInTimeRecoverySpecification={
                    'PointInTimeRecoveryEnabled': True
                }
            )
            
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning(f"Could not enable backup for {table_name} in {region}: {str(e)}")

# Initialize global tables manager
global_tables_manager = GlobalTablesManager()
    🌐 Regional Routing in Django
    # middleware/regional_routing_middleware.py
from django.utils.deprecation import MiddlewareMixin
from global_deployment.global_tables_manager import global_tables_manager
import geoip2.database
from django.conf import settings

class RegionalRoutingMiddleware(MiddlewareMixin):
    """Route DynamoDB requests to optimal region based on user location"""
    
    def __init__(self, get_response):
        self.get_response = get_response
        
        # Initialize GeoIP database for location detection
        try:
            self.geoip_reader = geoip2.database.Reader(settings.GEOIP_DATABASE_PATH)
        except:
            self.geoip_reader = None
    
    def process_request(self, request):
        """Determine optimal region for user and set in request context"""
        
        # Get user's country from IP
        user_country = self._get_user_country(request)
        
        # Get optimal region
        optimal_region = global_tables_manager.get_regional_endpoint(user_country)
        
        # Create regional DynamoDB client
        request.dynamodb = global_tables_manager.create_regional_dynamodb_client(
            region=optimal_region,
            user_location=user_country
        )
        
        # Store region info for analytics
        request.aws_region = optimal_region
        request.user_country = user_country
        
        return None
    
    def _get_user_country(self, request):
        """Extract user's country from IP address"""
        
        if not self.geoip_reader:
            return None
        
        # Get client IP
        ip_address = self._get_client_ip(request)
        
        try:
            response = self.geoip_reader.country(ip_address)
            return response.country.iso_code
        except:
            return None
    
    def _get_client_ip(self, request):
        """Get client IP address"""
        x_forwarded_for = request.META.get('HTTP_X_FORWARDED_FOR')
        if x_forwarded_for:
            ip = x_forwarded_for.split(',')[0].strip()
        else:
            ip = request.META.get('REMOTE_ADDR')
        return ip
    🌍 Global Tables Benefits for anihpj:
    • Global Performance: Single-digit millisecond latency worldwide
    • High Availability: 99.999% availability with multi-region redundancy
    • Data Compliance: Meet local data residency requirements (GDPR, etc.)
    • Disaster Recovery: Automatic failover between regions
    • Eventual Consistency: Data synchronized across regions in seconds
    • Cost Optimization: Pay only for what you use in each region

    ⚡ Advanced Topics - Transactions & DAX

    ACID transactions and microsecond caching for anihpj to ensure data consistency and ultra-fast performance.

    🎯 Transaction Use Cases
    Transaction Type anihpj Scenario ACID Benefit Implementation
    💰 Payment Processing Job posting payment No partial charges TransactWriteItems
    🎫 Credit System Deduct application credits Prevent over-spending Conditional writes
    🔄 State Changes Job application status Consistent state Multi-table updates
    📊 Analytics Updates User activity + counters Accurate metrics Atomic increments
    🔐 Security Operations Login + audit log Complete audit trail TransactWriteItems
    💳 Transaction Implementation
    # transactions/dynamodb_transactions.py
import boto3
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError
from django.utils import timezone
from decimal import Decimal
import json
import uuid

class DynamoDBTransactionManager:
    def __init__(self):
        """Initialize transaction manager for anihpj"""
        self.dynamodb = boto3.resource('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
        self.client = boto3.client('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
        
        # Table references
        self.user_credits_table = self.dynamodb.Table('anihpj-user-credits')
        self.transactions_table = self.dynamodb.Table('anihpj-transactions')
        self.job_applications_table = self.dynamodb.Table('anihpj-job-applications')
        self.analytics_table = self.dynamodb.Table('anihpj-job-analytics')
    
    def process_job_application_with_credits(self, user_id, job_id, application_data, credit_cost=1):
        """Process job application with credit deduction using ACID transaction"""
        
        transaction_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        timestamp = timezone.now().isoformat()
        
        try:
            # Prepare transaction items
            transact_items = [
                {
                    # 1. Check and deduct user credits
                    'Update': {
                        'TableName': 'anihpj-user-credits',
                        'Key': {'user_id': {'N': str(user_id)}},
                        'UpdateExpression': 'ADD available_credits :decrement SET last_updated = :timestamp',
                        'ConditionExpression': 'available_credits >= :cost',
                        'ExpressionAttributeValues': {
                            ':decrement': {'N': str(-credit_cost)},
                            ':cost': {'N': str(credit_cost)},
                            ':timestamp': {'S': timestamp}
                        }
                    }
                },
                {
                    # 2. Create job application record
                    'Put': {
                        'TableName': 'anihpj-job-applications',
                        'Item': {
                            'application_id': {'S': f"app_{transaction_id}"},
                            'user_id': {'N': str(user_id)},
                            'job_id': {'N': str(job_id)},
                            'status': {'S': 'pending'},
                            'application_data': {'S': json.dumps(application_data)},
                            'applied_at': {'S': timestamp},
                            'credits_used': {'N': str(credit_cost)},
                            'transaction_id': {'S': transaction_id}
                        },
                        'ConditionExpression': 'attribute_not_exists(application_id)'
                    }
                },
                {
                    # 3. Update job analytics
                    'Update': {
                        'TableName': 'anihpj-job-analytics',
                        'Key': {
                            'job_id': {'N': str(job_id)},
                            'date': {'S': timezone.now().date().isoformat()}
                        },
                        'UpdateExpression': 'ADD application_count :inc SET last_updated = :timestamp',
                        'ExpressionAttributeValues': {
                            ':inc': {'N': '1'},
                            ':timestamp': {'S': timestamp}
                        }
                    }
                },
                {
                    # 4. Log transaction
                    'Put': {
                        'TableName': 'anihpj-transactions',
                        'Item': {
                            'transaction_id': {'S': transaction_id},
                            'user_id': {'N': str(user_id)},
                            'transaction_type': {'S': 'job_application'},
                            'amount': {'N': str(credit_cost)},
                            'status': {'S': 'completed'},
                            'timestamp': {'S': timestamp},
                            'metadata': {'S': json.dumps({
                                'job_id': job_id,
                                'application_id': f"app_{transaction_id}"
                            })}
                        }
                    }
                }
            ]
            
            # Execute transaction
            response = self.client.transact_write_items(TransactItems=transact_items)
            
            print(f"Job application transaction completed: {transaction_id}")
            return {
                'success': True,
                'transaction_id': transaction_id,
                'application_id': f"app_{transaction_id}"
            }
            
        except ClientError as e:
            error_code = e.response['Error']['Code']
            
            if error_code == 'TransactionCanceledException':
                # Handle specific cancellation reasons
                cancellation_reasons = e.response['Error'].get('CancellationReasons', [])
                
                for reason in cancellation_reasons:
                    if reason.get('Code') == 'ConditionalCheckFailed':
                        if 'anihpj-user-credits' in reason.get('Item', ''):
                            return {
                                'success': False,
                                'error': 'insufficient_credits',
                                'message': 'Not enough credits to apply for this job'
                            }
                
                return {
                    'success': False,
                    'error': 'transaction_failed',
                    'message': 'Job application could not be processed'
                }
            
            else:
                print(f"Transaction error: {str(e)}")
                return {
                    'success': False,
                    'error': 'system_error',
                    'message': 'System error occurred during application'
                }
    
    def purchase_credits_transaction(self, user_id, credit_amount, payment_info):
        """Purchase credits with payment processing transaction"""
        
        transaction_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        timestamp = timezone.now().isoformat()
        
        try:
            # Simulate payment processing (integrate with Stripe/PayPal)
            payment_result = self._process_payment(payment_info, credit_amount)
            
            if not payment_result['success']:
                return {
                    'success': False,
                    'error': 'payment_failed',
                    'message': payment_result['message']
                }
            
            # Execute credit purchase transaction
            transact_items = [
                {
                    # 1. Add credits to user account
                    'Update': {
                        'TableName': 'anihpj-user-credits',
                        'Key': {'user_id': {'N': str(user_id)}},
                        'UpdateExpression': 'ADD available_credits :credits SET last_purchase = :timestamp',
                        'ExpressionAttributeValues': {
                            ':credits': {'N': str(credit_amount)},
                            ':timestamp': {'S': timestamp}
                        }
                    }
                },
                {
                    # 2. Log purchase transaction
                    'Put': {
                        'TableName': 'anihpj-transactions',
                        'Item': {
                            'transaction_id': {'S': transaction_id},
                            'user_id': {'N': str(user_id)},
                            'transaction_type': {'S': 'credit_purchase'},
                            'amount': {'N': str(credit_amount)},
                            'payment_amount': {'N': str(payment_result['amount'])},
                            'payment_method': {'S': payment_result['method']},
                            'payment_id': {'S': payment_result['payment_id']},
                            'status': {'S': 'completed'},
                            'timestamp': {'S': timestamp}
                        }
                    }
                }
            ]
            
            response = self.client.transact_write_items(TransactItems=transact_items)
            
            return {
                'success': True,
                'transaction_id': transaction_id,
                'credits_added': credit_amount,
                'payment_id': payment_result['payment_id']
            }
            
        except Exception as e:
            # Refund payment if transaction fails
            if 'payment_result' in locals() and payment_result.get('success'):
                self._refund_payment(payment_result['payment_id'])
            
            return {
                'success': False,
                'error': 'transaction_failed',
                'message': str(e)
            }
    
    def _process_payment(self, payment_info, amount):
        """Simulate payment processing"""
        # In production, integrate with actual payment processor
        return {
            'success': True,
            'payment_id': f"pay_{uuid.uuid4()}",
            'amount': amount * 0.10,  # $0.10 per credit
            'method': 'stripe'
        }
    
    def _refund_payment(self, payment_id):
        """Simulate payment refund"""
        print(f"Refunding payment: {payment_id}")
        return True

# Initialize transaction manager
transaction_manager = DynamoDBTransactionManager()
    ⚡ DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) Implementation
    # caching/dax_manager.py
import boto3
from django.conf import settings
from django.core.cache import cache
import json
import logging

class DAXManager:
    def __init__(self):
        """Initialize DAX cluster for microsecond caching"""
        
        # DAX cluster configuration
        self.dax_endpoint = settings.DAX_CLUSTER_ENDPOINT  # e.g., 'anihpj-dax.abc123.dax-clusters.us-east-1.amazonaws.com:8111'
        
        try:
            # Create DAX client (requires amazon-dax-client package)
            import amazondax
            self.dax_client = amazondax.AmazonDaxClient.resource(
                endpoint_url=self.dax_endpoint,
                region_name=settings.AWS_DEFAULT_REGION
            )
            self.use_dax = True
            
        except ImportError:
            # Fallback to regular DynamoDB if DAX client not available
            self.dax_client = boto3.resource('dynamodb', region_name=settings.AWS_DEFAULT_REGION)
            self.use_dax = False
            logging.warning("DAX client not available, using regular DynamoDB")
        
        # Cache configuration
        self.cache_ttl = 300  # 5 minutes
        self.hot_data_tables = [
            'anihpj-user-sessions',
            'anihpj-job-analytics',
            'anihpj-trending-jobs'
        ]
    
    def get_user_session_cached(self, session_key):
        """Get user session with DAX caching"""
        
        try:
            table = self.dax_client.Table('anihpj-user-sessions')
            
            response = table.get_item(
                Key={'session_key': session_key},
                ConsistentRead=False  # Use eventually consistent reads for better performance
            )
            
            if 'Item' in response:
                return response['Item']
            else:
                return None
                
        except Exception as e:
            logging.error(f"Error getting cached session: {str(e)}")
            return None
    
    def get_trending_jobs_cached(self, limit=10):
        """Get trending jobs with multi-layer caching"""
        
        cache_key = f"trending_jobs_{limit}"
        
        # Layer 1: Application cache (Redis/Memcached)
        cached_jobs = cache.get(cache_key)
        if cached_jobs:
            return cached_jobs
        
        # Layer 2: DAX cache (microsecond latency)
        try:
            table = self.dax_client.Table('anihpj-trending-jobs')
            
            response = table.scan(
                FilterExpression='trending_score > :min_score',
                ExpressionAttributeValues={':min_score': 0.7},
                Limit=limit
            )
            
            trending_jobs = response.get('Items', [])
            
            # Sort by trending score
            trending_jobs.sort(key=lambda x: x.get('trending_score', 0), reverse=True)
            
            # Cache in application layer
            cache.set(cache_key, trending_jobs, self.cache_ttl)
            
            return trending_jobs
            
        except Exception as e:
            logging.error(f"Error getting trending jobs: {str(e)}")
            return []
    
    def setup_dax_cluster(self):
        """Setup DAX cluster configuration"""
        
        dax_client = boto3.client('dax', region_name=settings.AWS_DEFAULT_REGION)
        
        cluster_config = {
            'ClusterName': 'anihpj-dax-cluster',
            'NodeType': 'dax.r5.large',  # Choose based on workload
            'ReplicationFactor': 3,      # Multi-AZ for high availability
            'Description': 'DAX cluster for anihpj job portal caching',
            
            # Security configuration
            'SecurityGroupIds': [settings.DAX_SECURITY_GROUP_ID],
            'SubnetGroupName': 'anihpj-dax-subnet-group',
            'IAMRoleArn': settings.DAX_IAM_ROLE_ARN,
            
            # Parameter group for optimization
            'ParameterGroupName': 'anihpj-dax-parameters',
            
            # Notification configuration
            'NotificationTopicArn': settings.SNS_DAX_NOTIFICATIONS_TOPIC,
            
            # Maintenance window
            'PreferredMaintenanceWindow': 'sun:03:00-sun:04:00',
            
            # Enable encryption
            'SSESpecification': {
                'Enabled': True
            },
            
            # Tags
            'Tags': [
                {'Key': 'Application', 'Value': 'anihpj'},
                {'Key': 'Environment', 'Value': settings.ENVIRONMENT},
                {'Key': 'CostCenter', 'Value': 'infrastructure'}
            ]
        }
        
        try:
            response = dax_client.create_cluster(**cluster_config)
            cluster_arn = response['Cluster']['ClusterArn']
            
            print(f"DAX cluster created: {cluster_arn}")
            return cluster_arn
            
        except Exception as e:
            if "already exists" in str(e):
                print("DAX cluster already exists")
            else:
                raise
    
    def monitor_dax_performance(self):
        """Monitor DAX cluster performance"""
        
        cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch', region_name=settings.AWS_DEFAULT_REGION)
        
        # Get DAX metrics
        metrics = [
            'CPUUtilization',
            'NetworkBytesIn',
            'NetworkBytesOut',
            'GetItemRequestCount',
            'QueryRequestCount',
            'ItemCacheHits',
            'ItemCacheMisses'
        ]
        
        performance_data = {}
        
        for metric_name in metrics:
            try:
                response = cloudwatch.get_metric_statistics(
                    Namespace='AWS/DAX',
                    MetricName=metric_name,
                    Dimensions=[
                        {
                            'Name': 'ClusterName',
                            'Value': 'anihpj-dax-cluster'
                        }
                    ],
                    StartTime=timezone.now() - timezone.timedelta(hours=1),
                    EndTime=timezone.now(),
                    Period=300,
                    Statistics=['Average', 'Maximum']
                )
                
                performance_data[metric_name] = response['Datapoints']
                
            except Exception as e:
                logging.error(f"Error getting DAX metric {metric_name}: {str(e)}")
        
        return performance_data

# Initialize DAX manager
dax_manager = DAXManager()
    📊 Performance Comparison: Regular vs DAX vs Transactions
    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                         PERFORMANCE COMPARISON DASHBOARD                                │
│                              anihpj Production Metrics                                 │
│                                                                                         │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                             LATENCY COMPARISON                                      │ │
│  │                                                                                     │ │
│  │   📊 Read Operations:                                                              │ │
│  │   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │
│  │   │ Standard DynamoDB    │████████████████████████▓▓▓░░ 2.3ms avg │ 4.1ms p95   │ │ │
│  │   │ DAX Cache Hit        │▓░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░░ 0.2ms avg │ 0.4ms p95   │ │ │
│  │   │ DAX Cache Miss       │████████████████████████████▓ 2.8ms avg │ 4.3ms p95   │ │ │
│  │   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │
│  │                                                                                     │ │
│  │   💳 Transaction Operations:                                                       │ │
│  │   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ │
│  │   │ Single Item Write    │██████████████████████████▓▓░ 3.1ms avg │ 5.2ms p95   │ │ │
│  │   │ Transaction (4 items)│████████████████████████████████████████ 12.4ms avg   │ │ │
│  │   │ Failed Transaction   │█████████████████████████████████▓▓▓░░░░ 8.9ms avg    │ │ │
│  │   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │ │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                         │

│  │   ├─ Job Analytics: 89.2% hit rate (good for trending data)                       │ │

│  │                                                                                     │ │

│  │   ├─ 45% improvement in page load times for dashboard                              │ │

│  │   └─ Total: $1,233.80/month                                                       │ │
│  │                                                                                     │ │

│  │   ├─ User experience: 45% faster page loads                                       │ │
│  │   ├─ Cost per user/month: $2.34 (based on 527 active users)                      │ │
│  │   └─ ROI: Positive (increased user engagement +23%)                               │ │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    ⚡ Transactions & DAX Benefits for anihpj:
    • ACID Compliance: Ensure data consistency for payments and credit systems
    • Micro-second Latency: DAX provides sub-millisecond read performance
    • High Cache Hit Rates: 90%+ hit rates for frequently accessed data
    • Cost Optimization: Reduce DynamoDB read costs by 67% with caching
    • Improved UX: 45% faster page loads and instant data access
    • Business Logic Safety: Prevent double-spending and data corruption

    🎯 Best Practices

    Production-ready best practices for implementing DynamoDB in your anihpj Django application.

    🏗️ Design Patterns
    Category Best Practice anihpj Implementation Impact
    🔑 Key Design Distribute load evenly Use user_id, avoid timestamps as PK No hot partitions
    📊 Data Modeling Single table design Combine related entities Reduce costs, improve performance
    🔍 Query Patterns Design GSIs for access patterns Email lookup, job category queries Efficient data access
    ⚡ Performance Use batch operations Batch user activity logging Reduced latency and costs
    💰 Cost Control Right-size capacity modes On-demand for spikes, provisioned for steady Optimize spending
    📋 Production Checklist
    # best_practices/production_checklist.py
from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand
import boto3
import json

class DynamoDBProductionChecker:
    def __init__(self):
        """Production readiness checker for anihpj DynamoDB implementation"""
        self.dynamodb = boto3.client('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
        self.cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch', region_name='us-east-1')
        
        self.tables = [
            'anihpj-user-sessions',
            'anihpj-user-activity',
            'anihpj-job-analytics',
            'anihpj-notifications'
        ]
        
        self.checks = []
    
    def run_production_checks(self):
        """Run comprehensive production readiness checks"""
        
        print("🔍 Running DynamoDB Production Readiness Checks...")
        print("=" * 60)
        
        # 1. Table Configuration Checks
        self._check_table_configurations()
        
        # 2. Security Checks
        self._check_security_configurations()
        
        # 3. Performance Checks
        self._check_performance_configurations()
        
        # 4. Monitoring Checks
        self._check_monitoring_setup()
        
        # 5. Backup and Recovery Checks
        self._check_backup_configurations()
        
        # 6. Cost Optimization Checks
        self._check_cost_optimizations()
        
        # Generate report
        self._generate_report()
    
    def _check_table_configurations(self):
        """Check table configuration best practices"""
        
        print("\n📊 Table Configuration Checks:")
        
        for table_name in self.tables:
            try:
                response = self.dynamodb.describe_table(TableName=table_name)
                table = response['Table']
                
                # Check 1: Encryption at rest
                sse_description = table.get('SSEDescription', {})
                if sse_description.get('Status') == 'ENABLED':
                    self._add_check('✅', f"{table_name}: Encryption at rest enabled")
                else:
                    self._add_check('❌', f"{table_name}: Encryption at rest NOT enabled")
                
                # Check 2: Point-in-time recovery
                pitr_response = self.dynamodb.describe_continuous_backups(TableName=table_name)
                pitr_enabled = pitr_response['ContinuousBackupsDescription']['PointInTimeRecoveryDescription']['PointInTimeRecoveryStatus'] == 'ENABLED'
                
                if pitr_enabled:
                    self._add_check('✅', f"{table_name}: Point-in-time recovery enabled")
                else:
                    self._add_check('❌', f"{table_name}: Point-in-time recovery NOT enabled")
                
                # Check 3: DynamoDB Streams
                stream_arn = table.get('LatestStreamArn')
                if stream_arn:
                    self._add_check('✅', f"{table_name}: DynamoDB Streams enabled")
                else:
                    self._add_check('⚠️', f"{table_name}: DynamoDB Streams not enabled (may be intentional)")
                
                # Check 4: Global Tables
                global_table_version = table.get('GlobalTableVersion')
                if global_table_version:
                    self._add_check('✅', f"{table_name}: Global Tables enabled (v{global_table_version})")
                else:
                    self._add_check('⚠️', f"{table_name}: Global Tables not enabled")
                
                # Check 5: Billing mode optimization
                billing_mode = table['BillingModeSummary']['BillingMode']
                if billing_mode == 'PAY_PER_REQUEST':
                    self._add_check('✅', f"{table_name}: Using on-demand billing (good for variable workloads)")
                else:
                    # Check if auto-scaling is enabled
                    provisioned = table['ProvisionedThroughput']
                    if 'LastIncreaseDateTime' in provisioned or 'LastDecreaseDateTime' in provisioned:
                        self._add_check('✅', f"{table_name}: Provisioned with auto-scaling")
                    else:
                        self._add_check('⚠️', f"{table_name}: Provisioned without auto-scaling")
                
            except Exception as e:
                self._add_check('❌', f"{table_name}: Error checking configuration - {str(e)}")
    
    def _check_security_configurations(self):
        """Check security best practices"""
        
        print("\n🔒 Security Configuration Checks:")
        
        # Check 1: IAM roles and policies
        iam = boto3.client('iam')
        
        try:
            # Check if application role exists with proper permissions
            role_response = iam.get_role(RoleName='anihpj-application-role')
            self._add_check('✅', "Application IAM role exists")
            
            # Check attached policies
            policies_response = iam.list_attached_role_policies(RoleName='anihpj-application-role')
            
            dynamodb_policy_found = False
            for policy in policies_response['AttachedPolicies']:
                if 'DynamoDB' in policy['PolicyName'] or 'dynamodb' in policy['PolicyName'].lower():
                    dynamodb_policy_found = True
                    break
            
            if dynamodb_policy_found:
                self._add_check('✅', "DynamoDB-specific IAM policy attached")
            else:
                self._add_check('❌', "No DynamoDB-specific IAM policy found")
                
        except Exception as e:
            self._add_check('❌', f"IAM role check failed: {str(e)}")
        
        # Check 2: VPC Endpoints
        ec2 = boto3.client('ec2')
        try:
            vpc_endpoints = ec2.describe_vpc_endpoints(
                Filters=[
                    {'Name': 'service-name', 'Values': ['com.amazonaws.us-east-1.dynamodb']}
                ]
            )
            
            if vpc_endpoints['VpcEndpoints']:
                self._add_check('✅', "DynamoDB VPC endpoint configured")
            else:
                self._add_check('⚠️', "No DynamoDB VPC endpoint (traffic goes over internet)")
                
        except Exception as e:
            self._add_check('⚠️', f"VPC endpoint check inconclusive: {str(e)}")
    
    def _check_performance_configurations(self):
        """Check performance optimization settings"""
        
        print("\n⚡ Performance Configuration Checks:")
        
        # Check 1: DAX cluster
        try:
            dax = boto3.client('dax')
            clusters = dax.describe_clusters()
            
            if clusters['Clusters']:
                cluster = clusters['Clusters'][0]
                if cluster['Status'] == 'available':
                    self._add_check('✅', f"DAX cluster active: {cluster['ClusterName']}")
                else:
                    self._add_check('⚠️', f"DAX cluster exists but status: {cluster['Status']}")
            else:
                self._add_check('⚠️', "No DAX cluster found (may impact read performance)")
                
        except Exception as e:
            self._add_check('⚠️', f"DAX check inconclusive: {str(e)}")
        
        # Check 2: GSI optimization
        for table_name in self.tables:
            try:
                response = self.dynamodb.describe_table(TableName=table_name)
                table = response['Table']
                
                gsi_count = len(table.get('GlobalSecondaryIndexes', []))
                
                if gsi_count > 0:
                    self._add_check('✅', f"{table_name}: {gsi_count} GSI(s) configured for query optimization")
                else:
                    if table_name in ['anihpj-user-activity', 'anihpj-job-analytics']:
                        self._add_check('⚠️', f"{table_name}: Consider adding GSIs for common query patterns")
                    else:
                        self._add_check('ℹ️', f"{table_name}: No GSIs (may be appropriate for this table)")
                        
            except Exception as e:
                self._add_check('❌', f"{table_name}: Error checking GSI configuration")
    
    def _check_monitoring_setup(self):
        """Check monitoring and alerting configuration"""
        
        print("\n📊 Monitoring Configuration Checks:")
        
        # Check CloudWatch alarms
        alarms = self.cloudwatch.describe_alarms(
            AlarmNamePrefix='anihpj-dynamodb'
        )
        
        if alarms['MetricAlarms']:
            self._add_check('✅', f"CloudWatch alarms configured: {len(alarms['MetricAlarms'])} alarms")
            
            # Check for specific alarm types
            alarm_types = {
                'latency': False,
                'capacity': False,
                'throttling': False,
                'errors': False
            }
            
            for alarm in alarms['MetricAlarms']:
                alarm_name = alarm['AlarmName'].lower()
                if 'latency' in alarm_name:
                    alarm_types['latency'] = True
                elif 'capacity' in alarm_name or 'utilization' in alarm_name:
                    alarm_types['capacity'] = True
                elif 'throttl' in alarm_name:
                    alarm_types['throttling'] = True
                elif 'error' in alarm_name:
                    alarm_types['errors'] = True
            
            for alarm_type, configured in alarm_types.items():
                if configured:
                    self._add_check('✅', f"{alarm_type.title()} alarms configured")
                else:
                    self._add_check('⚠️', f"No {alarm_type} alarms found")
        else:
            self._add_check('❌', "No CloudWatch alarms configured for DynamoDB")
        
        # Check dashboard
        dashboards = self.cloudwatch.list_dashboards(
            DashboardNamePrefix='anihpj-dynamodb'
        )
        
        if dashboards['DashboardEntries']:
            self._add_check('✅', "Custom CloudWatch dashboard configured")
        else:
            self._add_check('⚠️', "No custom CloudWatch dashboard found")
    
    def _check_backup_configurations(self):
        """Check backup and disaster recovery setup"""
        
        print("\n💾 Backup Configuration Checks:")
        
        for table_name in self.tables:
            try:
                # Check on-demand backups
                backups = self.dynamodb.list_backups(
                    TableName=table_name,
                    BackupType='ALL'
                )
                
                recent_backups = [
                    b for b in backups['BackupSummaries']
                    if (timezone.now() - b['BackupCreationDateTime']).days <= 7
                ]
                
                if recent_backups:
                    self._add_check('✅', f"{table_name}: Recent backups available ({len(recent_backups)} in last 7 days)")
                else:
                    self._add_check('⚠️', f"{table_name}: No recent backups found")
                    
            except Exception as e:
                self._add_check('❌', f"{table_name}: Error checking backups - {str(e)}")
    
    def _check_cost_optimizations(self):
        """Check cost optimization opportunities"""
        
        print("\n💰 Cost Optimization Checks:")
        
        # Check for reserved capacity
        try:
            reserved_capacity = self.dynamodb.describe_reserved_capacity()
            
            if reserved_capacity['ReservedCapacityDescriptions']:
                total_savings = sum(
                    rc.get('FixedPrice', 0) for rc in reserved_capacity['ReservedCapacityDescriptions']
                )
                self._add_check('✅', f"Reserved capacity configured (potential savings: ${total_savings:.2f})")
            else:
                self._add_check('ℹ️', "No reserved capacity (consider for predictable workloads)")
                
        except Exception as e:
            self._add_check('⚠️', f"Reserved capacity check inconclusive: {str(e)}")
        
        # Check table sizes for archiving opportunities
        for table_name in self.tables:
            try:
                response = self.dynamodb.describe_table(TableName=table_name)
                table = response['Table']
                
                size_gb = table.get('TableSizeBytes', 0) / (1024**3)
                item_count = table.get('ItemCount', 0)
                
                if size_gb > 10:  # Tables larger than 10GB
                    self._add_check('ℹ️', f"{table_name}: Large table ({size_gb:.1f}GB, {item_count:,} items) - consider archiving old data")
                else:
                    self._add_check('✅', f"{table_name}: Reasonable size ({size_gb:.1f}GB)")
                    
            except Exception as e:
                self._add_check('⚠️', f"{table_name}: Size check inconclusive")
    
    def _add_check(self, status, message):
        """Add check result to list"""
        self.checks.append({'status': status, 'message': message})
        print(f"  {status} {message}")
    
    def _generate_report(self):
        """Generate summary report"""
        
        print("\n" + "=" * 60)
        print("📋 PRODUCTION READINESS SUMMARY")
        print("=" * 60)
        
        total_checks = len(self.checks)
        passed_checks = len([c for c in self.checks if c['status'] == '✅'])
        warning_checks = len([c for c in self.checks if c['status'] == '⚠️'])
        failed_checks = len([c for c in self.checks if c['status'] == '❌'])
        
        print(f"Total Checks: {total_checks}")
        print(f"✅ Passed: {passed_checks}")
        print(f"⚠️  Warnings: {warning_checks}")
        print(f"❌ Failed: {failed_checks}")
        
        if failed_checks == 0 and warning_checks <= 3:
            print("\n🎉 PRODUCTION READY! Your DynamoDB implementation follows best practices.")
        elif failed_checks == 0:
            print("\n✅ MOSTLY READY. Address warnings before production deployment.")
        else:
            print("\n❌ NOT READY. Address failed checks before production deployment.")
        
        return {
            'total': total_checks,
            'passed': passed_checks,
            'warnings': warning_checks,
            'failed': failed_checks,
            'ready': failed_checks == 0 and warning_checks <= 3
        }

# Django management command
class Command(BaseCommand):
    help = 'Run DynamoDB production readiness checks'
    
    def handle(self, *args, **options):
        checker = DynamoDBProductionChecker()
        checker.run_production_checks()
    🔧 Performance Optimization Guide
    # best_practices/performance_optimizer.py
import boto3
from django.utils import timezone
import json

class DynamoDBPerformanceOptimizer:
    """Performance optimization recommendations for anihpj DynamoDB tables"""
    
    def __init__(self):
        self.dynamodb = boto3.client('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
        self.cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch', region_name='us-east-1')
        
    def analyze_hot_partitions(self, table_name):
        """Identify hot partitions and recommend solutions"""
        
        try:
            # Enable Contributors Insights if not already enabled
            self.dynamodb.put_contributor_insights(
                TableName=table_name,
                ContributorInsightsAction='ENABLE'
            )
            
            # Get contributor insights data
            response = self.cloudwatch.get_insight_results(
                InsightArn=f"arn:aws:dynamodb:us-east-1:123456789012:table/{table_name}/contributor-insights/PartitionKey"
            )
            
            recommendations = []
            
            for result in response.get('InsightResults', []):
                contributor = result.get('Contributor', {})
                metrics = result.get('Metrics', [])
                
                if metrics and len(metrics) > 0:
                    request_count = metrics[0].get('Value', 0)
                    
                    # If a single partition key is handling > 10% of requests
                    if request_count > 1000:  # Adjust threshold based on your traffic
                        recommendations.append({
                            'type': 'hot_partition',
                            'partition_key': contributor.get('Keys', [{}])[0].get('S', 'unknown'),
                            'request_count': request_count,
                            'recommendation': 'Consider adding randomness to partition key or using a composite key'
                        })
            
            return recommendations
            
        except Exception as e:
            return [{'type': 'error', 'message': f"Could not analyze partitions: {str(e)}"}]
    
    def optimize_gsi_usage(self, table_name):
        """Analyze and optimize Global Secondary Index usage"""
        
        try:
            response = self.dynamodb.describe_table(TableName=table_name)
            table = response['Table']
            
            recommendations = []
            gsis = table.get('GlobalSecondaryIndexes', [])
            
            for gsi in gsis:
                gsi_name = gsi['IndexName']
                
                # Get GSI metrics
                metrics = self._get_gsi_metrics(table_name, gsi_name)
                
                # Check utilization
                read_utilization = metrics.get('read_utilization', 0)
                write_utilization = metrics.get('write_utilization', 0)
                
                if read_utilization < 10:  # Less than 10% utilization
                    recommendations.append({
                        'type': 'underutilized_gsi',
                        'gsi_name': gsi_name,
                        'utilization': read_utilization,
                        'recommendation': f'GSI {gsi_name} is underutilized ({read_utilization}%). Consider removing if not needed.'
                    })
                
                elif read_utilization > 80:  # High utilization
                    recommendations.append({
                        'type': 'high_gsi_utilization',
                        'gsi_name': gsi_name,
                        'utilization': read_utilization,
                        'recommendation': f'GSI {gsi_name} has high utilization ({read_utilization}%). Consider increasing capacity or optimizing queries.'
                    })
                
                # Check projection efficiency
                projection = gsi.get('Projection', {})
                if projection.get('ProjectionType') == 'ALL':
                    recommendations.append({
                        'type': 'inefficient_projection',
                        'gsi_name': gsi_name,
                        'recommendation': f'GSI {gsi_name} projects ALL attributes. Consider using INCLUDE or KEYS_ONLY for better performance.'
                    })
            
            return recommendations
            
        except Exception as e:
            return [{'type': 'error', 'message': f"Could not analyze GSI usage: {str(e)}"}]
    
    def _get_gsi_metrics(self, table_name, gsi_name):
        """Get CloudWatch metrics for a GSI"""
        
        try:
            # Get read capacity utilization
            read_response = self.cloudwatch.get_metric_statistics(
                Namespace='AWS/DynamoDB',
                MetricName='ConsumedReadCapacityUnits',
                Dimensions=[
                    {'Name': 'TableName', 'Value': table_name},
                    {'Name': 'GlobalSecondaryIndexName', 'Value': gsi_name}
                ],
                StartTime=timezone.now() - timezone.timedelta(hours=24),
                EndTime=timezone.now(),
                Period=3600,
                Statistics=['Average']
            )
            
            read_utilization = 0
            if read_response['Datapoints']:
                avg_consumed = sum(dp['Average'] for dp in read_response['Datapoints']) / len(read_response['Datapoints'])
                # Assuming provisioned capacity (would need to fetch actual provisioned capacity)
                read_utilization = (avg_consumed / 100) * 100  # Placeholder calculation
            
            return {
                'read_utilization': read_utilization,
                'write_utilization': 0  # Similar calculation for writes
            }
            
        except Exception:
            return {'read_utilization': 0, 'write_utilization': 0}
    
    def recommend_capacity_mode(self, table_name):
        """Recommend optimal capacity mode based on usage patterns"""
        
        try:
            # Analyze traffic patterns over the last 30 days
            end_time = timezone.now()
            start_time = end_time - timezone.timedelta(days=30)
            
            # Get read capacity metrics
            read_response = self.cloudwatch.get_metric_statistics(
                Namespace='AWS/DynamoDB',
                MetricName='ConsumedReadCapacityUnits',
                Dimensions=[{'Name': 'TableName', 'Value': table_name}],
                StartTime=start_time,
                EndTime=end_time,
                Period=3600,
                Statistics=['Average', 'Maximum']
            )
            
            # Analyze patterns
            if not read_response['Datapoints']:
                return {'recommendation': 'insufficient_data', 'message': 'Not enough data to make recommendation'}
            
            datapoints = read_response['Datapoints']
            avg_reads = [dp['Average'] for dp in datapoints]
            max_reads = [dp['Maximum'] for dp in datapoints]
            
            # Calculate variability
            avg_read_capacity = sum(avg_reads) / len(avg_reads)
            max_read_capacity = max(max_reads)
            variability_ratio = max_read_capacity / avg_read_capacity if avg_read_capacity > 0 else float('inf')
            
            # Make recommendation
            if variability_ratio > 3:  # High variability
                return {
                    'recommendation': 'on_demand',
                    'reason': f'High traffic variability (ratio: {variability_ratio:.1f})',
                    'message': 'On-demand billing recommended for unpredictable traffic patterns'
                }
            elif avg_read_capacity > 100:  # High consistent usage
                return {
                    'recommendation': 'provisioned_with_autoscaling',
                    'reason': f'Consistent high usage (avg: {avg_read_capacity:.1f} RCU)',
                    'message': 'Provisioned capacity with auto-scaling recommended for cost optimization'
                }
            else:  # Low usage
                return {
                    'recommendation': 'on_demand',
                    'reason': f'Low usage (avg: {avg_read_capacity:.1f} RCU)',
                    'message': 'On-demand billing recommended for low, variable usage'
                }
                
        except Exception as e:
            return {'recommendation': 'error', 'message': f"Could not analyze capacity: {str(e)}"}
    
    def generate_optimization_report(self):
        """Generate comprehensive optimization report"""
        
        tables = [
            'anihpj-user-sessions',
            'anihpj-user-activity',
            'anihpj-job-analytics',
            'anihpj-notifications'
        ]
        
        report = {
            'timestamp': timezone.now().isoformat(),
            'tables': {}
        }
        
        for table_name in tables:
            table_report = {
                'hot_partitions': self.analyze_hot_partitions(table_name),
                'gsi_optimization': self.optimize_gsi_usage(table_name),
                'capacity_recommendation': self.recommend_capacity_mode(table_name)
            }
            
            report['tables'][table_name] = table_report
        
        return report

# Initialize optimizer
performance_optimizer = DynamoDBPerformanceOptimizer()
    🎯 Key Best Practices Summary for anihpj:
    • Design for Access Patterns: Model data based on how you'll query it, not relational principles
    • Distribute Load: Use user_id or compound keys to avoid hot partitions
    • Optimize Costs: Choose right capacity mode based on traffic patterns
    • Monitor Proactively: Set up comprehensive CloudWatch alarms and dashboards
    • Secure by Default: Enable encryption, use IAM roles, implement VPC endpoints
    • Plan for Scale: Use batch operations, implement caching, consider Global Tables
    • Test Thoroughly: Run production readiness checks before deployment

    📚 API/CLI Reference

    Essential DynamoDB API operations and AWS CLI commands for managing your anihpj tables and data.

    🔧 Essential Operations
    Operation API Method Use Case Cost Impact
    📖 Read Item GetItem Get user session 1 RCU per 4KB
    ✏️ Write Item PutItem Save user activity 1 WCU per 1KB
    🔍 Query Items Query Get user's activities RCU per item returned
    📊 Scan Table Scan Analytics queries RCU for entire table
    🔄 Update Item UpdateItem Increment counters 1 WCU per 1KB
    🐍 Python boto3 API Examples
    # api_examples/dynamodb_operations.py
import boto3
from boto3.dynamodb.conditions import Key, Attr
from decimal import Decimal
import json
from django.utils import timezone

class DynamoDBOperations:
    def __init__(self):
        """Initialize DynamoDB operations for anihpj"""
        self.dynamodb = boto3.resource('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
        self.client = boto3.client('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
        
        # Table references
        self.user_sessions = self.dynamodb.Table('anihpj-user-sessions')
        self.user_activity = self.dynamodb.Table('anihpj-user-activity')
        self.job_analytics = self.dynamodb.Table('anihpj-job-analytics')
        self.notifications = self.dynamodb.Table('anihpj-notifications')
    
    # ==================== READ OPERATIONS ====================
    
    def get_user_session(self, session_key):
        """Get user session by session key"""
        try:
            response = self.user_sessions.get_item(
                Key={'session_key': session_key},
                # Use consistent reads for critical data
                ConsistentRead=True
            )
            
            return response.get('Item')
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error getting session: {str(e)}")
            return None
    
    def query_user_activities(self, user_id, limit=50, start_date=None):
        """Query user activities with optional date filtering"""
        try:
            # Build key condition
            key_condition = Key('user_id').eq(user_id)
            
            # Add date range if provided
            if start_date:
                end_date = timezone.now().date().isoformat()
                key_condition = key_condition & Key('timestamp').between(
                    start_date, end_date
                )
            
            response = self.user_activity.query(
                KeyConditionExpression=key_condition,
                ScanIndexForward=False,  # Latest first
                Limit=limit,
                # Only return needed attributes to save bandwidth
                ProjectionExpression='#ts, action_type, action_data',
                ExpressionAttributeNames={'#ts': 'timestamp'}
            )
            
            return response['Items']
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error querying activities: {str(e)}")
            return []
    
    def scan_trending_jobs(self, min_views=100):
        """Scan for trending jobs (use sparingly - expensive operation)"""
        try:
            response = self.job_analytics.scan(
                FilterExpression=Attr('view_count').gt(min_views),
                ProjectionExpression='job_id, view_count, application_count',
                # Use parallel scan for better performance on large tables
                Segment=0,
                TotalSegments=4
            )
            
            return response['Items']
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error scanning trending jobs: {str(e)}")
            return []
    
    # ==================== WRITE OPERATIONS ====================
    
    def create_user_session(self, session_key, user_id, session_data):
        """Create new user session"""
        try:
            response = self.user_sessions.put_item(
                Item={
                    'session_key': session_key,
                    'user_id': user_id,
                    'session_data': json.loads(json.dumps(session_data), parse_float=Decimal),
                    'created_at': timezone.now().isoformat(),
                    'expires_at': int((timezone.now() + timezone.timedelta(days=7)).timestamp())
                },
                # Prevent overwriting existing sessions
                ConditionExpression='attribute_not_exists(session_key)'
            )
            
            return True
            
        except self.client.exceptions.ConditionalCheckFailedException:
            print("Session already exists")
            return False
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error creating session: {str(e)}")
            return False
    
    def log_user_activity(self, user_id, action_type, action_data=None):
        """Log user activity"""
        try:
            timestamp = timezone.now().isoformat()
            
            response = self.user_activity.put_item(
                Item={
                    'user_id': user_id,
                    'timestamp': timestamp,
                    'action_type': action_type,
                    'action_data': json.loads(json.dumps(action_data or {}), parse_float=Decimal),
                    'date': timezone.now().date().isoformat(),  # For GSI queries
                    'ttl': int((timezone.now() + timezone.timedelta(days=365)).timestamp())  # Auto-delete after 1 year
                }
            )
            
            return True
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error logging activity: {str(e)}")
            return False
    
    # ==================== UPDATE OPERATIONS ====================
    
    def increment_job_views(self, job_id, increment=1):
        """Atomically increment job view counter"""
        try:
            today = timezone.now().date().isoformat()
            
            response = self.job_analytics.update_item(
                Key={'job_id': job_id, 'date': today},
                UpdateExpression='ADD view_count :inc SET last_updated = :timestamp',
                ExpressionAttributeValues={
                    ':inc': increment,
                    ':timestamp': timezone.now().isoformat()
                },
                ReturnValues='ALL_NEW'
            )
            
            return response['Attributes']
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error incrementing views: {str(e)}")
            return None
    
    def update_session_data(self, session_key, new_data):
        """Update session data using conditional update"""
        try:
            response = self.user_sessions.update_item(
                Key={'session_key': session_key},
                UpdateExpression='SET session_data = :data, last_updated = :timestamp',
                ConditionExpression='attribute_exists(session_key)',  # Ensure session exists
                ExpressionAttributeValues={
                    ':data': json.loads(json.dumps(new_data), parse_float=Decimal),
                    ':timestamp': timezone.now().isoformat()
                },
                ReturnValues='UPDATED_NEW'
            )
            
            return response['Attributes']
            
        except self.client.exceptions.ConditionalCheckFailedException:
            print("Session does not exist")
            return None
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error updating session: {str(e)}")
            return None
    
    # ==================== BATCH OPERATIONS ====================
    
    def batch_log_activities(self, activities):
        """Efficiently batch write multiple activities"""
        try:
            with self.user_activity.batch_writer() as batch:
                for activity in activities:
                    timestamp = timezone.now().isoformat()
                    
                    item = {
                        'user_id': activity['user_id'],
                        'timestamp': timestamp,
                        'action_type': activity['action_type'],
                        'action_data': json.loads(json.dumps(activity.get('action_data', {})), parse_float=Decimal),
                        'date': timezone.now().date().isoformat()
                    }
                    
                    batch.put_item(Item=item)
            
            return True
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error batch logging activities: {str(e)}")
            return False
    
    def batch_get_sessions(self, session_keys):
        """Get multiple sessions in a single request"""
        try:
            response = self.dynamodb.batch_get_item(
                RequestItems={
                    'anihpj-user-sessions': {
                        'Keys': [{'session_key': key} for key in session_keys],
                        'ProjectionExpression': 'session_key, user_id, session_data'
                    }
                }
            )
            
            return response['Responses']['anihpj-user-sessions']
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Error batch getting sessions: {str(e)}")
            return []
    
    # ==================== TRANSACTION OPERATIONS ====================
    
    def transact_job_application(self, user_id, job_id, credit_cost=1):
        """Job application with credit deduction using transaction"""
        try:
            transaction_items = [
                {
                    'Update': {
                        'TableName': 'anihpj-user-credits',
                        'Key': {'user_id': {'N': str(user_id)}},
                        'UpdateExpression': 'ADD available_credits :decrement',
                        'ConditionExpression': 'available_credits >= :cost',
                        'ExpressionAttributeValues': {
                            ':decrement': {'N': str(-credit_cost)},
                            ':cost': {'N': str(credit_cost)}
                        }
                    }
                },
                {
                    'Put': {
                        'TableName': 'anihpj-job-applications',
                        'Item': {
                            'user_id': {'N': str(user_id)},
                            'job_id': {'N': str(job_id)},
                            'applied_at': {'S': timezone.now().isoformat()},
                            'status': {'S': 'pending'}
                        }
                    }
                }
            ]
            
            response = self.client.transact_write_items(TransactItems=transaction_items)
            return True
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"Transaction failed: {str(e)}")
            return False

# Initialize operations manager
db_ops = DynamoDBOperations()
    💻 AWS CLI Commands
    # ==================== TABLE MANAGEMENT ====================

# Create table with encryption and streams
aws dynamodb create-table \
    --table-name anihpj-user-sessions \
    --attribute-definitions \
        AttributeName=session_key,AttributeType=S \
    --key-schema \
        AttributeName=session_key,KeyType=HASH \
    --billing-mode PAY_PER_REQUEST \
    --stream-specification StreamEnabled=true,StreamViewType=NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES \
    --sse-specification Enabled=true,SSEType=KMS,KMSMasterKeyId=arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012 \
    --point-in-time-recovery-specification PointInTimeRecoveryEnabled=true \
    --tags Key=Application,Value=anihpj Key=Environment,Value=production

# Create table with GSI
aws dynamodb create-table \
    --table-name anihpj-user-activity \
    --attribute-definitions \
        AttributeName=user_id,AttributeType=N \
        AttributeName=timestamp,AttributeType=S \
        AttributeName=date,AttributeType=S \
        AttributeName=action_type,AttributeType=S \
    --key-schema \
        AttributeName=user_id,KeyType=HASH \
        AttributeName=timestamp,KeyType=RANGE \
    --global-secondary-indexes \
        'IndexName=DateActionIndex,KeySchema=[{AttributeName=date,KeyType=HASH},{AttributeName=action_type,KeyType=RANGE}],Projection={ProjectionType=INCLUDE,NonKeyAttributes=[user_id,action_data]},BillingMode=PAY_PER_REQUEST' \
    --billing-mode PAY_PER_REQUEST \
    --stream-specification StreamEnabled=true,StreamViewType=NEW_AND_OLD_IMAGES

# Update table capacity
aws dynamodb update-table \
    --table-name anihpj-user-sessions \
    --provisioned-throughput ReadCapacityUnits=100,WriteCapacityUnits=50

# Enable auto-scaling
aws application-autoscaling register-scalable-target \
    --service-namespace dynamodb \
    --resource-id table/anihpj-user-sessions \
    --scalable-dimension dynamodb:table:ReadCapacityUnits \
    --min-capacity 5 \
    --max-capacity 200

aws application-autoscaling put-scaling-policy \
    --service-namespace dynamodb \
    --resource-id table/anihpj-user-sessions \
    --scalable-dimension dynamodb:table:ReadCapacityUnits \
    --policy-name anihpj-sessions-scaling-policy \
    --policy-type TargetTrackingScaling \
    --target-tracking-scaling-policy-configuration '{
        "TargetValue": 70.0,
        "ScaleInCooldown": 60,
        "ScaleOutCooldown": 60,
        "PredefinedMetricSpecification": {
            "PredefinedMetricType": "DynamoDBReadCapacityUtilization"
        }
    }'

# ==================== DATA OPERATIONS ====================

# Get item
aws dynamodb get-item \
    --table-name anihpj-user-sessions \
    --key '{"session_key": {"S": "django-session-abc123xyz"}}' \
    --consistent-read

# Put item
aws dynamodb put-item \
    --table-name anihpj-user-activity \
    --item '{
        "user_id": {"N": "12345"},
        "timestamp": {"S": "2025-11-10T14:30:00.123Z"},
        "action_type": {"S": "view_job"},
        "action_data": {"M": {
            "job_id": {"N": "789"},
            "job_title": {"S": "Senior Python Developer"}
        }}
    }'

# Query with projection
aws dynamodb query \
    --table-name anihpj-user-activity \
    --key-condition-expression "user_id = :uid" \
    --expression-attribute-values '{":uid": {"N": "12345"}}' \
    --projection-expression "timestamp, action_type, action_data" \
    --scan-index-forward false \
    --limit 20

# Update item with condition
aws dynamodb update-item \
    --table-name anihpj-job-analytics \
    --key '{"job_id": {"N": "789"}, "date": {"S": "2025-11-10"}}' \
