    --update-expression "ADD view_count :inc SET last_updated = :timestamp" \
    --expression-attribute-values '{
        ":inc": {"N": "1"},
        ":timestamp": {"S": "2025-11-10T14:30:00Z"}
    }' \
    --return-values ALL_NEW

# Batch write items
aws dynamodb batch-write-item \
    --request-items '{
        "anihpj-user-activity": [
            {
                "PutRequest": {
                    "Item": {
                        "user_id": {"N": "12345"},
                        "timestamp": {"S": "2025-11-10T14:30:00Z"},
                        "action_type": {"S": "search"},
                        "action_data": {"M": {"search_term": {"S": "python developer"}}}
                    }
                }
            },
            {
                "PutRequest": {
                    "Item": {
                        "user_id": {"N": "12346"},
                        "timestamp": {"S": "2025-11-10T14:31:00Z"},
                        "action_type": {"S": "view_job"},
                        "action_data": {"M": {"job_id": {"N": "790"}}}
                    }
                }
            }
        ]
    }'

# ==================== BACKUP AND RESTORE ====================

# Create on-demand backup
aws dynamodb create-backup \
    --table-name anihpj-user-sessions \
    --backup-name anihpj-sessions-backup-$(date +%Y%m%d)

# Enable point-in-time recovery
aws dynamodb update-continuous-backups \
    --table-name anihpj-user-sessions \
    --point-in-time-recovery-specification PointInTimeRecoveryEnabled=true

# Restore from point-in-time
aws dynamodb restore-table-from-backup \
    --target-table-name anihpj-user-sessions-restored \
    --backup-arn arn:aws:dynamodb:us-east-1:123456789012:table/anihpj-user-sessions/backup/01234567890123-12345678

# ==================== MONITORING ====================

# Create CloudWatch alarm
aws cloudwatch put-metric-alarm \
    --alarm-name "anihpj-dynamodb-high-latency" \
    --alarm-description "DynamoDB high read latency alarm" \
    --metric-name SuccessfulRequestLatency \
    --namespace AWS/DynamoDB \
    --statistic Average \
    --period 300 \
    --threshold 50 \
    --comparison-operator GreaterThanThreshold \
    --evaluation-periods 2 \
    --dimensions Name=TableName,Value=anihpj-user-sessions Name=Operation,Value=GetItem \
    --alarm-actions arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:anihpj-alerts

# Get table metrics
aws cloudwatch get-metric-statistics \
    --namespace AWS/DynamoDB \
    --metric-name ConsumedReadCapacityUnits \
    --dimensions Name=TableName,Value=anihpj-user-sessions \
    --start-time 2025-11-10T00:00:00Z \
    --end-time 2025-11-10T23:59:59Z \
    --period 3600 \
    --statistics Sum

# ==================== GLOBAL TABLES ====================

# Create Global Table
aws dynamodb create-global-table \
    --global-table-name anihpj-user-sessions \
    --replication-group RegionName=us-east-1 RegionName=eu-west-1 RegionName=ap-southeast-1

# Check Global Table status
aws dynamodb describe-global-table \
    --global-table-name anihpj-user-sessions

# ==================== DAX CLUSTER ====================

# Create DAX cluster
aws dax create-cluster \
    --cluster-name anihpj-dax-cluster \
    --node-type dax.r5.large \
    --replication-factor 3 \
    --iam-role-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/DAXServiceRole \
    --subnet-group-name anihpj-dax-subnet-group \
    --security-group-ids sg-12345678 \
    --description "DAX cluster for anihpj job portal"
    📚 API/CLI Quick Reference:
    • Read Operations: GetItem (single), Query (partition), Scan (table-wide)
    • Write Operations: PutItem (create/replace), UpdateItem (modify), DeleteItem
    • Batch Operations: BatchGetItem, BatchWriteItem (up to 25 items)
    • Transactions: TransactWriteItems, TransactGetItems (ACID compliance)
    • Management: CreateTable, UpdateTable, DeleteTable, DescribeTable
    • Monitoring: CloudWatch metrics, Contributors Insights, CloudTrail logs

    💰 Pricing & Cost Optimization

    Understand DynamoDB pricing models and optimize costs for your anihpj job portal infrastructure.

    💳 Pricing Models Comparison
    Pricing Model Best For Read Cost Write Cost anihpj Use Case
    🔄 On-Demand Variable traffic $0.25/million RRU $1.25/million WRU Job search spikes
    ⚡ Provisioned Predictable traffic $0.125/RCU-hour $0.625/WCU-hour User sessions
    💾 Standard IA Infrequent access $0.375/million RRU $1.875/million WRU Old job analytics
    📊 anihpj Cost Analysis Dashboard
    # cost_optimization/dynamodb_cost_analyzer.py
import boto3
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import json
from decimal import Decimal

class DynamoDBCostAnalyzer:
    def __init__(self):
        """Initialize cost analyzer for anihpj DynamoDB usage"""
        self.cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch', region_name='us-east-1')
        self.dynamodb = boto3.client('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
        self.pricing = boto3.client('pricing', region_name='us-east-1')
        
        # anihpj pricing constants (US East 1)
        self.PRICING = {
            'on_demand': {
                'read_request_unit': 0.25,  # per million RRU
                'write_request_unit': 1.25,  # per million WRU
                'storage': 0.25,  # per GB-month
            },
            'provisioned': {
                'read_capacity_unit': 0.000125,  # per RCU-hour
                'write_capacity_unit': 0.000625,  # per WCU-hour
                'storage': 0.25,  # per GB-month
            },
            'dax': {
                'r5_large': 0.34,  # per node-hour
                'r5_xlarge': 0.68,  # per node-hour
            },
            'streams': {
                'read_request': 0.02,  # per 100k reads
            },
            'backups': {
                'continuous': 0.20,  # per GB-month
                'on_demand': 0.10,  # per GB
            }
        }
    
    def get_table_metrics(self, table_name, days=7):
        """Get comprehensive table metrics for cost analysis"""
        end_time = datetime.utcnow()
        start_time = end_time - timedelta(days=days)
        
        metrics = {}
        
        # Get consumed capacity metrics
        for metric_name in ['ConsumedReadCapacityUnits', 'ConsumedWriteCapacityUnits']:
            response = self.cloudwatch.get_metric_statistics(
                Namespace='AWS/DynamoDB',
                MetricName=metric_name,
                Dimensions=[
                    {'Name': 'TableName', 'Value': table_name}
                ],
                StartTime=start_time,
                EndTime=end_time,
                Period=3600,  # 1 hour
                Statistics=['Sum']
            )
            
            metrics[metric_name] = sum(point['Sum'] for point in response['Datapoints'])
        
        # Get table size
        table_info = self.dynamodb.describe_table(TableName=table_name)
        metrics['TableSizeBytes'] = table_info['Table']['TableSizeBytes']
        
        return metrics
    
    def calculate_monthly_costs(self, table_name, traffic_patterns=None):
        """Calculate detailed monthly cost breakdown"""
        
        # Default traffic patterns for anihpj tables
        if not traffic_patterns:
            traffic_patterns = {
                'anihpj-user-sessions': {
                    'reads_per_day': 50000,
                    'writes_per_day': 10000,
                    'avg_item_size_kb': 2,
                    'storage_gb': 5
                },
                'anihpj-user-activity': {
                    'reads_per_day': 20000,
                    'writes_per_day': 25000,
                    'avg_item_size_kb': 1,
                    'storage_gb': 15
                },
                'anihpj-job-analytics': {
                    'reads_per_day': 100000,
                    'writes_per_day': 5000,
                    'avg_item_size_kb': 3,
                    'storage_gb': 8
                }
            }
        
        pattern = traffic_patterns.get(table_name, traffic_patterns['anihpj-user-sessions'])
        
        # Calculate monthly volumes
        monthly_reads = pattern['reads_per_day'] * 30
        monthly_writes = pattern['writes_per_day'] * 30
        
        # Convert to request units (4KB chunks for reads, 1KB for writes)
        read_request_units = monthly_reads * max(1, pattern['avg_item_size_kb'] / 4)
        write_request_units = monthly_writes * pattern['avg_item_size_kb']
        
        # Calculate costs for both models
        costs = {}
        
        # On-Demand pricing
        costs['on_demand'] = {
            'reads': (read_request_units / 1000000) * self.PRICING['on_demand']['read_request_unit'],
            'writes': (write_request_units / 1000000) * self.PRICING['on_demand']['write_request_unit'],
            'storage': pattern['storage_gb'] * self.PRICING['on_demand']['storage'],
            'total': 0
        }
        costs['on_demand']['total'] = sum(costs['on_demand'].values()) - costs['on_demand']['total']
        
        # Provisioned pricing (assuming 70% utilization)
        required_rcu = int((read_request_units / (30 * 24 * 3600)) / 0.7) + 1
        required_wcu = int((write_request_units / (30 * 24 * 3600)) / 0.7) + 1
        
        costs['provisioned'] = {
            'reads': required_rcu * 24 * 30 * self.PRICING['provisioned']['read_capacity_unit'],
            'writes': required_wcu * 24 * 30 * self.PRICING['provisioned']['write_capacity_unit'],
            'storage': pattern['storage_gb'] * self.PRICING['provisioned']['storage'],
            'total': 0,
            'units': {'rcu': required_rcu, 'wcu': required_wcu}
        }
        costs['provisioned']['total'] = sum(v for k, v in costs['provisioned'].items() 
                                          if k not in ['total', 'units'])
        
        return costs
    
    def analyze_cost_savings_opportunities(self):
        """Analyze potential cost savings for anihpj tables"""
        
        anihpj_tables = [
            'anihpj-user-sessions',
            'anihpj-user-activity', 
            'anihpj-job-analytics',
            'anihpj-notifications'
        ]
        
        analysis = {
            'total_monthly_cost': 0,
            'optimization_opportunities': [],
            'table_recommendations': {}
        }
        
        for table in anihpj_tables:
            try:
                costs = self.calculate_monthly_costs(table)
                
                # Determine optimal pricing model
                on_demand_cost = costs['on_demand']['total']
                provisioned_cost = costs['provisioned']['total']
                
                if provisioned_cost < on_demand_cost:
                    savings = on_demand_cost - provisioned_cost
                    recommendation = {
                        'current_model': 'on-demand',
                        'recommended_model': 'provisioned',
                        'monthly_savings': savings,
                        'percentage_savings': (savings / on_demand_cost) * 100,
                        'required_capacity': costs['provisioned']['units']
                    }
                else:
                    recommendation = {
                        'current_model': 'provisioned',
                        'recommended_model': 'on-demand',
                        'monthly_savings': provisioned_cost - on_demand_cost,
                        'percentage_savings': ((provisioned_cost - on_demand_cost) / provisioned_cost) * 100
                    }
                
                analysis['table_recommendations'][table] = recommendation
                analysis['total_monthly_cost'] += min(on_demand_cost, provisioned_cost)
                
            except Exception as e:
                print(f"Error analyzing {table}: {str(e)}")
        
        # Global optimization opportunities
        if analysis['total_monthly_cost'] > 500:
            analysis['optimization_opportunities'].extend([
                "Consider implementing DAX caching for read-heavy workloads",
                "Evaluate Standard-IA storage class for older data",
                "Implement data lifecycle policies with TTL",
                "Consider Reserved Capacity for predictable workloads"
            ])
        
        if analysis['total_monthly_cost'] > 1000:
            analysis['optimization_opportunities'].extend([
                "Implement compression for large items",
                "Use DynamoDB Accelerator (DAX) for microsecond latency",
                "Consider Global Tables for multi-region deployment",
                "Implement smart caching at application layer"
            ])
        
        return analysis
    
    def generate_cost_report(self):
        """Generate comprehensive cost optimization report"""
        
        print("=" * 60)
        print("🏢 ANIHPJ DYNAMODB COST OPTIMIZATION REPORT")
        print("=" * 60)
        
        analysis = self.analyze_cost_savings_opportunities()
        
        print(f"\n💰 Total Estimated Monthly Cost: ${analysis['total_monthly_cost']:.2f}")
        print("\n📊 Table-by-Table Analysis:")
        print("-" * 60)
        
        for table, rec in analysis['table_recommendations'].items():
            print(f"\n🔍 {table}:")
            print(f"   Current Model: {rec['current_model']}")
            print(f"   Recommended: {rec['recommended_model']}")
            
            if rec['monthly_savings'] > 0:
                print(f"   💸 Monthly Savings: ${rec['monthly_savings']:.2f} ({rec['percentage_savings']:.1f}%)")
                if 'required_capacity' in rec:
                    print(f"   ⚡ Required: {rec['required_capacity']['rcu']} RCU, {rec['required_capacity']['wcu']} WCU")
            else:
                print(f"   ✅ Current model is optimal")
        
        print(f"\n🚀 Optimization Opportunities:")
        for i, opportunity in enumerate(analysis['optimization_opportunities'], 1):
            print(f"   {i}. {opportunity}")
        
        # Cost breakdown visualization
        print(f"\n📈 Monthly Cost Breakdown:")
        print("   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐")
        print("   │  Component          │  Cost    │  %    │")
        print("   ├─────────────────────────────────────────┤")
        
        total_cost = analysis['total_monthly_cost']
        components = {
            'Read Operations': total_cost * 0.40,
            'Write Operations': total_cost * 0.35,
            'Storage': total_cost * 0.20,
            'Backup & Recovery': total_cost * 0.05
        }
        
        for component, cost in components.items():
            percentage = (cost / total_cost) * 100
            print(f"   │  {component:<17} │ ${cost:>6.2f} │ {percentage:>4.1f}% │")
        
        print("   └─────────────────────────────────────────┘")
        
        return analysis

# Initialize cost analyzer
cost_analyzer = DynamoDBCostAnalyzer()

# Generate comprehensive report
if __name__ == "__main__":
    cost_analyzer.generate_cost_report()
    💡 Cost Optimization Strategies
    # cost_optimization/optimization_strategies.py
from datetime import datetime, timedelta
import boto3

class CostOptimizationStrategies:
    def __init__(self):
        """Cost optimization strategies for anihpj"""
        self.dynamodb = boto3.resource('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
        
    def implement_ttl_policy(self, table_name, ttl_attribute='ttl'):
        """Implement Time To Live for automatic data cleanup"""
        
        table = self.dynamodb.Table(table_name)
        
        try:
            # Enable TTL
            table.meta.client.update_time_to_live(
                TableName=table_name,
                TimeToLiveSpecification={
                    'AttributeName': ttl_attribute,
                    'Enabled': True
                }
            )
            
            print(f"✅ TTL enabled for {table_name} on attribute '{ttl_attribute}'")
            
            # TTL configuration examples for anihpj tables
            ttl_configs = {
                'anihpj-user-sessions': {
                    'retention_days': 30,
                    'description': 'Session cleanup after 30 days'
                },
                'anihpj-user-activity': {
                    'retention_days': 365,
                    'description': 'Activity logs kept for 1 year'
                },
                'anihpj-temporary-tokens': {
                    'retention_days': 1,
                    'description': 'Temporary tokens expire in 24 hours'
                }
            }
            
            if table_name in ttl_configs:
                config = ttl_configs[table_name]
                ttl_timestamp = int((datetime.now() + timedelta(days=config['retention_days'])).timestamp())
                print(f"📅 Recommended TTL timestamp: {ttl_timestamp} ({config['description']})")
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"❌ Error enabling TTL: {str(e)}")
    
    def configure_auto_scaling(self, table_name, min_capacity=5, max_capacity=100, target_utilization=70):
        """Configure auto-scaling for provisioned tables"""
        
        autoscaling = boto3.client('application-autoscaling', region_name='us-east-1')
        
        try:
            # Register scalable targets
            for capacity_type in ['ReadCapacityUnits', 'WriteCapacityUnits']:
                autoscaling.register_scalable_target(
                    ServiceNamespace='dynamodb',
                    ResourceId=f'table/{table_name}',
                    ScalableDimension=f'dynamodb:table:{capacity_type}',
                    MinCapacity=min_capacity,
                    MaxCapacity=max_capacity
                )
                
                # Create scaling policy
                autoscaling.put_scaling_policy(
                    PolicyName=f'{table_name}-{capacity_type.lower()}-scaling',
                    ServiceNamespace='dynamodb',
                    ResourceId=f'table/{table_name}',
                    ScalableDimension=f'dynamodb:table:{capacity_type}',
                    PolicyType='TargetTrackingScaling',
                    TargetTrackingScalingPolicyConfiguration={
                        'TargetValue': target_utilization,
                        'PredefinedMetricSpecification': {
                            'PredefinedMetricType': f'DynamoDB{capacity_type[:-5]}CapacityUtilization'
                        }
                    }
                )
            
            print(f"✅ Auto-scaling configured for {table_name}")
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"❌ Error configuring auto-scaling: {str(e)}")
    
    def analyze_hot_partitions(self, table_name):
        """Analyze partition distribution for cost optimization"""
        
        cloudwatch = boto3.client('cloudwatch', region_name='us-east-1')
        
        try:
            # Get hot partition metrics
            end_time = datetime.utcnow()
            start_time = end_time - timedelta(hours=24)
            
            response = cloudwatch.get_metric_statistics(
                Namespace='AWS/DynamoDB',
                MetricName='ThrottledRequests',
                Dimensions=[
                    {'Name': 'TableName', 'Value': table_name}
                ],
                StartTime=start_time,
                EndTime=end_time,
                Period=3600,
                Statistics=['Sum']
            )
            
            total_throttles = sum(point['Sum'] for point in response['Datapoints'])
            
            if total_throttles > 0:
                print(f"🚨 {table_name} has {total_throttles} throttled requests in 24h")
                print("💡 Recommendations:")
                print("   - Review partition key distribution")
                print("   - Consider composite partition keys")
                print("   - Implement exponential backoff")
                print("   - Increase provisioned capacity")
            else:
                print(f"✅ {table_name} has no throttling issues")
            
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"❌ Error analyzing partitions: {str(e)}")
    
    def estimate_dax_savings(self, table_name, read_requests_per_second=100):
        """Estimate potential DAX caching savings"""
        
        # DAX pricing (r5.large node)
        dax_hourly_cost = 0.34
        dax_monthly_cost = dax_hourly_cost * 24 * 30 * 3  # 3-node cluster
        
        # Calculate read cost savings
        monthly_reads = read_requests_per_second * 60 * 60 * 24 * 30
        
        # Assume 80% cache hit rate
        cache_hit_rate = 0.8
        cached_requests = monthly_reads * cache_hit_rate
        
        # Cost per million read request units
        cost_per_million_rru = 0.25
        
        # Savings from cached reads
        monthly_savings = (cached_requests / 1000000) * cost_per_million_rru
        
        net_savings = monthly_savings - dax_monthly_cost
        
        print(f"💰 DAX Cost Analysis for {table_name}:")
        print(f"   📊 Monthly reads: {monthly_reads:,.0f}")
        print(f"   ⚡ Cached requests (80%): {cached_requests:,.0f}")
        print(f"   💵 DynamoDB cost savings: ${monthly_savings:.2f}")
        print(f"   🖥️  DAX cluster cost: ${dax_monthly_cost:.2f}")
        print(f"   📈 Net monthly savings: ${net_savings:.2f}")
        
        if net_savings > 0:
            print(f"   ✅ DAX recommended - ROI: {(net_savings/dax_monthly_cost)*100:.1f}%")
        else:
            print(f"   ❌ DAX not cost-effective at current read volume")
        
        return {
            'dax_cost': dax_monthly_cost,
            'savings': monthly_savings,
            'net_savings': net_savings,
            'recommended': net_savings > 0
        }

# Cost optimization utilities
def calculate_anihpj_monthly_costs():
    """Calculate total monthly DynamoDB costs for anihpj"""
    
    tables_usage = {
        'anihpj-user-sessions': {
            'reads_per_second': 20,
            'writes_per_second': 5,
            'size_gb': 5,
            'model': 'provisioned'
        },
        'anihpj-user-activity': {
            'reads_per_second': 10,
            'writes_per_second': 15,
            'size_gb': 15,
            'model': 'on-demand'
        },
        'anihpj-job-analytics': {
            'reads_per_second': 50,
            'writes_per_second': 2,
            'size_gb': 8,
            'model': 'on-demand'
        }
    }
    
    total_cost = 0
    
    print("🏢 ANIHPJ MONTHLY DYNAMODB COST BREAKDOWN")
    print("=" * 50)
    
    for table, usage in tables_usage.items():
        monthly_reads = usage['reads_per_second'] * 60 * 60 * 24 * 30
        monthly_writes = usage['writes_per_second'] * 60 * 60 * 24 * 30
        
        if usage['model'] == 'on-demand':
            read_cost = (monthly_reads / 1000000) * 0.25
            write_cost = (monthly_writes / 1000000) * 1.25
        else:  # provisioned
            # Assume 70% utilization
            rcu_needed = int(usage['reads_per_second'] / 0.7) + 1
            wcu_needed = int(usage['writes_per_second'] / 0.7) + 1
            
            read_cost = rcu_needed * 24 * 30 * 0.000125
            write_cost = wcu_needed * 24 * 30 * 0.000625
        
        storage_cost = usage['size_gb'] * 0.25
        table_cost = read_cost + write_cost + storage_cost
        total_cost += table_cost
        
        print(f"\n📊 {table}:")
        print(f"   Model: {usage['model']}")
        print(f"   Reads: ${read_cost:.2f}")
        print(f"   Writes: ${write_cost:.2f}")
        print(f"   Storage: ${storage_cost:.2f}")
        print(f"   Total: ${table_cost:.2f}")
    
    print(f"\n💰 TOTAL MONTHLY COST: ${total_cost:.2f}")
    
    # Cost optimization recommendations
    if total_cost > 100:
        print(f"\n💡 Cost Optimization Recommendations:")
        print(f"   1. Implement TTL for automatic cleanup")
        print(f"   2. Consider DAX for read-heavy tables")
        print(f"   3. Use Standard-IA for infrequently accessed data")
        print(f"   4. Optimize partition key distribution")
        print(f"   5. Implement application-layer caching")
    
    return total_cost

# Initialize optimization strategies
optimizer = CostOptimizationStrategies()
    💰 Cost Optimization Checklist:
    • 🔄 Pricing Model: On-demand for variable, Provisioned for predictable traffic
    • 📅 TTL Implementation: Automatic cleanup of expired data
    • ⚡ Auto-scaling: Dynamic capacity adjustment based on demand
    • 💾 Storage Classes: Standard-IA for infrequently accessed data
    • 🚀 DAX Caching: Microsecond latency for read-heavy workloads
    • 📊 Monitoring: CloudWatch alarms for cost anomalies
    📈 anihpj Cost Projections (Monthly):
    Startup Phase: $50-150/month Basic tables, on-demand pricing
    Growth Phase: $200-500/month Mixed pricing, auto-scaling enabled
    Scale Phase: $800-2000/month DAX caching, Global Tables, Reserved Capacity

    🎯 Implementation Recommendations

    Strategic recommendations for implementing DynamoDB in your anihpj job portal architecture.

    🗺️ Implementation Roadmap
    Phase Timeline Components Expected Benefits Investment
    🚀 Phase 1: Foundation Week 1-2 User sessions, Basic analytics 40% faster response Low ($50-100/month)
    ⚡ Phase 2: Performance Week 3-4 DAX caching, Auto-scaling 90% latency reduction Medium ($200-400/month)
    🔒 Phase 3: Security Week 5-6 Encryption, VPC Endpoints Enterprise-grade security Low (+$20-50/month)
    🌍 Phase 4: Scale Week 7-8 Global Tables, Streams Global availability High ($500-1000/month)
    🎯 Strategic Decision Matrix
    # recommendations/decision_matrix.py
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import List, Dict
from enum import Enum

class BusinessPhase(Enum):
    STARTUP = "startup"
    GROWTH = "growth"
    SCALE = "scale"
    ENTERPRISE = "enterprise"

class Priority(Enum):
    CRITICAL = "critical"
    HIGH = "high"
    MEDIUM = "medium"
    LOW = "low"

@dataclass
class Recommendation:
    component: str
    description: str
    priority: Priority
    estimated_cost: str
    implementation_time: str
    benefits: List[str]
    considerations: List[str]

class DynamoDBRecommendationEngine:
    def __init__(self, business_phase: BusinessPhase, expected_users: int, budget_monthly: int):
        """Initialize recommendation engine for anihpj"""
        self.business_phase = business_phase
        self.expected_users = expected_users
        self.budget_monthly = budget_monthly
        
    def generate_recommendations(self) -> Dict[str, List[Recommendation]]:
        """Generate phase-specific recommendations"""
        
        recommendations = {
            "immediate": [],
            "short_term": [],
            "long_term": []
        }
        
        # ==================== IMMEDIATE RECOMMENDATIONS ====================
        
        if self.business_phase in [BusinessPhase.STARTUP, BusinessPhase.GROWTH]:
            recommendations["immediate"].extend([
                Recommendation(
                    component="User Sessions Table",
                    description="Replace Django sessions with DynamoDB for better performance",
                    priority=Priority.CRITICAL,
                    estimated_cost="$20-50/month",
                    implementation_time="1-2 days",
                    benefits=[
                        "40% faster session lookup",
                        "Horizontal scalability",
                        "Reduced database load",
                        "Better user experience"
                    ],
                    considerations=[
                        "Requires session middleware changes",
                        "Data migration needed",
                        "Session serialization changes"
                    ]
                ),
                
                Recommendation(
                    component="Activity Logging",
                    description="Track user interactions for analytics and personalization",
                    priority=Priority.HIGH,
                    estimated_cost="$30-80/month",
                    implementation_time="2-3 days",
                    benefits=[
                        "Real-time user insights",
                        "Behavior analysis",
                        "Improved job recommendations",
                        "A/B testing capabilities"
                    ],
                    considerations=[
                        "Privacy compliance required",
                        "Data retention policies needed",
                        "Query optimization important"
                    ]
                )
            ])
        
        # ==================== SHORT-TERM RECOMMENDATIONS ====================
        
        if self.expected_users > 1000:
            recommendations["short_term"].extend([
                Recommendation(
                    component="DAX Caching Layer",
                    description="Implement microsecond-latency caching for read-heavy operations",
                    priority=Priority.HIGH if self.expected_users > 5000 else Priority.MEDIUM,
                    estimated_cost="$250-500/month",
                    implementation_time="3-5 days",
                    benefits=[
                        "91% latency reduction",
                        "Improved user experience",
                        "Reduced DynamoDB costs",
                        "Higher throughput capacity"
                    ],
                    considerations=[
                        "VPC setup required",
                        "Cache invalidation strategy",
                        "Monitoring complexity"
                    ]
                ),
                
                Recommendation(
                    component="Global Secondary Indexes",
                    description="Optimize query patterns for job search and filtering",
                    priority=Priority.HIGH,
                    estimated_cost="$40-120/month",
                    implementation_time="2-4 days",
                    benefits=[
                        "Flexible query patterns",
                        "Better search performance",
                        "Advanced filtering options",
                        "Improved analytics queries"
                    ],
                    considerations=[
                        "Additional storage costs",
                        "Write amplification",
                        "Query pattern planning"
                    ]
                )
            ])
        
        # ==================== LONG-TERM RECOMMENDATIONS ====================
        
        if self.business_phase in [BusinessPhase.SCALE, BusinessPhase.ENTERPRISE]:
            recommendations["long_term"].extend([
                Recommendation(
                    component="Global Tables",
                    description="Multi-region deployment for global availability",
                    priority=Priority.MEDIUM,
                    estimated_cost="$500-1500/month",
                    implementation_time="1-2 weeks",
                    benefits=[
                        "Global low-latency access",
                        "Disaster recovery",
                        "Compliance with data residency",
                        "99.999% availability"
                    ],
                    considerations=[
                        "Cross-region data transfer costs",
                        "Eventual consistency model",
                        "Regional compliance requirements"
                    ]
                ),
                
                Recommendation(
                    component="Advanced Security",
                    description="Enterprise-grade security with encryption and access controls",
                    priority=Priority.CRITICAL,
                    estimated_cost="$50-150/month",
                    implementation_time="1 week",
                    benefits=[
                        "Data encryption at rest/transit",
                        "Fine-grained access control",
                        "Audit trail compliance",
                        "PCI/SOC compliance ready"
                    ],
                    considerations=[
                        "IAM policy complexity",
                        "Key management overhead",
                        "Performance impact minimal"
                    ]
                )
            ])
        
        return recommendations
    
    def calculate_roi_projection(self) -> Dict[str, float]:
        """Calculate ROI for DynamoDB implementation"""
        
        # Current costs (estimated)
        current_database_cost = 200  # PostgreSQL hosting
        current_session_cost = 50   # Redis/Memcached
        current_monitoring_cost = 30
        
        # Performance benefits (estimated value)
        user_experience_value = self.expected_users * 0.50  # $0.50 per user improved experience
        developer_productivity = 2000  # Monthly value of improved dev experience
        reduced_downtime = 500  # Monthly value of better availability
        
        # DynamoDB costs (projected)
        dynamodb_monthly_cost = min(self.budget_monthly * 0.3, 800)  # 30% of budget, max $800
        
        total_current_cost = current_database_cost + current_session_cost + current_monitoring_cost
        total_benefits = user_experience_value + developer_productivity + reduced_downtime
        
        monthly_savings = total_current_cost - dynamodb_monthly_cost
        monthly_value_creation = total_benefits
        
        roi_monthly = ((monthly_savings + monthly_value_creation) / dynamodb_monthly_cost) * 100
        
        return {
            "monthly_savings": monthly_savings,
            "monthly_value_creation": monthly_value_creation,
            "roi_percentage": roi_monthly,
            "payback_months": max(1, dynamodb_monthly_cost / (monthly_savings + monthly_value_creation)),
            "break_even_users": max(100, dynamodb_monthly_cost / 0.50)
        }
    
    def generate_implementation_plan(self) -> str:
        """Generate detailed implementation plan"""
        
        recommendations = self.generate_recommendations()
        roi = self.calculate_roi_projection()
        
        plan = f"""
🏢 ANIHPJ DYNAMODB IMPLEMENTATION PLAN
{'='*50}

📊 Business Context:
   Phase: {self.business_phase.value.title()}
   Expected Users: {self.expected_users:,}
   Monthly Budget: ${self.budget_monthly:,}

💰 ROI Projection:
   Monthly Savings: ${roi['monthly_savings']:.2f}
   Value Creation: ${roi['monthly_value_creation']:.2f}
   ROI: {roi['roi_percentage']:.1f}%
   Payback Period: {roi['payback_months']:.1f} months

🚀 IMMEDIATE ACTIONS (Week 1-2):
"""
        
        for rec in recommendations["immediate"]:
            plan += f"""
   📋 {rec.component}
      Priority: {rec.priority.value.upper()}
      Cost: {rec.estimated_cost}
      Time: {rec.implementation_time}
      Benefits: {', '.join(rec.benefits[:2])}
"""
        
        plan += f"""
⚡ SHORT-TERM GOALS (Month 2-3):
"""
        
        for rec in recommendations["short_term"]:
            plan += f"""
   📋 {rec.component}
      Priority: {rec.priority.value.upper()}
      Cost: {rec.estimated_cost}
      Benefits: {', '.join(rec.benefits[:2])}
"""
        
        plan += f"""
🌍 LONG-TERM VISION (Month 4+):
"""
        
        for rec in recommendations["long_term"]:
            plan += f"""
   📋 {rec.component}
      Priority: {rec.priority.value.upper()}
      Benefits: {', '.join(rec.benefits[:2])}
"""
        
        plan += f"""
🎯 SUCCESS METRICS:
   ✅ 40% improvement in page load times
   ✅ 99.9% uptime achievement
   ✅ 50% reduction in database-related issues
   ✅ 25% improvement in user engagement
   ✅ Cost optimization of 20-30%
"""
        
        return plan

# Usage examples for different business phases
def get_recommendations_for_anihpj():
    """Get tailored recommendations for anihpj"""
    
    # Startup phase recommendations
    startup_engine = DynamoDBRecommendationEngine(
        business_phase=BusinessPhase.STARTUP,
        expected_users=1000,
        budget_monthly=500
    )
    
    # Growth phase recommendations  
    growth_engine = DynamoDBRecommendationEngine(
        business_phase=BusinessPhase.GROWTH,
        expected_users=10000,
        budget_monthly=2000
    )
    
    print("🚀 STARTUP PHASE PLAN:")
    print(startup_engine.generate_implementation_plan())
    
    print("\n" + "="*60 + "\n")
    
    print("📈 GROWTH PHASE PLAN:")
    print(growth_engine.generate_implementation_plan())

if __name__ == "__main__":
    get_recommendations_for_anihpj()
    🔄 Migration Strategy
    # migration/migration_strategy.py
from datetime import datetime
import boto3
from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand

class DynamoDBMigrationStrategy:
    def __init__(self):
        """Initialize migration strategy for anihpj"""
        self.dynamodb = boto3.resource('dynamodb', region_name='us-east-1')
        self.phases = []
        
    def phase_1_session_migration(self):
        """Phase 1: Migrate Django sessions to DynamoDB"""
        
        migration_steps = [
            {
                "step": 1,
                "action": "Create DynamoDB session table",
                "command": "python manage.py create_dynamodb_session_table",
                "validation": "Table exists and accessible",
                "rollback": "Delete table if empty"
            },
            {
                "step": 2, 
                "action": "Deploy dual-write session middleware",
                "command": "Update SESSION_ENGINE setting",
                "validation": "Sessions written to both stores",
                "rollback": "Revert to original session engine"
            },
            {
                "step": 3,
                "action": "Validate DynamoDB session functionality",
                "command": "Run session integration tests",
                "validation": "All tests pass",
                "rollback": "Keep dual-write active"
            },
            {
                "step": 4,
                "action": "Switch to DynamoDB-only sessions",
                "command": "Remove dual-write, use DynamoDB only",
                "validation": "No session errors in logs",
                "rollback": "Re-enable dual-write"
            },
            {
                "step": 5,
                "action": "Clean up old session data",
                "command": "Archive old PostgreSQL sessions",
                "validation": "Data safely archived",
                "rollback": "Keep archive accessible"
            }
        ]
        
        return {
            "phase": "Session Migration",
            "duration": "1-2 weeks",
            "risk_level": "Low",
            "steps": migration_steps,
            "success_criteria": [
                "Zero session-related errors",
                "Improved session lookup performance",
                "Successful user authentication flow"
            ]
        }
    
    def phase_2_analytics_migration(self):
        """Phase 2: Migrate activity tracking to DynamoDB"""
        
        migration_steps = [
            {
                "step": 1,
                "action": "Design activity tracking schema",
                "command": "Create DynamoDB activity tables",
                "validation": "Tables created with correct indexes",
                "rollback": "Delete tables if unused"
            },
            {
                "step": 2,
                "action": "Implement activity logging middleware",
                "command": "Deploy activity tracking code",
                "validation": "Activities logged correctly",
                "rollback": "Disable activity logging"
            },
            {
                "step": 3,
                "action": "Backfill historical data",
                "command": "python manage.py migrate_activity_data",
                "validation": "Data integrity checks pass",
                "rollback": "Remove backfilled data"
            },
            {
                "step": 4,
                "action": "Update analytics dashboards",
                "command": "Switch dashboard queries to DynamoDB",
                "validation": "Dashboards show correct data",
                "rollback": "Revert to original queries"
            }
        ]
        
        return {
            "phase": "Analytics Migration", 
            "duration": "2-3 weeks",
            "risk_level": "Medium",
            "steps": migration_steps,
            "success_criteria": [
                "Real-time activity tracking",
                "Historical data preserved",
                "Analytics queries performing well"
            ]
        }
    
    def generate_migration_checklist(self):
        """Generate comprehensive migration checklist"""
        
        checklist = """
🔄 ANIHPJ DYNAMODB MIGRATION CHECKLIST
{'='*50}

📋 PRE-MIGRATION PREPARATION:
   ☐ AWS account setup and permissions configured
   ☐ DynamoDB tables designed and reviewed
   ☐ Backup strategy implemented
   ☐ Monitoring and alerting configured
   ☐ Development environment tested
   ☐ Team training completed

🚀 PHASE 1: SESSION MIGRATION
   ☐ Create anihpj-user-sessions table
   ☐ Deploy dual-write session middleware
   ☐ Run comprehensive session tests
   ☐ Monitor for 48 hours without issues  
   ☐ Switch to DynamoDB-only sessions
   ☐ Archive old session data
   ☐ Validate user login/logout flows

📊 PHASE 2: ANALYTICS MIGRATION  
   ☐ Create anihpj-user-activity table
   ☐ Create anihpj-job-analytics table
   ☐ Deploy activity tracking middleware
   ☐ Backfill 30 days of historical data
   ☐ Update dashboard queries
   ☐ Validate analytics accuracy
   ☐ Performance test analytics queries

⚡ PHASE 3: PERFORMANCE OPTIMIZATION
   ☐ Implement DAX caching cluster
   ☐ Configure auto-scaling policies
   ☐ Optimize query patterns
   ☐ Set up CloudWatch monitoring
   ☐ Performance test under load
   ☐ Cost optimization review

🔒 PHASE 4: SECURITY HARDENING
   ☐ Enable encryption at rest
   ☐ Configure VPC endpoints
   ☐ Implement fine-grained IAM policies
   ☐ Enable CloudTrail logging
   ☐ Security audit and penetration testing
   ☐ Compliance validation

🌍 PHASE 5: SCALE PREPARATION
   ☐ Set up Global Tables (if needed)
   ☐ Configure cross-region replication
   ☐ Implement disaster recovery procedures
   ☐ Load testing at scale
   ☐ Documentation and runbooks
   ☐ Team handover and training

✅ POST-MIGRATION VALIDATION:
   ☐ All user flows working correctly
   ☐ Performance metrics improved
   ☐ No data loss or corruption
   ☐ Monitoring and alerts functional
   ☐ Cost within expected range
   ☐ Team comfortable with operations

🚨 ROLLBACK PROCEDURES:
   ☐ Database backup restoration tested
   ☐ Session fallback mechanism ready
   ☐ Analytics data export procedures
   ☐ DNS/load balancer rollback plan
   ☐ Communication plan for users
   ☐ Escalation procedures documented
"""
        
        return checklist

# Django management command for migration
class Command(BaseCommand):
    help = 'Migrate anihpj data to DynamoDB'
    
    def add_arguments(self, parser):
        parser.add_argument('--phase', type=str, help='Migration phase: sessions, analytics, performance')
        parser.add_argument('--dry-run', action='store_true', help='Simulate migration without making changes')
        
    def handle(self, *args, **options):
        migration_strategy = DynamoDBMigrationStrategy()
        
        if options['phase'] == 'sessions':
            phase_plan = migration_strategy.phase_1_session_migration()
        elif options['phase'] == 'analytics':
            phase_plan = migration_strategy.phase_2_analytics_migration()
        else:
            self.stdout.write(migration_strategy.generate_migration_checklist())
            return
            
        self.stdout.write(f"Executing {phase_plan['phase']} migration...")
        
        for step in phase_plan['steps']:
            self.stdout.write(f"Step {step['step']}: {step['action']}")
            
            if not options['dry_run']:
                # Execute migration step
                self.stdout.write(f"Running: {step['command']}")
                # Add actual migration logic here
            else:
                self.stdout.write(f"DRY RUN: Would execute {step['command']}")
                
        self.stdout.write(self.style.SUCCESS('Migration phase completed successfully!'))
    🎯 Final Implementation Priorities:
    1. Start Small: Begin with user sessions - low risk, high impact
    2. Measure Everything: Implement comprehensive monitoring from day one
    3. Optimize Gradually: Add DAX, auto-scaling, and advanced features as you grow
    4. Security First: Enable encryption and proper IAM policies immediately
    5. Plan for Scale: Design table schemas with future growth in mind
    6. Cost Management: Regular cost reviews and optimization strategies
    📈 Success Metrics for anihpj:
    Performance: 40% faster page loads, 99.9% uptime
    User Experience: 25% higher engagement, personalized recommendations
    Development: 50% fewer database issues, faster feature delivery
    Business: 20-30% cost optimization, global scalability ready

    Amazon ElastiCache

    Category: Database - In-Memory Data Store  |  Use Case: Caching, Session Management, Real-time Analytics  |  Pricing: Pay-as-you-go, Reserved Nodes  |  Last Updated: November 2025  |  Documentation: AWS ElastiCache Docs

    Fully managed in-memory caching service supporting Redis and Memcached for ultra-fast data retrieval, session storage, and real-time analytics in your anihpj job portal.

    📋 Overview & Introduction

    Amazon ElastiCache is a fully managed in-memory caching service that dramatically improves application performance by serving data from fast, managed caches instead of relying solely on slower disk-based databases.

    🎯 Key Benefits
    • Sub-millisecond Latency: Retrieve data in microseconds for instant responses
    • Fully Managed: Automatic failover, backups, and software patching
    • Scalable: Scale from small caches to hundreds of nodes
    • Two Engines: Choose between Redis (feature-rich) or Memcached (simple)
    • High Availability: Multi-AZ with automatic failover
    • Security: VPC isolation, encryption at rest and in transit
    💡 When to Use ElastiCache for anihpj: Perfect for caching database query results, storing user sessions, caching job search results, leaderboards for top employers, real-time job posting counters, and reducing database load during traffic spikes.
    🤔 Which Engine Should anihpj Use?
    ✅ Recommendation: Redis
    Why Redis for anihpj?
    • Complex data structures for job rankings and leaderboards
    • Persistence for critical session data
    • Pub/Sub for real-time job notifications
    • Multi-AZ replication for high availability
    • Support for sorted sets (trending jobs, top employers)
    ⚖️ Redis vs Memcached Comparison
    Feature Redis Memcached anihpj Use Case
    Data Structures ✅ Strings, Lists, Sets, Sorted Sets, Hashes ❌ Strings only Job recommendations, rankings
    Persistence ✅ RDB & AOF snapshots ❌ No persistence Critical session data
    Replication ✅ Multi-AZ, replicas ❌ No native replication High availability needs
    Pub/Sub ✅ Built-in messaging ❌ Not supported Real-time notifications
    Performance Sub-millisecond Slightly faster for simple ops Both excellent
    Complexity More features, steeper learning ✅ Simple, easy to use Redis for anihpj
    Multi-threading Single-threaded (v6.0+ has multi-threaded I/O) ✅ Multi-threaded Large datasets
    🏗️ ElastiCache in anihpj Architecture
    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                       ANIHPJ JOB PORTAL - ELASTICACHE ARCHITECTURE                     │
│                                                                                         │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                              USER REQUEST FLOW                                   │ │
│  │                                                                                   │ │
│  │   👤 User Request                                                                │ │
│  │   │                                                                               │ │
│  │   ▼                                                                               │ │
│  │  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐      │ │
│  │  │                    Django Application (anihpj)                        │      │ │
│  │  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │      │ │
│  │  │  │  View Layer                                                    │   │      │ │
│  │  │  │  • Check cache first (cache-aside pattern)                    │   │      │ │
│  │  │  │  • If cache hit → return immediately                          │   │      │ │
│  │  │  │  • If cache miss → query database → update cache              │   │      │ │
│  │  │  └────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │      │ │
│  │  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘      │ │
│  │           │                                  │                                    │ │
│  │           ▼ (Cache Check)                   ▼ (Cache Miss → DB Query)           │ │
│  │  ┌─────────────────────┐          ┌──────────────────────────┐                  │ │
│  │  │   ELASTICACHE       │          │    Amazon RDS/Aurora     │                  │ │
│  │  │   (Redis Cluster)   │          │    (PostgreSQL)          │                  │ │
│  │  │                     │          │                          │                  │ │
│  │  │  ┌───────────────┐  │          │  ┌────────────────────┐ │                  │ │
│  │  │  │ Primary Node  │  │          │  │ Job Postings       │ │                  │ │
│  │  │  │ (Read/Write)  │  │          │  │ User Profiles      │ │                  │ │
│  │  │  │               │  │          │  │ Applications       │ │                  │ │
│  │  │  │ • Sessions    │  │          │  │ Companies          │ │                  │ │
│  │  │  │ • Job cache   │──┼──────────┼─▶│ Skills             │ │                  │ │
│  │  │  │ • Search      │  │   Update │  └────────────────────┘ │                  │ │
│  │  │  │ • Counters    │  │   Cache  │                          │                  │ │
│  │  │  └───────────────┘  │          └──────────────────────────┘                  │ │
│  │  │         │            │                                                        │ │
│  │  │         │ Replicate  │                                                        │ │
│  │  │         ▼            │                                                        │ │
│  │  │  ┌───────────────┐  │                                                        │ │
│  │  │  │ Replica Node  │  │   ┌────────────────────────────────────────────┐      │ │
│  │  │  │ (Read-only)   │  │   │        CACHED DATA EXAMPLES                │      │ │
│  │  │  │               │  │   │                                            │      │ │
│  │  │  │ • Failover    │  │   │  🔐 User Sessions                          │      │ │
│  │  │  │ • Read scale  │  │   │     Key: session:{session_id}              │      │ │
│  │  │  │ • HA support  │  │   │     TTL: 2 hours                           │      │ │
│  │  │  └───────────────┘  │   │                                            │      │ │
│  │  └─────────────────────┘   │  📊 Job Search Results                     │      │ │
│  │                             │     Key: search:{query}:{filters}          │      │ │
│  │                             │     TTL: 5 minutes                         │      │ │
│  │  ┌─────────────────────┐   │                                            │      │ │
│  │  │   CACHE STRATEGIES  │   │  👤 User Profiles                          │      │ │
│  │  │                     │   │     Key: user:{user_id}                    │      │ │
│  │  │  1️⃣  Cache-Aside    │   │     TTL: 15 minutes                        │      │ │
│  │  │  2️⃣  Write-Through  │   │                                            │      │ │
│  │  │  3️⃣  Write-Behind   │   │  🏢 Top Employers Leaderboard              │      │ │
│  │  │  4️⃣  Read-Through   │   │     Key: leaderboard:employers             │      │ │
│  │  │  5️⃣  TTL-Based      │   │     Type: Sorted Set                       │      │ │
│  │  └─────────────────────┘   │     Update: Every hour                     │      │ │
│  │                             │                                            │      │ │
│  │                             │  🔥 Trending Jobs                          │      │ │
│  │                             │     Key: trending:jobs:{timeframe}         │      │ │
│  │                             │     Type: Sorted Set                       │      │ │
│  │                             │     TTL: 10 minutes                        │      │ │
│  │                             └────────────────────────────────────────────┘      │ │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
│                                                                                         │
│  ┌───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │
│  │                          PERFORMANCE BENEFITS                                      │ │
│  │                                                                                     │ │
│  │  ⚡ Response Time:        Database: ~50-100ms  →  Cache: ~1-2ms  (50x faster)     │ │
│  │  💰 Cost Reduction:       90% fewer database queries = lower RDS costs            │ │
│  │  📈 Scalability:          Handle 10x more concurrent users                        │ │
│  │  🔒 Session Performance:  Session reads in < 1ms (was ~20ms from PostgreSQL)      │ │
│  │  🔍 Search Speed:         Complex job searches cached for instant results         │ │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    🚀 Quick Start: ElastiCache for anihpj in 5 Steps
    1️⃣ Create Cluster
    Set up Redis cluster with 2 nodes
    2️⃣ Configure Django
    Install django-redis and configure
    3️⃣ Session Storage
    Move sessions from DB to Redis
    4️⃣ Cache Queries
    Implement cache-aside pattern
    5️⃣ Monitor & Scale
    Set up CloudWatch and auto-scaling
    📊 Performance Impact on anihpj Job Portal
    50x
    Faster Response
    100ms → 2ms
    90%
    Fewer DB Queries
    Lower RDS costs
    10x
    Traffic Capacity
    Without scaling DB
    99.9%
    Availability SLA
    Multi-AZ setup

    🔧 2. Core Concepts

    Understanding the fundamental concepts of Amazon ElastiCache is essential for building robust, high-performance caching solutions for the anihpj Django job portal.

    🗃️ Clusters & Nodes
    🎯 What is a Cluster?

    A cluster is a logical grouping of one or more cache nodes, managed as a single unit. Think of it as your caching infrastructure for anihpj.

    Cluster Types:
    🔵 Redis Cluster (Recommended for anihpj)
    • Node Roles: Primary + Replicas
    • Data Distribution: Sharding across nodes
    • High Availability: Auto-failover
    • Scaling: Add/remove shards dynamically
    • Max Nodes: Up to 500 nodes per cluster
    ⚪ Memcached Cluster
    • Node Roles: All nodes equal
    • Data Distribution: Hash-based
    • High Availability: Limited (no replication)
    • Scaling: Add/remove nodes (data loss)
    • Max Nodes: Up to 40 nodes per cluster
    Node Types & Specifications (anihpj Recommendations):
    Node Type vCPUs Memory Network anihpj Use Case Monthly Cost
    cache.t4g.micro 2 0.5 GB Moderate Development/Testing ~$11
    cache.t4g.small 2 1.37 GB Moderate Low-traffic Production ~$24
    cache.m7g.large 2 6.38 GB Up to 12.5 Gbps Production (Recommended) ~$116
    cache.r7g.xlarge 4 26.32 GB Up to 15 Gbps High-traffic Production ~$301
    Example: Create Redis Cluster for anihpj
    # AWS CLI - Create Redis cluster with replicas
aws elasticache create-replication-group \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-prod-cache \
    --replication-group-description "Production cache for anihpj job portal" \
    --engine redis \
    --engine-version 7.1 \
    --cache-node-type cache.m7g.large \
    --num-cache-clusters 3 \
    --automatic-failover-enabled \
    --multi-az-enabled \
    --cache-subnet-group-name anihpj-cache-subnet \
    --security-group-ids sg-0123456789abcdef0 \
    --port 6379 \
    --preferred-cache-cluster-a-zs us-east-1a us-east-1b us-east-1c \
    --snapshot-retention-limit 5 \
    --snapshot-window "03:00-05:00" \
    --at-rest-encryption-enabled \
    --transit-encryption-enabled \
    --auth-token "YourSecurePasswordHere123!" \
    --tags "Key=Project,Value=anihpj" "Key=Environment,Value=production"

# Expected Output:
# {
#     "ReplicationGroup": {
#         "ReplicationGroupId": "anihpj-prod-cache",
#         "Status": "creating",
#         "MemberClusters": ["anihpj-prod-cache-001", "anihpj-prod-cache-002", "anihpj-prod-cache-003"],
#         "NumCacheClusters": 3
#     }
# }
    🔄 Replication & Sharding
    Replication Architecture:
    ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    REDIS REPLICATION FOR ANIHPJ                       │
│                                                                       │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │                    PRIMARY NODE (us-east-1a)                 │    │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │    │
│  │  │  • Accepts READ + WRITE operations                   │   │    │
│  │  │  • Replicates data to replica nodes                  │   │    │
│  │  │  • Endpoint: anihpj-prod.abc123.ng.0001.use1.cache  │   │    │
│  │  │                .amazonaws.com:6379                   │   │    │
│  │  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │    │
│  └──────────────────────┬──────────────────┬────────────────────┘    │
│                         │                  │                         │
│              Async      │                  │      Async              │
│            Replication  │                  │    Replication          │
│                         ▼                  ▼                         │
│  ┌────────────────────────────┐  ┌────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │  REPLICA NODE              │  │  REPLICA NODE              │    │
│  │  (us-east-1b)              │  │  (us-east-1c)              │    │
│  │                            │  │                            │    │
│  │  • READ-ONLY operations    │  │  • READ-ONLY operations    │    │
│  │  • Auto-promoted if        │  │  • Load balancing reads    │    │
│  │    primary fails           │  │  • HA failover target      │    │
│  │  • Reduces read latency    │  │  • Multi-AZ protection     │    │
│  └────────────────────────────┘  └────────────────────────────┘    │
│                                                                       │
│  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐    │
│  │              REPLICATION BENEFITS FOR ANIHPJ                 │    │
│  │                                                               │    │
│  │  ✅ High Availability: Auto-failover in < 30 seconds         │    │
│  │  ✅ Read Scaling: Distribute job search queries             │    │
│  │  ✅ Multi-AZ: Survive AZ failures                            │    │
│  │  ✅ Low Latency: Read from nearest replica                  │    │
│  │  ✅ Zero Downtime: Maintenance on replicas first            │    │
│  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘    │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    Sharding Architecture (Redis Cluster Mode):
    ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                  REDIS CLUSTER MODE SHARDING                              │
│                                                                           │
│  Data automatically distributed across shards (hash slots 0-16383)       │
│                                                                           │
│  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐  ┌────────────────┐            │
│  │   SHARD 1      │  │   SHARD 2      │  │   SHARD 3      │            │
│  │  Slots 0-5460  │  │ Slots 5461-    │  │ Slots 10922-   │            │
│  │                │  │   10921        │  │   16383        │            │
│  │  ┌──────────┐  │  │  ┌──────────┐  │  │  ┌──────────┐  │            │
│  │  │ Primary  │  │  │  │ Primary  │  │  │  │ Primary  │  │            │
│  │  │  Node    │  │  │  │  Node    │  │  │  │  Node    │  │            │
│  │  └────┬─────┘  │  │  └────┬─────┘  │  │  └────┬─────┘  │            │
│  │       │        │  │       │        │  │       │        │            │
│  │       ▼        │  │       ▼        │  │       ▼        │            │
│  │  ┌──────────┐  │  │  ┌──────────┐  │  │  ┌──────────┐  │            │
│  │  │ Replica  │  │  │  │ Replica  │  │  │  │ Replica  │  │            │
│  │  │  Node    │  │  │  │  Node    │  │  │  │  Node    │  │            │
│  │  └──────────┘  │  │  └──────────┘  │  │  └──────────┘  │            │
│  └────────────────┘  └────────────────┘  └────────────────┘            │
│                                                                           │
│  Example: User sessions distributed by session_id hash:                  │
│    session:user123    → Shard 1 (hash slot 3421)                        │
│    session:user456    → Shard 2 (hash slot 8765)                        │
│    session:user789    → Shard 3 (hash slot 14532)                       │
│                                                                           │
│  📊 CLUSTER BENEFITS:                                                     │
│    • Scale horizontally: Add shards for more capacity                    │
│    • Parallel operations: 3x throughput with 3 shards                    │
│    • Fault isolation: Shard failure doesn't affect others                │
│    • Memory scaling: 500GB+ total cache (vs 300GB single node max)       │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    Example: Enable Cluster Mode for anihpj
    # Django settings.py - Configure Redis Cluster
CACHES = {
    'default': {
        'BACKEND': 'django_redis.cache.RedisCache',
        'LOCATION': [
            'redis://anihpj-cluster.abc123.clustercfg.use1.cache.amazonaws.com:6379/0',
        ],
        'OPTIONS': {
            'CLIENT_CLASS': 'django_redis.client.DefaultClient',
            'CONNECTION_POOL_KWARGS': {
                'max_connections': 50,
                'retry_on_timeout': True,
                'socket_keepalive': True,
            },
            'REDIS_CLIENT_KWARGS': {
                'cluster_mode': True,  # Enable cluster mode
                'read_from_replicas': True,  # Read from replicas for scaling
            },
            'PASSWORD': os.environ.get('REDIS_PASSWORD'),
        }
    }
}

# Job portal views.py - Keys automatically distributed across shards
from django.core.cache import cache

def get_job_details(request, job_id):
    # Key automatically hashed and routed to correct shard
    cache_key = f"job:{job_id}"  
    job = cache.get(cache_key)
    
    if not job:
        job = Job.objects.get(id=job_id)
        cache.set(cache_key, job, timeout=300)  # 5 minutes
    
    return render(request, 'job_details.html', {'job': job})
    📊 Redis Data Types & Structures

    Redis supports rich data structures perfect for anihpj's caching needs:

    Data Type Description anihpj Use Case Common Commands
    String Simple key-value pairs User sessions, counters, flags GET, SET, INCR, DECR
    Hash Field-value pairs User profiles, job metadata HGET, HSET, HGETALL
    List Ordered collections Activity feeds, recent searches LPUSH, RPUSH, LRANGE
    Set Unordered unique values Tags, skills, categories SADD, SMEMBERS, SINTER
    Sorted Set Scored ordered sets Leaderboards, trending jobs ZADD, ZRANGE, ZRANK
    Bitmap Bit-level operations User activity tracking SETBIT, GETBIT, BITCOUNT
    HyperLogLog Probabilistic counting Unique visitors count PFADD, PFCOUNT
    🎨 Redis Data Structures for anihpj
    📝 String
    session:user123
    → "{user_id: 123, email: ...}"
    Simple key-value caching
    🗂️ Hash
    user:456
    → {name: "John", email: "..."}
    Structured user profiles
    📋 List
    recent:searches:789
    → ["Python", "Django", "AWS"]
    Recent search history
    🎯 Set
    job:101:skills
    → {Python, Django, PostgreSQL}
    Unique skill tags
    🏆 Sorted Set
    trending:jobs
    → {job_1: 5000, job_2: 3500}
    Leaderboards by score
    🔢 HyperLogLog
    unique:viewers:job_202
    → ~15,432 unique views
    Memory-efficient counting
    Real-World anihpj Examples:
    from django.core.cache import cache
import redis

# Connect to ElastiCache
r = redis.Redis(
    host='anihpj-prod.abc123.ng.0001.use1.cache.amazonaws.com',
    port=6379,
    password=os.environ.get('REDIS_PASSWORD'),
    decode_responses=True,
    ssl=True
)

# 1. STRING: Job view counter
def increment_job_views(job_id):
    key = f"job:views:{job_id}"
    views = r.incr(key)  # Atomic increment
    r.expire(key, 86400)  # 24-hour TTL
    return views

# 2. HASH: User profile caching
def cache_user_profile(user_id, profile_data):
    key = f"user:profile:{user_id}"
    r.hset(key, mapping={
        'name': profile_data['name'],
        'email': profile_data['email'],
        'title': profile_data['title'],
        'resume_url': profile_data['resume_url']
    })
    r.expire(key, 900)  # 15 minutes

def get_user_profile(user_id):
    key = f"user:profile:{user_id}"
    return r.hgetall(key)

# 3. LIST: Recent job searches (FIFO queue)
def add_recent_search(user_id, search_query):
    key = f"user:searches:{user_id}"
    r.lpush(key, search_query)  # Add to front
    r.ltrim(key, 0, 9)  # Keep only 10 most recent
    r.expire(key, 2592000)  # 30 days

def get_recent_searches(user_id):
    key = f"user:searches:{user_id}"
    return r.lrange(key, 0, -1)

# 4. SET: Job tags/skills
def cache_job_skills(job_id, skills):
    key = f"job:skills:{job_id}"
    r.sadd(key, *skills)  # Add multiple skills
    r.expire(key, 3600)  # 1 hour

def find_matching_jobs(required_skills):
    # Find jobs that have ALL required skills (set intersection)
    job_keys = [f"job:skills:{job_id}" for job_id in get_all_job_ids()]
    matching_jobs = []
    for job_key in job_keys:
        job_skills = r.smembers(job_key)
        if set(required_skills).issubset(job_skills):
            matching_jobs.append(job_key.split(':')[-1])
    return matching_jobs

# 5. SORTED SET: Top employers leaderboard
def update_employer_score(employer_id, job_count):
    key = "leaderboard:employers"
    r.zadd(key, {employer_id: job_count})  # Score = number of jobs
    r.expire(key, 3600)  # 1 hour

def get_top_employers(limit=10):
    key = "leaderboard:employers"
    # Get top employers with scores
    return r.zrevrange(key, 0, limit-1, withscores=True)

# 6. SORTED SET: Trending jobs (by view count)
def update_trending_job(job_id, views):
    key = "trending:jobs:daily"
    r.zadd(key, {job_id: views})
    r.expire(key, 86400)  # 24 hours

def get_trending_jobs(limit=20):
    key = "trending:jobs:daily"
    job_ids = r.zrevrange(key, 0, limit-1)
    return job_ids

# 7. BITMAP: Daily active users tracking
def mark_user_active(user_id, date):
    key = f"active:users:{date}"
    r.setbit(key, user_id, 1)
    r.expire(key, 2592000)  # 30 days

def get_active_users_count(date):
    key = f"active:users:{date}"
    return r.bitcount(key)

# 8. HYPERLOGLOG: Unique job viewers
def track_unique_viewer(job_id, user_id):
    key = f"job:unique_viewers:{job_id}"
    r.pfadd(key, user_id)
    r.expire(key, 86400)  # 24 hours

def get_unique_viewers_count(job_id):
    key = f"job:unique_viewers:{job_id}"
    return r.pfcount(key)  # Approximate count (very memory efficient)
    💡 Performance Tip: Choose the right data structure for your use case:
    • Strings: Simple counters, flags → O(1) operations
    • Hashes: Object caching → O(1) field access
    • Lists: Activity feeds → O(1) push/pop, O(N) range
    • Sets: Tags/membership → O(1) add/check, O(N) intersection
    • Sorted Sets: Rankings → O(log N) insert, O(1) rank lookup
    💾 Persistence Options

    Redis provides two persistence mechanisms to protect anihpj's cached data:

    📸 RDB (Redis Database) Snapshots

    How it works: Point-in-time snapshots of the entire dataset

    • Frequency: Configurable intervals (e.g., every hour)
    • Performance: Low overhead, done by child process
    • Storage: Compact binary format
    • Recovery: Fast startup (load .rdb file)
    • Data Loss: Up to snapshot interval
    anihpj Use Case: Daily backups for disaster recovery
    📝 AOF (Append-Only File)

    How it works: Logs every write operation

    • Frequency: Every second or every write
    • Performance: Higher overhead, write amplification
    • Storage: Text log of commands
    • Recovery: Slower (replay all commands)
    • Data Loss: Minimal (1 second max)
    anihpj Use Case: Critical session data protection
    Recommended Configuration for anihpj:
    # AWS CLI - Create cluster with persistence
aws elasticache create-replication-group \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-persistent-cache \
    --replication-group-description "Persistent cache with snapshots" \
    --engine redis \
    --cache-node-type cache.m7g.large \
    --num-cache-clusters 3 \
    --automatic-failover-enabled \
    \
    # RDB Snapshot Configuration
    --snapshot-retention-limit 7 \           # Keep 7 daily snapshots
    --snapshot-window "03:00-05:00" \        # Backup at 3-5 AM UTC
    --preferred-maintenance-window "sun:05:00-sun:07:00" \
    \
    # AOF Persistence (if needed - requires Redis 7.x parameter group)
    --cache-parameter-group-name anihpj-redis7-aof \
    \
    # Auto backups to S3
    --final-snapshot-identifier anihpj-final-snapshot
    Custom Parameter Group for AOF:
    # Create parameter group with AOF enabled
aws elasticache create-cache-parameter-group \
    --cache-parameter-group-name anihpj-redis7-aof \
    --cache-parameter-group-family redis7 \
    --description "Redis 7 with AOF persistence"

# Enable AOF persistence
aws elasticache modify-cache-parameter-group \
    --cache-parameter-group-name anihpj-redis7-aof \
    --parameter-name-values \
        "ParameterName=appendonly,ParameterValue=yes" \
        "ParameterName=appendfsync,ParameterValue=everysec"  # Sync every second

# AOF options:
#   - appendfsync always    → Sync every write (slowest, safest)
#   - appendfsync everysec  → Sync every second (balanced) ✅ RECOMMENDED
#   - appendfsync no        → Let OS decide (fastest, riskiest)
    Feature RDB Snapshots AOF RDB + AOF
    Durability ❌ Low (data loss possible) ✅ High (minimal loss) ✅✅ Highest
    Performance ✅ Best (low overhead) ⚠️ Moderate (write overhead) ⚠️ Moderate
    Recovery Time ✅ Fast ❌ Slow (replay log) ✅ Fast (RDB + AOF)
    File Size ✅ Small (compressed) ❌ Large (text log) ❌ Both files
    anihpj Recommendation ✅ Non-critical cache ✅ Session storage ✅ Mission-critical data
    ⚠️ Important Considerations:
    • Backup Storage: Snapshots stored in S3 (additional cost ~$0.023/GB/month)
    • Performance Impact: Snapshotting causes brief CPU spike
    • Memory Overhead: AOF can use 2x memory during rewrite
    • Cost Trade-off: Balance durability needs vs performance/cost
    anihpj Persistence Strategy:
    # settings.py - Different cache backends for different needs

CACHES = {
    # Non-critical cache (job listings, search results) - RDB only
    'default': {
        'BACKEND': 'django_redis.cache.RedisCache',
        'LOCATION': 'redis://anihpj-cache.abc123.use1.cache.amazonaws.com:6379/0',
        'OPTIONS': {
            'CLIENT_CLASS': 'django_redis.client.DefaultClient',
        }
    },
    
    # Session cache (critical) - RDB + AOF
    'sessions': {
        'BACKEND': 'django_redis.cache.RedisCache',
        'LOCATION': 'redis://anihpj-sessions.abc123.use1.cache.amazonaws.com:6379/0',
        'OPTIONS': {
            'CLIENT_CLASS': 'django_redis.client.DefaultClient',
            # This cluster has both RDB snapshots + AOF enabled
        }
    },
}

# Use persistent cache for sessions
SESSION_ENGINE = 'django.contrib.sessions.backends.cache'
SESSION_CACHE_ALIAS = 'sessions'

    🚀 3. Getting Started with ElastiCache

    This comprehensive guide walks you through setting up Amazon ElastiCache for the anihpj Django job portal, from cluster creation to production deployment.

    📋 Prerequisites
    🎯 Before You Begin:

    Ensure you have the following requirements in place for anihpj:

    1️⃣ AWS Account Setup
    • Active AWS account
    • IAM user with ElastiCache permissions
    • AWS CLI configured locally
    • Billing alerts enabled
    2️⃣ Network Infrastructure
    • VPC with private subnets
    • Subnet group (2+ AZs)
    • Security groups configured
    • Route tables configured
    3️⃣ Django Application
    • Django 4.2+ installed
    • django-redis package
    • redis-py client library
    • Environment variables setup
    4️⃣ Monitoring Tools
    • CloudWatch access
    • SNS topic for alerts
    • Logging configured
    • Grafana/Dashboard (optional)
    Install Required Python Packages:
    # Create virtual environment (if not exists)
python -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate  # Windows: venv\Scripts\activate

# Install Redis client libraries for anihpj
pip install redis==5.0.1           # Core Redis client
pip install django-redis==5.4.0     # Django cache backend
pip install hiredis==2.3.2          # C parser for performance
pip install boto3==1.34.34          # AWS SDK (for parameter store)

# Save to requirements.txt
pip freeze > requirements.txt
    IAM Permissions Required:
    {
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "elasticache:CreateReplicationGroup",
        "elasticache:CreateCacheCluster",
        "elasticache:DescribeReplicationGroups",
        "elasticache:DescribeCacheClusters",
        "elasticache:DescribeCacheSubnetGroups",
        "elasticache:ModifyReplicationGroup",
        "elasticache:DeleteReplicationGroup",
        "elasticache:CreateCacheParameterGroup",
        "elasticache:DescribeCacheParameters",
        "elasticache:ModifyCacheParameterGroup"
      ],
      "Resource": "*"
    },
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "ec2:DescribeVpcs",
        "ec2:DescribeSubnets",
        "ec2:DescribeSecurityGroups",
        "ec2:CreateSecurityGroup",
        "ec2:AuthorizeSecurityGroupIngress"
      ],
      "Resource": "*"
    },
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "cloudwatch:PutMetricAlarm",
        "cloudwatch:DescribeAlarms",
        "sns:CreateTopic",
        "sns:Subscribe"
      ],
      "Resource": "*"
    }
  ]
}
    🎯 Create Your First Redis Cluster

    Follow this step-by-step guide to create a production-ready Redis cluster for anihpj:

    Step 1: Create Subnet Group
    # List available subnets in your VPC
aws ec2 describe-subnets \
    --filters "Name=vpc-id,Values=vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8" \
    --query 'Subnets[*].[SubnetId,AvailabilityZone,CidrBlock]' \
    --output table

# Create cache subnet group (requires 2+ subnets in different AZs)
aws elasticache create-cache-subnet-group \
    --cache-subnet-group-name anihpj-cache-subnet-group \
    --cache-subnet-group-description "Subnet group for anihpj ElastiCache cluster" \
    --subnet-ids subnet-0a1b2c3d subnet-4e5f6g7h subnet-8i9j0k1l

# Expected output:
# {
#     "CacheSubnetGroup": {
#         "CacheSubnetGroupName": "anihpj-cache-subnet-group",
#         "Subnets": [
#             {"SubnetIdentifier": "subnet-0a1b2c3d", "AvailabilityZone": "us-east-1a"},
#             {"SubnetIdentifier": "subnet-4e5f6g7h", "AvailabilityZone": "us-east-1b"},
#             {"SubnetIdentifier": "subnet-8i9j0k1l", "AvailabilityZone": "us-east-1c"}
#         ]
#     }
# }
    Step 2: Create Security Group
    # Create security group for ElastiCache
aws ec2 create-security-group \
    --group-name anihpj-elasticache-sg \
    --description "Security group for anihpj ElastiCache cluster" \
    --vpc-id vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8

# Save the security group ID from output
SG_ID="sg-0123456789abcdef0"

# Allow inbound Redis traffic (port 6379) from Django EC2 instances
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress \
    --group-id $SG_ID \
    --protocol tcp \
    --port 6379 \
    --source-group sg-django-app-servers  # Your Django app security group

# Allow inbound from Lambda functions (if using serverless)
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress \
    --group-id $SG_ID \
    --protocol tcp \
    --port 6379 \
    --source-group sg-lambda-functions

# Verify security group rules
aws ec2 describe-security-groups \
    --group-ids $SG_ID \
    --query 'SecurityGroups[0].IpPermissions'
    Step 3: Create Redis Cluster (Production Configuration)
    # Create Redis 7.1 cluster with Multi-AZ and encryption
aws elasticache create-replication-group \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-prod-redis \
    --replication-group-description "Production Redis cache for anihpj job portal" \
    \
    # Engine Configuration
    --engine redis \
    --engine-version 7.1 \
    --cache-node-type cache.m7g.large \  # 2 vCPUs, 6.38 GB RAM
    \
    # High Availability
    --num-cache-clusters 3 \              # 1 primary + 2 replicas
    --automatic-failover-enabled \
    --multi-az-enabled \
    --preferred-cache-cluster-a-zs us-east-1a us-east-1b us-east-1c \
    \
    # Network Configuration
    --cache-subnet-group-name anihpj-cache-subnet-group \
    --security-group-ids sg-0123456789abcdef0 \
    --port 6379 \
    \
    # Security Configuration
    --at-rest-encryption-enabled \
    --transit-encryption-enabled \
    --auth-token "YourSecureRedisPassword123!@#" \  # 16-128 chars
    \
    # Backup Configuration
    --snapshot-retention-limit 7 \        # Keep 7 daily snapshots
    --snapshot-window "03:00-05:00" \     # Backup window (UTC)
    --preferred-maintenance-window "sun:05:00-sun:07:00" \
    \
    # Parameter Group (default for now, customize later)
    --cache-parameter-group-name default.redis7 \
    \
    # Monitoring
    --notification-topic-arn arn:aws:sns:us-east-1:123456789012:elasticache-alerts \
    \
    # Tags
    --tags \
        "Key=Project,Value=anihpj" \
        "Key=Environment,Value=production" \
        "Key=ManagedBy,Value=terraform" \
        "Key=CostCenter,Value=engineering"

# Monitor cluster creation (takes 10-15 minutes)
aws elasticache describe-replication-groups \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-prod-redis \
    --query 'ReplicationGroups[0].[Status,NodeGroups[0].PrimaryEndpoint.Address]'

# Wait until Status = "available"
    ✅ Cluster Creation Success!

    Your Redis cluster is being created. You'll receive:

    • Primary Endpoint: anihpj-prod-redis.abc123.ng.0001.use1.cache.amazonaws.com
    • Reader Endpoint: anihpj-prod-redis-ro.abc123.ng.0001.use1.cache.amazonaws.com
    • Configuration Endpoint: For cluster mode enabled
    Step 4: Get Connection Details
    # Get primary endpoint (for read/write operations)
