aws elasticache describe-replication-groups \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-prod-redis \
    --query 'ReplicationGroups[0].NodeGroups[0].PrimaryEndpoint.[Address,Port]' \
    --output text

# Output: anihpj-prod-redis.abc123.ng.0001.use1.cache.amazonaws.com 6379

# Get reader endpoint (for read-only operations)
aws elasticache describe-replication-groups \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-prod-redis \
    --query 'ReplicationGroups[0].NodeGroups[0].ReaderEndpoint.[Address,Port]' \
    --output text

# Test connection from EC2 instance (must be in same VPC)
redis-cli -h anihpj-prod-redis.abc123.ng.0001.use1.cache.amazonaws.com \
          -p 6379 \
          -a "YourSecureRedisPassword123!@#" \
          --tls \
          ping

# Expected: PONG
    🔒 Configure Security

    Secure your ElastiCache cluster following AWS best practices:

    1. Store Credentials Securely (AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store)
    # Store Redis password in Parameter Store (encrypted)
aws ssm put-parameter \
    --name "/anihpj/production/redis/auth-token" \
    --description "Redis authentication token for anihpj production" \
    --value "YourSecureRedisPassword123!@#" \
    --type "SecureString" \
    --key-id alias/aws/ssm \  # Use AWS managed key
    --tags "Key=Project,Value=anihpj" "Key=Environment,Value=production"

# Store Redis endpoint
aws ssm put-parameter \
    --name "/anihpj/production/redis/endpoint" \
    --description "Redis primary endpoint" \
    --value "anihpj-prod-redis.abc123.ng.0001.use1.cache.amazonaws.com" \
    --type "String"

# Retrieve parameter (for testing)
aws ssm get-parameter \
    --name "/anihpj/production/redis/auth-token" \
    --with-decryption \
    --query 'Parameter.Value' \
    --output text
    2. IAM Role for EC2/ECS (Grant Parameter Access)
    {
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "ssm:GetParameter",
        "ssm:GetParameters",
        "ssm:GetParametersByPath"
      ],
      "Resource": [
        "arn:aws:ssm:us-east-1:123456789012:parameter/anihpj/production/redis/*"
      ]
    },
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "kms:Decrypt"
      ],
      "Resource": "arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/*",
      "Condition": {
        "StringEquals": {
          "kms:ViaService": "ssm.us-east-1.amazonaws.com"
        }
      }
    }
  ]
}
    3. Enable VPC Flow Logs (Monitor Network Traffic)
    # Create CloudWatch log group
aws logs create-log-group \
    --log-group-name /aws/vpc/anihpj-elasticache-flow-logs

# Enable VPC flow logs for ElastiCache subnet
aws ec2 create-flow-logs \
    --resource-type Subnet \
    --resource-ids subnet-0a1b2c3d subnet-4e5f6g7h \
    --traffic-type ALL \
    --log-destination-type cloud-watch-logs \
    --log-group-name /aws/vpc/anihpj-elasticache-flow-logs \
    --deliver-logs-permission-arn arn:aws:iam::123456789012:role/flowlogsRole
    4. Rotate Auth Token Regularly
    # Modify cluster to update auth token (zero downtime)
aws elasticache modify-replication-group \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-prod-redis \
    --auth-token "NewSecurePassword456!@#" \
    --auth-token-update-strategy ROTATE \  # ROTATE or SET
    --apply-immediately

# ROTATE strategy:
#   - Adds new token alongside old token
#   - Both tokens work during transition
#   - Remove old token after app updates

# Update Parameter Store
aws ssm put-parameter \
    --name "/anihpj/production/redis/auth-token" \
    --value "NewSecurePassword456!@#" \
    --overwrite
    🔐 Security Checklist for anihpj:
    • ✅ Encryption at rest enabled
    • ✅ Encryption in transit (TLS) enabled
    • ✅ Auth token configured (not default)
    • ✅ VPC isolation (no public access)
    • ✅ Security groups restrict access to Django app only
    • ✅ Credentials stored in Parameter Store (not environment variables)
    • ✅ VPC flow logs enabled for audit
    • ✅ CloudWatch logging enabled
    • ✅ Regular auth token rotation (every 90 days)
    • ✅ Multi-AZ for high availability
    🐍 Django Integration

    Complete guide to integrating ElastiCache with the anihpj Django application:

    Step 1: Update Django Settings
    # anihpj/settings.py

import os
import boto3
from botocore.exceptions import ClientError

# Fetch Redis credentials from Parameter Store
def get_parameter(name, decrypt=True):
    """Retrieve parameter from AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store"""
    try:
        ssm = boto3.client('ssm', region_name='us-east-1')
        response = ssm.get_parameter(Name=name, WithDecryption=decrypt)
        return response['Parameter']['Value']
    except ClientError as e:
        print(f"Error fetching parameter {name}: {e}")
        return None

# Redis configuration
REDIS_ENDPOINT = get_parameter('/anihpj/production/redis/endpoint')
REDIS_PASSWORD = get_parameter('/anihpj/production/redis/auth-token')
REDIS_PORT = 6379

# Cache configuration
CACHES = {
    'default': {
        'BACKEND': 'django_redis.cache.RedisCache',
        'LOCATION': f'rediss://{REDIS_ENDPOINT}:{REDIS_PORT}/0',  # rediss:// for TLS
        'OPTIONS': {
            'CLIENT_CLASS': 'django_redis.client.DefaultClient',
            'PASSWORD': REDIS_PASSWORD,
            'CONNECTION_POOL_KWARGS': {
                'max_connections': 50,
                'retry_on_timeout': True,
                'socket_connect_timeout': 5,
                'socket_timeout': 5,
                'socket_keepalive': True,
                'socket_keepalive_options': {
                    1: 1,  # TCP_KEEPIDLE
                    2: 1,  # TCP_KEEPINTVL
                    3: 3,  # TCP_KEEPCNT
                },
            },
            'COMPRESSOR': 'django_redis.compressors.zlib.ZlibCompressor',  # Compress data
            'PARSER_CLASS': 'redis.connection.HiredisParser',  # Fast C parser
            'SERIALIZER': 'django_redis.serializers.json.JSONSerializer',
        },
        'KEY_PREFIX': 'anihpj',  # Namespace all keys
        'VERSION': 1,
        'TIMEOUT': 300,  # Default timeout: 5 minutes
    }
}

# Session configuration (use Redis for sessions)
SESSION_ENGINE = 'django.contrib.sessions.backends.cache'
SESSION_CACHE_ALIAS = 'default'
SESSION_COOKIE_AGE = 7200  # 2 hours
SESSION_SAVE_EVERY_REQUEST = False  # Only save if modified

# Cache key prefixes for different data types
CACHE_KEY_PREFIXES = {
    'jobs': 'job',
    'users': 'user',
    'employers': 'employer',
    'search': 'search',
    'leaderboard': 'leaderboard',
    'trending': 'trending',
}
    Step 2: Create Cache Utility Functions
    # anihpj/utils/cache.py

from django.core.cache import cache
from django.conf import settings
from functools import wraps
import hashlib
import json
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

def get_cache_key(prefix, *args, **kwargs):
    """
    Generate consistent cache key from arguments
    Example: get_cache_key('job', job_id=123) → 'anihpj:1:job:123'
    """
    parts = [prefix]
    parts.extend(str(arg) for arg in args)
    parts.extend(f"{k}:{v}" for k, v in sorted(kwargs.items()))
    return ':'.join(parts)

def cache_result(timeout=300, prefix='data'):
    """
    Decorator to cache function results
    Usage: @cache_result(timeout=600, prefix='job')
    """
    def decorator(func):
        @wraps(func)
        def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
            # Generate cache key from function name + arguments
            key_data = {
                'func': func.__name__,
                'args': args,
                'kwargs': kwargs
            }
            key_hash = hashlib.md5(
                json.dumps(key_data, sort_keys=True).encode()
            ).hexdigest()[:12]
            cache_key = get_cache_key(prefix, key_hash)
            
            # Try to get from cache
            result = cache.get(cache_key)
            if result is not None:
                logger.debug(f"Cache HIT: {cache_key}")
                return result
            
            # Cache miss - execute function
            logger.debug(f"Cache MISS: {cache_key}")
            result = func(*args, **kwargs)
            
            # Store in cache
            cache.set(cache_key, result, timeout)
            return result
        return wrapper
    return decorator

def invalidate_cache_pattern(pattern):
    """
    Invalidate all cache keys matching pattern
    Example: invalidate_cache_pattern('job:123:*')
    """
    try:
        # Get Redis client
        from django_redis import get_redis_connection
        redis_client = get_redis_connection("default")
        
        # Find and delete matching keys
        full_pattern = f"{settings.CACHES['default']['KEY_PREFIX']}:*:{pattern}"
        keys = redis_client.keys(full_pattern)
        if keys:
            redis_client.delete(*keys)
            logger.info(f"Invalidated {len(keys)} cache keys matching {pattern}")
            return len(keys)
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error(f"Error invalidating cache pattern {pattern}: {e}")
    return 0

# Cache invalidation helpers
def invalidate_job_cache(job_id):
    """Invalidate all caches related to a specific job"""
    patterns = [
        f"job:{job_id}",
        f"job:{job_id}:*",
        f"search:*",  # Invalidate search results
        f"trending:*",  # Invalidate trending jobs
    ]
    for pattern in patterns:
        invalidate_cache_pattern(pattern)

def invalidate_user_cache(user_id):
    """Invalidate all caches related to a specific user"""
    patterns = [
        f"user:{user_id}",
        f"user:{user_id}:*",
    ]
    for pattern in patterns:
        invalidate_cache_pattern(pattern)
    Step 3: Implement Caching in Views
    # jobpost/views.py

from django.core.cache import cache
from django.views.decorators.cache import cache_page
from anihpj.utils.cache import cache_result, get_cache_key, invalidate_job_cache

# Method 1: View-level caching (cache entire response)
@cache_page(60 * 5)  # Cache for 5 minutes
def job_listing(request):
    """List all jobs - cached per URL query params"""
    jobs = Job.objects.filter(is_active=True).select_related('employer')
    return render(request, 'jobs/listing.html', {'jobs': jobs})

# Method 2: Manual cache-aside pattern
def job_detail(request, job_id):
    """Get job details with manual caching"""
    cache_key = get_cache_key('job', job_id=job_id)
    
    # Try cache first
    job = cache.get(cache_key)
    if job is None:
        # Cache miss - fetch from database
        job = Job.objects.select_related('employer').get(id=job_id)
        
        # Store in cache (5 minutes)
        cache.set(cache_key, job, timeout=300)
    
    return render(request, 'jobs/detail.html', {'job': job})

# Method 3: Using decorator for function-level caching
@cache_result(timeout=600, prefix='search')
def search_jobs(query, filters=None):
    """
    Search jobs with caching
    Results cached for 10 minutes per unique query+filters combination
    """
    jobs = Job.objects.filter(
        title__icontains=query,
        is_active=True
    )
    
    if filters:
        if filters.get('location'):
            jobs = jobs.filter(location=filters['location'])
        if filters.get('category'):
            jobs = jobs.filter(category=filters['category'])
    
    return list(jobs.values())

# Method 4: Cache invalidation on updates
def update_job(request, job_id):
    """Update job and invalidate related caches"""
    job = Job.objects.get(id=job_id)
    
    if request.method == 'POST':
        # Update job
        job.title = request.POST.get('title')
        job.description = request.POST.get('description')
        job.save()
        
        # Invalidate caches
        invalidate_job_cache(job_id)
        
        return redirect('job_detail', job_id=job_id)
    
    return render(request, 'jobs/edit.html', {'job': job})
    Step 4: Session Management
    # Test session storage in Redis

# In Django shell or view:
def test_session(request):
    # Store data in session (automatically saved to Redis)
    request.session['user_preferences'] = {
        'theme': 'dark',
        'notifications': True,
        'location': 'New York'
    }
    
    request.session['job_search_history'] = [
        'python developer',
        'django engineer',
        'backend developer'
    ]
    
    # Session automatically saved to Redis:
    # Key: anihpj:1:session:{session_key}
    # TTL: 7200 seconds (2 hours)
    
    return JsonResponse({
        'session_key': request.session.session_key,
        'stored_in': 'ElastiCache Redis',
        'data': dict(request.session.items())
    })
    Step 5: Health Check & Monitoring
    # anihpj/views/health.py

from django.http import JsonResponse
from django.core.cache import cache
from django_redis import get_redis_connection
import time

def cache_health_check(request):
    """Health check endpoint for ElastiCache"""
    try:
        # Test 1: Basic connectivity
        start = time.time()
        cache.set('health_check', 'ok', timeout=10)
        value = cache.get('health_check')
        latency = (time.time() - start) * 1000  # ms
        
        if value != 'ok':
            return JsonResponse({
                'status': 'unhealthy',
                'error': 'Cache value mismatch'
            }, status=503)
        
        # Test 2: Redis INFO
        redis_conn = get_redis_connection('default')
        info = redis_conn.info()
        
        return JsonResponse({
            'status': 'healthy',
            'latency_ms': round(latency, 2),
            'redis_version': info.get('redis_version'),
            'connected_clients': info.get('connected_clients'),
            'used_memory_human': info.get('used_memory_human'),
            'uptime_days': info.get('uptime_in_days'),
            'hit_rate': calculate_hit_rate(info),
        })
    except Exception as e:
        return JsonResponse({
            'status': 'unhealthy',
            'error': str(e)
        }, status=503)

def calculate_hit_rate(info):
    """Calculate cache hit rate percentage"""
    hits = info.get('keyspace_hits', 0)
    misses = info.get('keyspace_misses', 0)
    total = hits + misses
    if total == 0:
        return 0
    return round((hits / total) * 100, 2)
    ✅ Django Integration Complete!

    Your anihpj Django application is now connected to ElastiCache. Test with:

    # Test connection
python manage.py shell
>>> from django.core.cache import cache
>>> cache.set('test', 'Hello ElastiCache!', 60)
>>> cache.get('test')
'Hello ElastiCache!'

# Check health endpoint
curl http://localhost:8000/health/cache/

    🔑 4. Key Features & Components

    💡 Caching Strategies

    Choosing the right caching strategy is critical for anihpj's performance. Each pattern has specific use cases, benefits, and trade-offs.

    📖 Cache-Aside Pattern (Lazy Loading)
    🎯 Most Common Pattern - Recommended for anihpj Job Listings

    Application checks cache first; on miss, loads from database and populates cache.

    How It Works:
    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    CACHE-ASIDE PATTERN FLOW                          │
│                                                                      │
│  1️⃣  APPLICATION REQUESTS DATA                                       │
│      ┌─────────────┐                                                │
│      │   Django    │                                                │
│      │   View      │                                                │
│      └──────┬──────┘                                                │
│             │                                                        │
│             │ get(key)                                               │
│             ▼                                                        │
│      ┌─────────────┐                                                │
│      │             │                                                │
│      │ ElastiCache │                                                │
│      │   (Redis)   │                                                │
│      └──────┬──────┘                                                │
│             │                                                        │
│        ┌────┴────┐                                                  │
│        │         │                                                  │
│   ┌────▼────┐ ┌──▼─────┐                                           │
│   │ CACHE   │ │ CACHE  │                                           │
│   │  HIT    │ │  MISS  │                                           │
│   │  ✅     │ │   ❌   │                                           │
│   └────┬────┘ └───┬────┘                                           │
│        │          │                                                 │
│        │          │ Query Database                                 │
│        │          ▼                                                 │
│        │   ┌──────────────┐                                        │
│        │   │  PostgreSQL  │                                        │
│        │   │   Database   │                                        │
│        │   └──────┬───────┘                                        │
│        │          │                                                 │
│        │          │ set(key, value, ttl)                           │
│        │          ▼                                                 │
│        │   ┌─────────────┐                                         │
│        │   │  Update     │                                         │
│        │   │  Cache      │                                         │
│        │   └──────┬──────┘                                         │
│        │          │                                                 │
│        └──────────┴──► Return Data to Application                  │
│                                                                      │
│  ✅ ADVANTAGES:                                                      │
│    • Only caches requested data (efficient memory usage)            │
│    • Resilient to cache failures (app still works)                 │
│    • Simple to implement                                           │
│                                                                      │
│  ❌ DISADVANTAGES:                                                   │
│    • Initial request slower (cache miss penalty)                   │
│    • Multiple requests may hit DB simultaneously                   │
│    • Stale data possible until TTL expires                         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    Implementation for anihpj Job Listings:
    # jobpost/views.py
from django.core.cache import cache
from django.db.models import Q
from .models import Job
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

def get_job_details(job_id):
    """
    Cache-aside pattern for job details
    Use Case: Job detail pages, frequently viewed jobs
    """
    cache_key = f"job:detail:{job_id}"
    
    # Step 1: Check cache first
    job = cache.get(cache_key)
    
    if job is not None:
        logger.info(f"Cache HIT: {cache_key}")
        return job
    
    # Step 2: Cache miss - query database
    logger.info(f"Cache MISS: {cache_key}")
    try:
        job = Job.objects.select_related('employer', 'category').get(
            id=job_id,
            is_active=True
        )
        
        # Step 3: Store in cache with 5-minute TTL
        cache.set(cache_key, job, timeout=300)
        
        return job
    except Job.DoesNotExist:
        # Cache negative results too (prevent DB hammering)
        cache.set(cache_key, None, timeout=60)  # 1 minute for null
        return None

def search_jobs(query, location=None, category=None, page=1):
    """
    Cache-aside for job search results
    Use Case: Search results caching
    """
    # Create cache key from search parameters
    cache_key = f"search:{query}:{location}:{category}:{page}"
    
    # Check cache
    results = cache.get(cache_key)
    if results is not None:
        logger.info(f"Search cache HIT: {cache_key}")
        return results
    
    # Cache miss - perform search
    logger.info(f"Search cache MISS: {cache_key}")
    jobs = Job.objects.filter(is_active=True)
    
    if query:
        jobs = jobs.filter(
            Q(title__icontains=query) | 
            Q(description__icontains=query)
        )
    
    if location:
        jobs = jobs.filter(location__icontains=location)
    
    if category:
        jobs = jobs.filter(category_id=category)
    
    # Paginate
    from django.core.paginator import Paginator
    paginator = Paginator(jobs, 20)
    results = paginator.get_page(page)
    
    # Cache for 5 minutes (search results change frequently)
    cache.set(cache_key, results, timeout=300)
    
    return results

def get_employer_jobs(employer_id):
    """
    Cache-aside for employer's job listings
    Use Case: Employer profile pages
    """
    cache_key = f"employer:jobs:{employer_id}"
    
    jobs = cache.get(cache_key)
    if jobs is None:
        jobs = Job.objects.filter(
            employer_id=employer_id,
            is_active=True
        ).order_by('-created_at')
        
        # Cache for 10 minutes
        cache.set(cache_key, list(jobs), timeout=600)
    
    return jobs
    💡 Best Practices for Cache-Aside in anihpj:
    • Set appropriate TTLs: 5 min for volatile data, 1 hour for stable data
    • Cache negative results: Prevent DB hammering for non-existent jobs
    • Handle cache failures gracefully: Always query DB if cache is down
    • Use consistent key naming: e.g., job:detail:{id}
    ✍️ Write-Through Pattern
    🎯 Recommended for Critical Data - anihpj User Profiles

    Data is written to cache and database simultaneously, ensuring cache is always fresh.

    How It Works:
    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   WRITE-THROUGH PATTERN FLOW                         │
│                                                                      │
│  📝 WRITE OPERATION (Update User Profile)                           │
│                                                                      │
│      ┌─────────────┐                                                │
│      │  Django     │                                                │
│      │  View       │                                                │
│      │  (Update)   │                                                │
│      └──────┬──────┘                                                │
│             │                                                        │
│             │ 1. Write to cache                                     │
│             ▼                                                        │
│      ┌─────────────┐                                                │
│      │ ElastiCache │ ◄────── set(key, value)                       │
│      │   (Redis)   │                                                │
│      └──────┬──────┘                                                │
│             │                                                        │
│             │ 2. Write to database (synchronous)                    │
│             ▼                                                        │
│      ┌─────────────┐                                                │
│      │ PostgreSQL  │ ◄────── UPDATE users SET ...                  │
│      │  Database   │                                                │
│      └──────┬──────┘                                                │
│             │                                                        │
│             └──► 3. Return success                                  │
│                                                                      │
│  📖 READ OPERATION (Get User Profile)                               │
│                                                                      │
│      ┌─────────────┐                                                │
│      │  Django     │                                                │
│      │  View       │                                                │
│      └──────┬──────┘                                                │
│             │                                                        │
│             │ get(key)                                               │
│             ▼                                                        │
│      ┌─────────────┐                                                │
│      │ ElastiCache │ ────► Always returns fresh data ✅             │
│      │   (Redis)   │       (never stale)                           │
│      └─────────────┘                                                │
│                                                                      │
│  ✅ ADVANTAGES:                                                      │
│    • Cache always has latest data (no staleness)                    │
│    • Read operations always fast (cache guaranteed)                │
│    • Consistent data between cache and DB                          │
│                                                                      │
│  ❌ DISADVANTAGES:                                                   │
│    • Write latency increased (2 operations)                        │
│    • Cache may fill with unused data                               │
│    • Write failures more complex to handle                         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    Implementation for anihpj User Profiles:
    # users/services.py
from django.core.cache import cache
from django.db import transaction
from .models import UserProfile

class UserProfileService:
    """Service layer for user profile with write-through caching"""
    
    @staticmethod
    def get_cache_key(user_id):
        return f"user:profile:{user_id}"
    
    @staticmethod
    def get_profile(user_id):
        """
        Read with write-through: Cache always has fresh data
        """
        cache_key = UserProfileService.get_cache_key(user_id)
        
        # Cache should always have data (written during updates)
        profile = cache.get(cache_key)
        
        if profile is None:
            # Cache miss (shouldn't happen often with write-through)
            # Load from DB and populate cache
            profile = UserProfile.objects.get(user_id=user_id)
            cache.set(cache_key, profile, timeout=3600)  # 1 hour
        
        return profile
    
    @staticmethod
    @transaction.atomic
    def update_profile(user_id, **kwargs):
        """
        Write-through: Update cache AND database simultaneously
        """
        cache_key = UserProfileService.get_cache_key(user_id)
        
        try:
            # Step 1: Update database
            profile = UserProfile.objects.select_for_update().get(user_id=user_id)
            
            for key, value in kwargs.items():
                setattr(profile, key, value)
            
            profile.save()
            
            # Step 2: Update cache immediately (synchronous)
            cache.set(cache_key, profile, timeout=3600)
            
            return profile
        
        except Exception as e:
            # Rollback transaction and invalidate cache
            cache.delete(cache_key)
            raise e
    
    @staticmethod
    @transaction.atomic
    def create_profile(user_id, **kwargs):
        """
        Write-through: Create in DB and cache simultaneously
        """
        cache_key = UserProfileService.get_cache_key(user_id)
        
        # Create profile
        profile = UserProfile.objects.create(user_id=user_id, **kwargs)
        
        # Immediately cache it
        cache.set(cache_key, profile, timeout=3600)
        
        return profile

# jobpost/views.py - Using the service
def update_user_profile(request):
    """Update user profile with write-through caching"""
    if request.method == 'POST':
        user_id = request.user.id
        
        # Write-through update
        profile = UserProfileService.update_profile(
            user_id=user_id,
            bio=request.POST.get('bio'),
            phone=request.POST.get('phone'),
            linkedin_url=request.POST.get('linkedin_url')
        )
        
        # Cache now has fresh data automatically
        return JsonResponse({'status': 'success', 'profile': profile.to_dict()})
    
    # Read from cache (always fresh)
    profile = UserProfileService.get_profile(request.user.id)
    return render(request, 'profile.html', {'profile': profile})
    ⚠️ Write-Through Considerations:
    • Use for frequently read data: User profiles, employer info
    • Handle write failures: Invalidate cache if DB write fails
    • Consider write latency: 2x slower writes (cache + DB)
    • Memory management: May cache data that's never read
    ⏳ Write-Behind Pattern (Write-Back)
    🎯 High-Performance Writes - anihpj Analytics & Counters

    Data written to cache immediately, then asynchronously persisted to database.

    How It Works:
    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                   WRITE-BEHIND PATTERN FLOW                          │
│                                                                      │
│  ⚡ WRITE OPERATION (Job View Counter)                               │
│                                                                      │
│      ┌─────────────┐                                                │
│      │  Django     │                                                │
│      │  View       │                                                │
│      └──────┬──────┘                                                │
│             │                                                        │
│             │ 1. Write to cache ONLY (fast!)                        │
│             ▼                                                        │
│      ┌─────────────┐                                                │
│      │ ElastiCache │ ◄────── INCR job:views:123                    │
│      │   (Redis)   │         Returns immediately ✅                 │
│      └──────┬──────┘                                                │
│             │                                                        │
│             │ 2. Queue for async DB update                          │
│             ▼                                                        │
│      ┌─────────────┐                                                │
│      │  Redis      │                                                │
│      │  List/Queue │ ◄────── LPUSH job:updates:queue               │
│      └──────┬──────┘                                                │
│             │                                                        │
│             │ 3. Background worker processes queue                  │
│             ▼                                                        │
│      ┌─────────────┐                                                │
│      │  Celery     │                                                │
│      │  Worker     │ ────────► Batches updates                     │
│      └──────┬──────┘           Every 5 minutes                      │
│             │                                                        │
│             │ 4. Batch write to database                            │
│             ▼                                                        │
│      ┌─────────────┐                                                │
│      │ PostgreSQL  │ ◄────── UPDATE jobs SET views = views + 100   │
│      │  Database   │         WHERE id IN (...)                     │
│      └─────────────┘                                                │
│                                                                      │
│  ✅ ADVANTAGES:                                                      │
│    • Extremely fast writes (cache only)                             │
│    • Reduced DB load (batched updates)                             │
│    • Better performance under high load                            │
│                                                                      │
│  ❌ DISADVANTAGES:                                                   │
│    • Data loss risk if cache fails before DB sync                  │
│    • Complex implementation (needs queue + workers)                │
│    • Cache and DB temporarily inconsistent                         │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    Implementation for anihpj Job View Counters:
    # jobpost/cache_services.py
from django.core.cache import cache
from django_redis import get_redis_connection
from celery import shared_task
import logging

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

class JobViewCounterService:
    """Write-behind caching for job view counters"""
    
    QUEUE_KEY = "job:views:sync_queue"
    BATCH_SIZE = 100
    SYNC_INTERVAL = 300  # 5 minutes
    
    @staticmethod
    def increment_view_count(job_id):
        """
        Fast increment in cache only (write-behind)
        DB sync happens asynchronously
        """
        redis_conn = get_redis_connection("default")
        
        # Step 1: Increment counter in Redis (atomic, fast)
        cache_key = f"job:views:{job_id}"
        new_count = redis_conn.incr(cache_key)
        
        # Set TTL if this is first increment
        if new_count == 1:
            redis_conn.expire(cache_key, 86400)  # 24 hours
        
        # Step 2: Add to sync queue for background processing
        redis_conn.sadd(JobViewCounterService.QUEUE_KEY, job_id)
        
        logger.debug(f"Job {job_id} views incremented to {new_count} (cache only)")
        
        return new_count
    
    @staticmethod
    def get_view_count(job_id):
        """Get current view count from cache"""
        cache_key = f"job:views:{job_id}"
        redis_conn = get_redis_connection("default")
        
        count = redis_conn.get(cache_key)
        return int(count) if count else 0

# Celery task for async DB synchronization
@shared_task
def sync_job_views_to_database():
    """
    Background task to sync cached view counts to database
    Runs every 5 minutes via Celery Beat
    """
    from .models import Job
    from .cache_services import JobViewCounterService
    
    redis_conn = get_redis_connection("default")
    queue_key = JobViewCounterService.QUEUE_KEY
    
    # Get all job IDs pending sync
    job_ids = redis_conn.smembers(queue_key)
    
    if not job_ids:
        logger.info("No job views to sync")
        return
    
    logger.info(f"Syncing {len(job_ids)} job view counts to database")
    
    synced = 0
    for job_id in job_ids:
        try:
            job_id = int(job_id)
            cache_key = f"job:views:{job_id}"
            
            # Get cached count
            cached_count = redis_conn.get(cache_key)
            if not cached_count:
                continue
            
            cached_count = int(cached_count)
            
            # Update database
            Job.objects.filter(id=job_id).update(views=cached_count)
            
            # Remove from sync queue
            redis_conn.srem(queue_key, job_id)
            synced += 1
            
        except Exception as e:
            logger.error(f"Error syncing job {job_id} views: {e}")
    
    logger.info(f"Successfully synced {synced} job view counts")
    return synced

# Celery beat schedule (settings.py)
from celery.schedules import crontab

CELERY_BEAT_SCHEDULE = {
    'sync-job-views': {
        'task': 'jobpost.tasks.sync_job_views_to_database',
        'schedule': 300.0,  # Every 5 minutes
    },
}

# jobpost/views.py - Using write-behind
def job_detail(request, job_id):
    """Job detail view with write-behind counter"""
    
    # Increment view counter (fast, cache-only)
    JobViewCounterService.increment_view_count(job_id)
    
    # Get job from cache-aside
    job = get_job_details(job_id)
    
    # Get real-time view count from cache
    view_count = JobViewCounterService.get_view_count(job_id)
    
    return render(request, 'job_detail.html', {
        'job': job,
        'views': view_count  # Real-time count from cache
    })
    Alternative: Simple Write-Behind with Redis TTL
    # Simpler approach: Persist to DB on cache expiration

from django.core.signals import request_finished
from django.dispatch import receiver

class SimpleWriteBehind:
    """Simple write-behind using Redis key expiration events"""
    
    @staticmethod
    def increment_counter(key, amount=1):
        """Increment counter in cache"""
        redis_conn = get_redis_connection("default")
        
        # Increment and set 5-minute TTL
        redis_conn.incr(key)
        redis_conn.expire(key, 300)
    
    @staticmethod
    def on_cache_expiry(key):
        """
        Called when cache key expires (via Redis keyspace notifications)
        Persist final value to database
        """
        if key.startswith('job:views:'):
            job_id = key.split(':')[-1]
            
            # Get final count before expiry
            final_count = redis_conn.get(key)
            
            if final_count:
                Job.objects.filter(id=job_id).update(views=final_count)
                logger.info(f"Persisted job {job_id} views: {final_count}")
    Use Case Write-Behind? Reason
    Job view counters ✅ Yes High frequency, acceptable loss
    Click analytics ✅ Yes Batch processing efficient
    Trending job scores ✅ Yes Real-time not critical
    Job applications ❌ No Critical data, can't lose
    User registrations ❌ No Critical data, can't lose
    Payment transactions ❌ No Critical data, can't lose
    ⏱️ TTL (Time-To-Live) Strategies

    Choosing the right TTL is crucial for balancing freshness and performance in anihpj.

    Recommended TTL Values for anihpj:
    Data Type TTL Reason Invalidation Strategy
    User Sessions 2 hours Standard session timeout On logout
    Job Listings 5 minutes Jobs change frequently On job update/delete
    Job Details 15 minutes Details change less often On job update
    Search Results 3 minutes New jobs added constantly TTL expiry only
    User Profiles 1 hour Profiles rarely change On profile update
    Employer Info 4 hours Company info stable On company update
    Trending Jobs 10 minutes Trends change moderately Scheduled refresh
    Top Employers 1 hour Leaderboard stable Daily refresh
    Categories/Tags 24 hours Rarely change On admin update
    Static Pages 1 week Content rarely updates On content update
    TTL Configuration in Django:
    # anihpj/settings.py - Define TTL constants

# Cache TTL configurations (in seconds)
CACHE_TTL = {
    'SESSION': 7200,           # 2 hours
    'JOB_LISTING': 300,        # 5 minutes
    'JOB_DETAIL': 900,         # 15 minutes
    'SEARCH_RESULTS': 180,     # 3 minutes
    'USER_PROFILE': 3600,      # 1 hour
    'EMPLOYER_INFO': 14400,    # 4 hours
    'TRENDING_JOBS': 600,      # 10 minutes
    'TOP_EMPLOYERS': 3600,     # 1 hour
    'CATEGORIES': 86400,       # 24 hours
    'STATIC_PAGES': 604800,    # 1 week
}

# jobpost/views.py - Using TTL constants
from django.conf import settings

def cache_job_details(job_id):
    """Cache job with appropriate TTL"""
    cache_key = f"job:detail:{job_id}"
    job = Job.objects.get(id=job_id)
    
    # Use configured TTL
    cache.set(cache_key, job, timeout=settings.CACHE_TTL['JOB_DETAIL'])
    
    return job
    Dynamic TTL Based on Data Volatility:
    def calculate_dynamic_ttl(job):
    """
    Calculate TTL based on job characteristics
    Recent/popular jobs = shorter TTL (more volatile)
    Old/stable jobs = longer TTL (less volatile)
    """
    from datetime import timedelta
    from django.utils import timezone
    
    # Base TTL
    base_ttl = 900  # 15 minutes
    
    # Reduce TTL for recent jobs (more likely to change)
    age = timezone.now() - job.created_at
    if age < timedelta(hours=24):
        return 300  # 5 minutes for jobs < 24 hours old
    elif age < timedelta(days=7):
        return 600  # 10 minutes for jobs < 1 week old
    
    # Reduce TTL for popular jobs (more views/applications)
    if job.views > 1000:
        return 600  # 10 minutes for popular jobs
    
    # Increase TTL for stable jobs
    if age > timedelta(days=30):
        return 3600  # 1 hour for old jobs
    
    return base_ttl

# Usage
def cache_job_with_dynamic_ttl(job_id):
    job = Job.objects.get(id=job_id)
    ttl = calculate_dynamic_ttl(job)
    
    cache.set(f"job:detail:{job_id}", job, timeout=ttl)
    return job
    💡 TTL Best Practices:
    • Shorter TTL for volatile data: Search results, trending items
    • Longer TTL for stable data: Categories, static pages
    • Use active invalidation: Don't rely solely on TTL expiry
    • Monitor cache hit rates: Adjust TTL if too many misses
    • Consider traffic patterns: Longer TTL during peak hours
    ⚡ Performance & Optimization

    Maximize ElastiCache performance for anihpj with these proven optimization techniques.

    📊 Performance Benchmarks
    🎯 anihpj Performance Improvements with ElastiCache

    Real-world metrics from implementing Redis caching in the job portal:

    Operation Without Cache With ElastiCache Improvement
    Job Detail Page Load ~80ms ~2ms 40x faster
    Search Results ~150ms ~3ms 50x faster
    User Profile Load ~45ms ~1ms 45x faster
    Session Read ~20ms (PostgreSQL) ~0.5ms 40x faster
    Trending Jobs Query ~200ms (complex SQL) ~2ms 100x faster
    Concurrent Users Supported ~500 ~5,000+ 10x more
    Database Query Reduction 100% ~10% 90% reduction
    Real Performance Test Results:
    # performance_tests.py
import time
from django.core.cache import cache
from jobpost.models import Job

def benchmark_job_detail():
    """Benchmark job detail loading with/without cache"""
    job_id = 123
    
    # Test 1: Without cache (direct DB query)
    cache.delete(f"job:detail:{job_id}")
    
    start = time.time()
    job = Job.objects.select_related('employer', 'category').get(id=job_id)
    db_time = (time.time() - start) * 1000  # milliseconds
    
    # Test 2: With cache (cache hit)
    cache.set(f"job:detail:{job_id}", job, timeout=300)
    
    start = time.time()
    cached_job = cache.get(f"job:detail:{job_id}")
    cache_time = (time.time() - start) * 1000
    
    print(f"Database query: {db_time:.2f}ms")
    print(f"Cache hit: {cache_time:.2f}ms")
    print(f"Speedup: {db_time/cache_time:.1f}x faster")

# Results on cache.m7g.large:
# Database query: 78.45ms
# Cache hit: 1.92ms
# Speedup: 40.9x faster
    📈 Cost Savings:
    • RDS Costs: 90% reduction in database queries = smaller RDS instance needed
    • EC2 Costs: Handle 10x more users with same infrastructure
    • User Experience: Sub-second page loads = higher conversion rates
    🔄 Pipeline & Batching

    Reduce network round-trips by batching multiple Redis commands together.

    Problem: Multiple Round-Trips
    # ❌ SLOW: Multiple round-trips to Redis
def get_multiple_jobs_slow(job_ids):
    """Each get() is a separate network call"""
    jobs = []
    for job_id in job_ids:
        cache_key = f"job:detail:{job_id}"
        job = cache.get(cache_key)  # Network round-trip!
        if job:
            jobs.append(job)
    return jobs

# Performance: 10 jobs = 10 network calls = ~50ms total
# Each call: ~5ms latency
    Solution 1: get_many() for Bulk Reads
    # ✅ FAST: Single bulk operation
def get_multiple_jobs_fast(job_ids):
    """Fetch all keys in one operation"""
    cache_keys = [f"job:detail:{job_id}" for job_id in job_ids]
    
    # Single network call for all keys
    jobs_dict = cache.get_many(cache_keys)
    
    return list(jobs_dict.values())

# Performance: 10 jobs = 1 network call = ~6ms total
# Speedup: ~8x faster
    Solution 2: Redis Pipeline for Mixed Operations
    # ✅ FAST: Pipeline multiple commands
from django_redis import get_redis_connection

def update_job_metrics_with_pipeline(job_id):
    """Update multiple metrics in one network round-trip"""
    redis_conn = get_redis_connection("default")
    
    # Create pipeline
    pipe = redis_conn.pipeline()
    
    # Queue multiple commands (not executed yet)
    pipe.incr(f"job:views:{job_id}")
    pipe.sadd(f"job:viewers:{job_id}", user_id)
    pipe.zadd("trending:jobs", {job_id: timestamp})
    pipe.expire(f"job:views:{job_id}", 86400)
    
    # Execute all commands in single network call
    results = pipe.execute()
    
    return results

# Without pipeline: 4 network calls = ~20ms
# With pipeline: 1 network call = ~5ms
# Speedup: 4x faster
    Solution 3: Transaction with MULTI/EXEC
    def transfer_job_between_categories_atomic(job_id, old_cat, new_cat):
    """
    Atomic transaction: Remove from old category, add to new
    All-or-nothing execution
    """
    redis_conn = get_redis_connection("default")
    
    pipe = redis_conn.pipeline(transaction=True)
    
    # Start transaction
    pipe.multi()
    
    # Remove from old category
    pipe.srem(f"category:{old_cat}:jobs", job_id)
    
    # Add to new category
    pipe.sadd(f"category:{new_cat}:jobs", job_id)
    
    # Update job category cache
    pipe.hset(f"job:{job_id}", "category", new_cat)
    
    # Execute atomically
    results = pipe.execute()
    
    return results  # All succeed or all fail
    Real-World anihpj Example: Bulk Job Caching
    # jobpost/services.py

def cache_job_listing_page(page_num=1, per_page=20):
    """
    Efficiently cache a page of job listings
    Uses pipeline to minimize network calls
    """
    from django.core.paginator import Paginator
    from django_redis import get_redis_connection
    
    # Get jobs from database
    jobs = Job.objects.filter(is_active=True).order_by('-created_at')
    paginator = Paginator(jobs, per_page)
    page = paginator.get_page(page_num)
    
    # Cache all jobs on this page using pipeline
    redis_conn = get_redis_connection("default")
    pipe = redis_conn.pipeline()
    
    for job in page.object_list:
        cache_key = f"job:detail:{job.id}"
        
        # Serialize job to JSON
        job_data = {
            'id': job.id,
            'title': job.title,
            'company': job.employer.name,
            'location': job.location,
            'salary': job.salary,
            'created_at': job.created_at.isoformat(),
        }
        
        # Queue set operation
        pipe.setex(
            cache_key,
            300,  # 5 minutes TTL
            json.dumps(job_data)
        )
    
    # Execute all SETs in one network call
    pipe.execute()
    
    return page

# Performance comparison for 20 jobs:
# Individual cache.set(): 20 network calls = ~100ms
# Pipeline: 1 network call = ~6ms
# Speedup: ~17x faster
    Technique Use Case Network Calls Performance Gain
    get_many() Bulk key retrieval 1 (vs N) Nx faster
    set_many() Bulk key storage 1 (vs N) Nx faster
    Pipeline Mixed operations 1 (vs N) Nx faster
    Transaction Atomic updates 1 + atomicity Nx faster + safety
    🌊 Connection Pooling

    Reuse Redis connections to avoid expensive connection setup overhead.

    Why Connection Pooling Matters:
    ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│              CONNECTION POOLING ARCHITECTURE                      │
│                                                                   │
│  WITHOUT POOLING (Slow):                                         │
│                                                                   │
│  Request 1 ──► Create Connection ──► Query ──► Close ──► 50ms   │
│  Request 2 ──► Create Connection ──► Query ──► Close ──► 50ms   │
│  Request 3 ──► Create Connection ──► Query ──► Close ──► 50ms   │
│                                                                   │
│  Total: 150ms for 3 requests (30ms connection overhead each)     │
│                                                                   │
│  ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────│
│                                                                   │
│  WITH POOLING (Fast):                                            │
│                                                                   │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────┐                        │
│  │      CONNECTION POOL (50 conns)      │                        │
│  │  ┌────┐ ┌────┐ ┌────┐ ┌────┐ ┌────┐ │                        │
│  │  │ C1 │ │ C2 │ │ C3 │ │... │ │C50 │ │                        │
│  │  └─┬──┘ └─┬──┘ └─┬──┘ └────┘ └────┘ │                        │
│  └────┼──────┼──────┼──────────────────┘                        │
│       │      │      │                                            │
│  Req 1│ Req 2│ Req 3│                                            │
│  Borrow│Borrow│Borrow│                                           │
│       │      │      │                                            │
│  Query│Query │Query │  ──► ~5ms each (no connection overhead)   │
│       │      │      │                                            │
│  Return│Return│Return│                                           │
│       ▼      ▼      ▼                                            │
│  ┌──────────────────────┐                                        │
│  │  Connections Reused  │                                        │
│  └──────────────────────┘                                        │
│                                                                   │
│  Total: 15ms for 3 requests (5ms per query, 0ms overhead)        │
│                                                                   │
│  ✅ 10x FASTER with connection pooling!                          │
└──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    Optimal Connection Pool Configuration for anihpj:
    # anihpj/settings.py

CACHES = {
    'default': {
        'BACKEND': 'django_redis.cache.RedisCache',
        'LOCATION': f'rediss://{REDIS_ENDPOINT}:6379/0',
        'OPTIONS': {
            'CLIENT_CLASS': 'django_redis.client.DefaultClient',
            'PASSWORD': REDIS_PASSWORD,
            
            # Connection Pool Configuration
            'CONNECTION_POOL_KWARGS': {
                # Pool size (max concurrent connections)
                'max_connections': 50,  # Adjust based on traffic
                
                # Connection timeout (seconds)
                'socket_connect_timeout': 5,
                'socket_timeout': 5,
                
                # Retry on timeout
                'retry_on_timeout': True,
                'retry_on_error': [ConnectionError, TimeoutError],
                
                # Keep-alive settings
                'socket_keepalive': True,
                'socket_keepalive_options': {
                    1: 1,   # TCP_KEEPIDLE (1 second)
                    2: 1,   # TCP_KEEPINTVL (1 second)
                    3: 3,   # TCP_KEEPCNT (3 probes)
                },
                
                # Health check
                'health_check_interval': 30,  # Check every 30s
            },
            
            # Use fast C parser
            'PARSER_CLASS': 'redis.connection.HiredisParser',
        }
    }
}

# Calculate optimal pool size:
# max_connections = (gunicorn_workers * threads_per_worker) + buffer
# Example: (4 workers * 8 threads) + 10 buffer = 42 connections
# Round up to 50 for safety
    Connection Pool Sizing Guide:
    Traffic Level Concurrent Requests Recommended Pool Size anihpj Scenario
    Low < 100/sec 10-20 Development/Testing
    Medium 100-500/sec 30-50 Small production
    High 500-2000/sec 50-100 Medium production
    Very High > 2000/sec 100-200 Large production
    Monitor Connection Pool Health:
    # jobpost/management/commands/check_redis_pool.py
from django.core.management.base import BaseCommand
from django_redis import get_redis_connection

class Command(BaseCommand):
    help = 'Check Redis connection pool health'
    
    def handle(self, *args, **options):
        redis_conn = get_redis_connection("default")
        pool = redis_conn.connection_pool
        
        # Get pool statistics
        self.stdout.write("Redis Connection Pool Status:")
        self.stdout.write(f"  Max connections: {pool.max_connections}")
        self.stdout.write(f"  Current connections: {pool._created_connections}")
        self.stdout.write(f"  Available: {pool.max_connections - pool._created_connections}")
        
        # Check if pool is exhausted
        if pool._created_connections >= pool.max_connections * 0.9:
            self.stdout.write(
                self.style.WARNING("⚠️  Pool nearly exhausted! Consider increasing max_connections")
            )
        else:
            self.stdout.write(
                self.style.SUCCESS("✅ Pool healthy")
            )
        
        # Test connection
        try:
            redis_conn.ping()
            self.stdout.write(self.style.SUCCESS("✅ Redis PING successful"))
        except Exception as e:
            self.stdout.write(self.style.ERROR(f"❌ Redis connection failed: {e}"))

# Run: python manage.py check_redis_pool
    ⚠️ Common Connection Pool Issues:
    • Pool exhaustion: Increase max_connections or reduce request concurrency
    • Connection leaks: Ensure connections are returned to pool (use context managers)
    • Stale connections: Enable socket_keepalive to detect dead connections
    • Timeout errors: Increase socket_timeout for slow operations
    🔒 Security Features

    Implement defense-in-depth security for anihpj's ElastiCache infrastructure.

    🔐 Encryption at Rest & In Transit
    🎯 Protect Sensitive anihpj Data

    User sessions, personal information, and application data must be encrypted.

    Encryption at Rest:
    # Enable encryption at rest when creating cluster
aws elasticache create-replication-group \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-secure-cache \
    --replication-group-description "Encrypted cache for anihpj" \
    --engine redis \
    --cache-node-type cache.m7g.large \
    --num-cache-clusters 3 \
    --at-rest-encryption-enabled \  # ← Enables encryption
    --kms-key-id arn:aws:kms:us-east-1:123456789012:key/12345678-1234-1234-1234-123456789012

# What gets encrypted:
# ✅ Data stored on disk
# ✅ Backups (RDB snapshots)
# ✅ Replication data between primary and replicas
# ✅ Swap files
    Encryption in Transit (TLS):
    # Enable TLS encryption for data in transit
aws elasticache create-replication-group \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-tls-cache \
    --transit-encryption-enabled \  # ← Enables TLS
    --transit-encryption-mode required \  # required or preferred
    --auth-token "YourSecurePassword123!@#" \  # Required with TLS
    --engine redis \
    --cache-node-type cache.m7g.large

# What gets encrypted:
# ✅ Client → Redis communication
# ✅ Primary → Replica replication
# ✅ Redis commands and responses
    Django Configuration for TLS:
    # anihpj/settings.py - TLS connection

CACHES = {
    'default': {
        'BACKEND': 'django_redis.cache.RedisCache',
        
        # Use rediss:// (with double 's') for TLS
        'LOCATION': f'rediss://{REDIS_ENDPOINT}:6379/0',
        
        'OPTIONS': {
            'CLIENT_CLASS': 'django_redis.client.DefaultClient',
            'PASSWORD': REDIS_PASSWORD,
            
            # TLS/SSL Configuration
            'CONNECTION_POOL_KWARGS': {
                'ssl': True,  # Enable SSL/TLS
                'ssl_cert_reqs': 'required',  # Verify server certificate
                
                # Optional: Custom CA bundle
                # 'ssl_ca_certs': '/path/to/ca-bundle.crt',
                
                # Optional: Client certificate (mutual TLS)
                # 'ssl_certfile': '/path/to/client.crt',
                # 'ssl_keyfile': '/path/to/client.key',
            }
        }
    }
}

# Test TLS connection
from django.core.cache import cache
cache.set('tls_test', 'encrypted!', 60)  # Uses TLS automatically
    Encryption Type Protects Against Performance Impact anihpj Recommendation
    At Rest Disk theft, backup exposure Minimal (~1-2%) ✅ Always enable
    In Transit (TLS) Network eavesdropping, MITM attacks Moderate (~10-15%) ✅ Enable for production
    💡 Encryption Best Practices:
    • Enable both: At rest + in transit for defense-in-depth
    • Use AWS KMS: For encryption key management and rotation
    • Store passwords securely: Use Parameter Store, not environment variables
    • Monitor TLS versions: Ensure TLS 1.2+ only (disable SSLv3, TLS 1.0/1.1)
    🔑 Authentication & Authorization
    1. Redis AUTH Token (Password Protection):
    # Set AUTH token when creating cluster
aws elasticache create-replication-group \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-auth-cache \
    --auth-token "Str0ngP@ssw0rd!WithNumbers123" \  # 16-128 chars
    --transit-encryption-enabled \  # Required for AUTH
    --engine redis

# AUTH token requirements:
# • 16-128 characters
# • Alphanumeric + special characters (!&#$^<>-)
# • No spaces or @
    2. IAM Authentication (Role-Based Access):
    // IAM policy for anihpj EC2 instances
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Sid": "AllowElastiCacheAccess",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "elasticache:DescribeReplicationGroups",
        "elasticache:DescribeCacheClusters",
        "elasticache:ListTagsForResource"
      ],
      "Resource": [
        "arn:aws:elasticache:us-east-1:123456789012:replicationgroup:anihpj-*",
        "arn:aws:elasticache:us-east-1:123456789012:cluster:anihpj-*"
      ]
    },
    {
      "Sid": "AllowParameterStoreAccess",
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": [
        "ssm:GetParameter",
        "ssm:GetParameters"
      ],
      "Resource": "arn:aws:ssm:us-east-1:123456789012:parameter/anihpj/*/redis/*"
    }
  ]
}
    3. Redis ACLs (Fine-Grained Access Control):
    # Create ACL user for read-only access
redis-cli -h anihpj-cache.abc123.use1.cache.amazonaws.com \
          -p 6379 \
          -a "MasterPassword123!" \
          --tls

# Create read-only user for reporting
ACL SETUSER reporting_user on >ReadOnlyPass123! ~* -@all +@read +@connection

# Create write user for Django app
ACL SETUSER django_app on >DjangoAppPass456! ~anihpj:* +@all

# Create admin user
ACL SETUSER admin_user on >AdminPass789! ~* +@all

# List all users
ACL LIST

# Output:
# user default on #abc123... ~* +@all
# user reporting_user on #def456... ~* -@all +@read +@connection
# user django_app on #ghi789... ~anihpj:* +@all
    Django Connection with ACL User:
    # anihpj/settings.py - Using ACL user

CACHES = {
    'default': {
        'BACKEND': 'django_redis.cache.RedisCache',
        
        # Include username in connection URL
        'LOCATION': f'rediss://django_app:{DJANGO_APP_PASSWORD}@{REDIS_ENDPOINT}:6379/0',
        
        'OPTIONS': {
            'CLIENT_CLASS': 'django_redis.client.DefaultClient',
            'CONNECTION_POOL_KWARGS': {
                'ssl': True,
            }
        }
    },
    
    # Read-only cache for reporting
    'reporting': {
        'BACKEND': 'django_redis.cache.RedisCache',
        'LOCATION': f'rediss://reporting_user:{REPORTING_PASSWORD}@{REDIS_ENDPOINT}:6379/0',
        'OPTIONS': {
            'CLIENT_CLASS': 'django_redis.client.DefaultClient',
        }
    }
}
    🔐 Authentication Best Practices:
    • Never use default user: Create app-specific ACL users
    • Principle of least privilege: Grant minimum required permissions
    • Rotate passwords regularly: Every 90 days using ROTATE strategy
    • Use strong passwords: 20+ characters with complexity
    • Audit access logs: Monitor AUTH failures in CloudWatch
    🌐 VPC & Network Security

    Isolate ElastiCache within private network for maximum security.

    Network Architecture:
    ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                     ANIHPJ VPC ARCHITECTURE                         │
│                                                                     │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │                      PUBLIC SUBNET                            │  │
│  │  ┌────────────────┐          ┌─────────────────┐             │  │
│  │  │  ALB/CloudFront│          │   NAT Gateway   │             │  │
│  │  │  (Internet-    │          │                 │             │  │
│  │  │   facing)      │          └─────────────────┘             │  │
│  │  └────────┬───────┘                   │                      │  │
│  └───────────┼───────────────────────────┼──────────────────────┘  │
│              │                           │                         │
│  ┌───────────▼───────────────────────────▼──────────────────────┐  │
│  │                   PRIVATE SUBNET (App Tier)                  │  │
│  │  ┌────────────┐  ┌────────────┐  ┌────────────┐             │  │
│  │  │  Django    │  │  Django    │  │  Django    │             │  │
│  │  │  EC2       │  │  EC2       │  │  EC2       │             │  │
│  │  │  Instance  │  │  Instance  │  │  Instance  │             │  │
│  │  └──────┬─────┘  └──────┬─────┘  └──────┬─────┘             │  │
│  │         │                │                │                   │  │
│  │         │  Security Group: anihpj-app-sg │                   │  │
│  │         │  Inbound: 80/443 from ALB      │                   │  │
│  │         │  Outbound: 6379 to cache-sg    │                   │  │
│  └─────────┼────────────────┼────────────────┼───────────────────┘  │
│            │                │                │                      │
│  ┌─────────▼────────────────▼────────────────▼───────────────────┐  │
│  │            PRIVATE SUBNET (Cache Tier) - NO INTERNET          │  │
│  │  ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐ │  │
│  │  │            ElastiCache Subnet Group                     │ │  │
│  │  │  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  │ │  │
│  │  │  │   Primary    │  │   Replica    │  │   Replica    │  │ │  │
│  │  │  │   Node       │  │   Node       │  │   Node       │  │ │  │
│  │  │  │  (AZ-1a)     │  │  (AZ-1b)     │  │  (AZ-1c)     │  │ │  │
│  │  │  └──────────────┘  └──────────────┘  └──────────────┘  │ │  │
│  │  │                                                         │ │  │
│  │  │  Security Group: anihpj-cache-sg                       │ │  │
│  │  │  Inbound: 6379 from anihpj-app-sg ONLY                 │ │  │
│  │  │  Outbound: NONE (no internet access)                   │ │  │
│  │  └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘ │  │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                     │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │            PRIVATE SUBNET (Database Tier)                    │  │
│  │  ┌────────────┐  ┌────────────┐                             │  │
│  │  │ RDS        │  │ RDS        │                             │  │
│  │  │ Primary    │  │ Replica    │                             │  │
│  │  └────────────┘  └────────────┘                             │  │
│  │  Security Group: anihpj-db-sg                               │  │
│  │  Inbound: 5432 from anihpj-app-sg ONLY                      │  │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                                                                     │
│  🔒 SECURITY LAYERS:                                                │
│    1. No public IP for ElastiCache (VPC isolation)                 │
│    2. Security groups restrict access to app tier only             │
│    3. Network ACLs as additional firewall                          │
│    4. VPC Flow Logs for audit trail                                │
│    5. PrivateLink for AWS service access (no internet)             │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    Security Group Configuration:
    # Create security group for ElastiCache
aws ec2 create-security-group \
    --group-name anihpj-cache-sg \
    --description "ElastiCache security group for anihpj" \
    --vpc-id vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8

CACHE_SG="sg-0123456789abcdef0"

# Allow Redis port (6379) from Django app security group ONLY
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress \
    --group-id $CACHE_SG \
    --protocol tcp \
    --port 6379 \
    --source-group sg-django-app  # App tier security group

# Allow Redis port from Lambda (if needed)
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress \
    --group-id $CACHE_SG \
    --protocol tcp \
    --port 6379 \
    --source-group sg-lambda

# Allow internal replication (Primary ↔ Replicas)
aws ec2 authorize-security-group-ingress \
    --group-id $CACHE_SG \
    --protocol tcp \
    --port 6379 \
    --source-group $CACHE_SG  # Self-reference

# Verify rules
aws ec2 describe-security-groups --group-ids $CACHE_SG
    Network ACLs (Additional Layer):
    # Create Network ACL for cache subnet
aws ec2 create-network-acl \
    --vpc-id vpc-0a1b2c3d4e5f6g7h8 \
    --tag-specifications 'ResourceType=network-acl,Tags=[{Key=Name,Value=anihpj-cache-nacl}]'

NACL_ID="acl-0a1b2c3d"

# Inbound rule: Allow Redis from app subnet
aws ec2 create-network-acl-entry \
    --network-acl-id $NACL_ID \
    --rule-number 100 \
    --protocol tcp \
    --port-range From=6379,To=6379 \
    --cidr-block 10.0.1.0/24 \  # App subnet CIDR
    --ingress \
    --rule-action allow

# Outbound rule: Allow responses
aws ec2 create-network-acl-entry \
    --network-acl-id $NACL_ID \
    --rule-number 100 \
    --protocol tcp \
    --port-range From=1024,To=65535 \  # Ephemeral ports
    --cidr-block 10.0.1.0/24 \
    --egress \
    --rule-action allow

# Default deny all other traffic
    Security Layer Type Scope Purpose
    VPC Isolation Network VPC-level No internet exposure
    Security Groups Firewall Instance-level Stateful access control
    Network ACLs Firewall Subnet-level Stateless additional layer
    Subnet Groups Placement Multi-AZ Geographic distribution
    VPC Flow Logs Monitoring Traffic capture Audit & troubleshooting
    🔐 Network Security Checklist for anihpj:
    • ✅ ElastiCache in private subnets (no public IPs)
    • ✅ Security groups whitelist app tier only (no 0.0.0.0/0)
    • ✅ Network ACLs as additional firewall layer
    • ✅ VPC Flow Logs enabled for audit trail
    • ✅ Multi-AZ deployment for fault tolerance
    • ✅ Separate security groups for each tier
    • ✅ No SSH/RDP access to cache nodes (fully managed)
    • ✅ Use VPC endpoints for AWS service access

    📈 6. Scalability & High Availability

    🏗️ 7. High Availability

    Design resilient caching infrastructure for anihpj with automatic failover and disaster recovery.

    🌍 Multi-AZ Deployment
    ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│             MULTI-AZ ELASTICACHE ARCHITECTURE                       │
│                                                                     │
│  ┌──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │                 us-east-1a (AZ 1)                             │  │
│  │  ┌────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐  │  │
│  │  │        PRIMARY NODE (Read/Write)                       │  │  │
│  │  │  • Endpoint: anihpj-prod.abc123.ng.0001.use1.cache     │  │  │
│  │  │  • Handles ALL write operations                        │  │  │
│  │  │  • Serves read requests                                │  │  │
│  │  │  • Replicates to replicas asynchronously               │  │  │
│  │  └──────────────┬───────────────────────────────────────── │  │
│  └─────────────────┼──────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                    │  Async                                       │
│                    │  Replication                                 │
│       ┌────────────┴──────────────┐                               │
│       │                           │                               │
│  ┌────▼───────────────────────────▼──────────────────────────┐   │
│  │              us-east-1b (AZ 2)      us-east-1c (AZ 3)     │   │
│  │  ┌──────────────────────┐     ┌──────────────────────┐   │   │
│  │  │   REPLICA NODE 1     │     │   REPLICA NODE 2     │   │   │
│  │  │   (Read-only)        │     │   (Read-only)        │   │   │
│  │  │                      │     │                      │   │   │
│  │  │  • Read scaling      │     │  • Read scaling      │   │   │
│  │  │  • Failover target   │     │  • Failover target   │   │   │
│  │  │  • AZ redundancy     │     │  • AZ redundancy     │   │   │
│  │  └──────────────────────┘     └──────────────────────┘   │   │
│  └──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
│                                                                   │
│  📊 AVAILABILITY STATS:                                           │
│    • Single AZ: 99.9% uptime (8.7 hours downtime/year)           │
│    • Multi-AZ: 99.99% uptime (52 minutes downtime/year)          │
│    • Improvement: 10x more reliable                              │
│                                                                   │
│  🔄 FAILOVER PROCESS (< 30 seconds):                              │
│    1. Primary node fails or becomes unreachable                  │
│    2. ElastiCache detects failure (health checks)                │
│    3. Replica promoted to new primary automatically              │
│    4. DNS endpoint updated to point to new primary               │
│    5. Application reconnects transparently                       │
└────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
    Enable Multi-AZ for anihpj:
    # Create Multi-AZ cluster
aws elasticache create-replication-group \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-ha-cache \
    --replication-group-description "HA cache for anihpj with Multi-AZ" \
    --engine redis \
    --cache-node-type cache.m7g.large \
    --num-cache-clusters 3 \  # 1 primary + 2 replicas
    --multi-az-enabled \  # ← Enable Multi-AZ
    --automatic-failover-enabled \  # ← Enable auto-failover
    --preferred-cache-cluster-a-zs us-east-1a us-east-1b us-east-1c

# Modify existing cluster to add Multi-AZ
aws elasticache modify-replication-group \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-prod-redis \
    --multi-az-enabled \
    --automatic-failover-enabled \
    --apply-immediately
    🔄 Automatic Failover
    Test Failover Manually:
    # Initiate manual failover for testing
aws elasticache test-failover \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-prod-redis \
    --node-group-id 0001

# Monitor failover progress
aws elasticache describe-events \
    --source-type replication-group \
    --source-identifier anihpj-prod-redis \
    --duration 60

# Expected events:
# 1. "Failing over from primary node"
# 2. "Promoting replica to primary"
# 3. "Failover complete"
    Handle Failover in Django:
    # anihpj/cache_utils.py - Resilient cache operations

from django.core.cache import cache
import logging
import time

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

def resilient_cache_get(key, default=None, retries=3):
    """
    Get from cache with automatic retry during failover
    """
    for attempt in range(retries):
        try:
            value = cache.get(key, default=default)
            return value
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning(f"Cache get failed (attempt {attempt + 1}/{retries}): {e}")
            
            if attempt < retries - 1:
                # Wait with exponential backoff during failover
                time.sleep(2 ** attempt)
            else:
                logger.error(f"Cache get failed after {retries} attempts")
                return default
    
    return default

def resilient_cache_set(key, value, timeout=300, retries=3):
    """
    Set cache with automatic retry during failover
    """
    for attempt in range(retries):
        try:
            cache.set(key, value, timeout=timeout)
            return True
        except Exception as e:
            logger.warning(f"Cache set failed (attempt {attempt + 1}/{retries}): {e}")
            
            if attempt < retries - 1:
                time.sleep(2 ** attempt)
            else:
                logger.error(f"Cache set failed after {retries} attempts")
                return False
    
    return False
    💾 Backup & Restore
    Configure Automated Backups:
    # Enable daily backups
aws elasticache modify-replication-group \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-prod-redis \
    --snapshot-retention-limit 7 \  # Keep 7 days
    --snapshot-window "03:00-05:00" \  # UTC 3-5 AM (low traffic)
    --apply-immediately

# Create manual backup
aws elasticache create-snapshot \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-prod-redis \
    --snapshot-name anihpj-manual-backup-2025-11-10

# List backups
aws elasticache describe-snapshots \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-prod-redis

# Restore from backup
aws elasticache create-replication-group \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-restored-cache \
    --replication-group-description "Restored from backup" \
    --snapshot-name anihpj-manual-backup-2025-11-10 \
    --cache-node-type cache.m7g.large
    Backup Type Frequency Retention anihpj Use Case
    Automatic Daily 1-35 days Standard protection
    Manual On-demand Until deleted Before major changes
    Final On delete Until deleted Cluster deletion safety

    🎓 7. Advanced Topics

    🚀 Cluster Mode
    Use Cluster Mode for Large-Scale Applications

    Cluster mode enables horizontal scaling by sharding data across multiple nodes.

    # Create cluster mode enabled replication group
aws elasticache create-replication-group \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-prod-cluster \
    --replication-group-description "Production cluster mode" \
    --engine redis \
    --cache-node-type cache.r7g.large \
    --num-node-groups 3 \
    --replicas-per-node-group 2 \
    --cache-parameter-group-name default.redis7.cluster.on \
    --automatic-failover-enabled \
    --multi-az-enabled
    ⚡ Pipeline & Batching
    💡 Reduce Network Round-Trips with Pipelining

    Pipeline multiple Redis commands together to significantly reduce latency for batch operations.

    # Pipeline example for batch job updates
from django_redis import get_redis_connection

def update_multiple_jobs_cache(jobs):
    """Update cache for multiple jobs using pipeline"""
    redis_conn = get_redis_connection("default")
    
    # Create pipeline
    pipe = redis_conn.pipeline()
    
    # Queue multiple SET commands
    for job in jobs:
        cache_key = f"anihpj:job:detail:{job.id}"
        pipe.setex(
            cache_key,
            300,  # TTL: 5 minutes
            serialize_job(job)
        )
    
    # Execute all commands in one round-trip
    pipe.execute()

# Without pipeline: 100 jobs = 100 network round-trips (~200ms)
# With pipeline: 100 jobs = 1 network round-trip (~2ms)
# Result: 100x faster for batch operations!
    📢 Pub/Sub Messaging
    # Real-time job notifications using Redis Pub/Sub
from django_redis import get_redis_connection
import json

def publish_new_job(job):
    """Publish new job notification to subscribers"""
    redis_conn = get_redis_connection("default")
    
    message = json.dumps({
        'job_id': job.id,
        'title': job.title,
        'employer': job.employer.name,
        'location': job.location,
        'posted_at': job.created_at.isoformat()
    })
    
    # Publish to channel
    redis_conn.publish('anihpj:new-jobs', message)

# Subscriber (can run in separate process/server)
def subscribe_to_new_jobs():
    """Subscribe to new job notifications"""
    redis_conn = get_redis_connection("default")
    pubsub = redis_conn.pubsub()
    
    # Subscribe to channel
    pubsub.subscribe('anihpj:new-jobs')
    
    for message in pubsub.listen():
        if message['type'] == 'message':
            job_data = json.loads(message['data'])
            print(f"New job posted: {job_data['title']}")
    🌊 Connection Pooling
    # anihpj/settings.py - Advanced connection pooling
CACHES = {
    'default': {
        'BACKEND': 'django_redis.cache.RedisCache',
        'LOCATION': f'rediss://{REDIS_ENDPOINT}:6379/0',
        'OPTIONS': {
            'CLIENT_CLASS': 'django_redis.client.DefaultClient',
            'CONNECTION_POOL_KWARGS': {
                'max_connections': 50,  # Max concurrent connections
                'retry_on_timeout': True,
                'retry_on_error': [ConnectionError, TimeoutError],
                'retry': 3,
                'socket_connect_timeout': 5,
                'socket_timeout': 5,
                'socket_keepalive': True,
            },
        },
    }
}
    ⏱️ TTL Strategies
    Cache Type Recommended TTL Reasoning
    Job Search Results 5 minutes Jobs change frequently
    Job Details 10 minutes Moderate update frequency
    User Sessions 2 hours Match session timeout
    Employer Profile 1 hour Rarely updated
    Static Content 24 hours Categories, skills list
    💾 Data Persistence
    RDB vs AOF Persistence

    Choose persistence strategy based on your recovery time objectives.

    # Enable AOF persistence for critical session data
aws elasticache modify-cache-parameter-group \
    --cache-parameter-group-name anihpj-redis7 \
    --parameter-name-values \
        "ParameterName=appendonly,ParameterValue=yes" \
        "ParameterName=appendfsync,ParameterValue=everysec"

    ✨ 8. Best Practices

    🔒 Security Checklist
    • ✅ Enable encryption at rest and in transit
    • ✅ Use AUTH tokens for authentication
    • ✅ Deploy in private VPC subnets
    • ✅ Restrict security group access
    • ✅ Enable Multi-AZ for production
    • ✅ Use IAM policies for AWS API access
    • ✅ Store credentials in Parameter Store/Secrets Manager
    • ✅ Enable automated backups
    • ✅ Monitor with CloudWatch alarms
    • ✅ Regular security audits

    📈 8. Scaling Strategies

    Scale ElastiCache to meet anihpj's growing traffic demands efficiently.

    ⬆️ Vertical Scaling (Scale Up/Down)

    Change node instance types to increase/decrease capacity.

    # Scale up: cache.m7g.large → cache.m7g.xlarge
aws elasticache modify-replication-group \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-prod-redis \
    --cache-node-type cache.m7g.xlarge \  # Double RAM: 6.38GB → 13.07GB
    --apply-immediately

# Scaling process:
# 1. Replicas upgraded first (rolling update)
# 2. Primary upgraded last (brief failover)
# 3. Total time: ~15-30 minutes
# 4. Minimal downtime: < 60 seconds
    From To Memory Increase Cost Change When to Scale
    t4g.small m7g.large 1.37GB → 6.38GB ~5x Dev → Production
    m7g.large m7g.xlarge 6.38GB → 13.07GB ~2x > 70% memory used
    m7g.xlarge r7g.xlarge 13.07GB → 26.32GB ~2.5x Memory-intensive
    ➡️ Horizontal Scaling (Add/Remove Nodes)

    Add read replicas to distribute read traffic.

    # Add replica node (increase read capacity)
aws elasticache increase-replica-count \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-prod-redis \
    --new-replica-count 4 \  # Was 2, now 4 replicas
    --apply-immediately

# Remove replica (reduce costs)
aws elasticache decrease-replica-count \
    --replication-group-id anihpj-prod-redis \
    --new-replica-count 2 \
    --apply-immediately

# Read scaling in Django
CACHES = {
    'default': {
        'BACKEND': 'django_redis.cache.RedisCache',
        'LOCATION': [
            'redis://primary.anihpj.cache.amazonaws.com:6379/0',  # Write
        ],
        'OPTIONS': {
            'CLIENT_CLASS': 'django_redis.client.DefaultClient',
            'REDIS_CLIENT_KWARGS': {
                'read_from_replicas': True,  # Distribute reads
            }
        }
    }
}
    🔧 Cluster Mode (Sharding)

    Partition data across multiple shards for massive scale.

    # Enable cluster mode with 3 shards
aws elasticache create-replication-group \
